Data Health Checks

    Runs automatically after every data refresh. Checks for sum mismatches, duplicate IDs, identical-cache twins, and month-over-month spikes.

    Tin Inventory & Demand
    Runway = (FG stock + empty tins + tins incoming) ÷ monthly demand. Petra POs aren't additive — they consume tins from the pool to produce FG. Tin Buffer = tins left after Petra consumes its open POs.
    Tin SKU Linked SKU Size FG Stock Petra PO Tin QOH Tin Inc Tin Buffer Total Supply Demand/Mo Runway (mo) Order By 12mo Short Unit Cost Status
    Suggested Pallet Combinations
    (fills pallets by most-urgent tin first)
    35g and 85g tins ship on separate pallets. Each pallet uses batch sizes 5k/10k/15k to total 15k (35g) or 10k (85g).

    Paper Packaging

    Default lead time: 4 months · Source of truth: SalesBinder weekly → Logiwa monthly physical count reconciles
    Per-SKU lead times can be overridden in src/salesbinder.js → PACKAGING_OVERRIDES.leadTimes once Lauren confirms supplier timings.
    SRT Packaging Trays (per scent)
    Demand = monthly Woo SRT bundles sold for this scent (CA + US) + Xero CTN cartons sold × 4 (each carton holds 4 SRTs). Runway = (On Hand + Incoming) ÷ demand.
    SRT SKU Name Linked Tin On Hand Incoming Demand/Mo Woo SRT/mo CTN×4/mo Runway (mo) Lead Order By 12mo Short Unit Cost Status
    Envelopes
    Demand = total Woo CA + US monthly orders (1 envelope per order, 3-month average). Only the printed P-ENV-A1 envelope is in regular rotation; blank P-BLANK-ENV-A1 stock is tracked in the watchlist below.
    SKU Name On Hand Incoming Demand/Mo Runway (mo) Lead Order By 12mo Short Unit Cost Status
    Postcard Inserts (PCIs)
    Demand = total Woo CA + US monthly orders (1 PCI per Woo order, 3-month average). Logiwa reconciles the count at month-end.
    SKU Name On Hand Incoming Demand/Mo Runway (mo) Lead Order By 12mo Short Unit Cost Status
    Inventory Watchlist (blank stock, no demand model)
    Items tracked for at-a-glance visibility. Includes outer cartons for 2-SRT / 4-SRT orders and blank envelopes (backup stock, not in regular Woo rotation). Demand is hard to predict from order data — flagged here so you can eyeball stock when Logiwa reconciles. Lead time is TBD with Lauren.
    SKU Name Holds On Hand Incoming Low Threshold Unit Cost Status
    No Pong Natural Products · North America Dashboard · Live data from WooCommerce, Amazon, Xero, SalesBinder & Logiwa
    v2.43 · July 2026

    v2.43 — 18 July 2026 (Woo order-item writes made atomic per order)

    • An order's line items can no longer be left half-written. The Woo sync writes each order as a delete-then-insert of its items; the old 500-statement chunking could split that across two transactions, so a failure at a chunk boundary could delete an order's items without re-inserting them (wrong per-SKU units until the next re-fetch). Batching now respects order boundaries, keeping each order's rewrite in one transaction. Applies to both the incremental sync and the new modified-date safety-net.

    v2.42 — 18 July 2026 (Inventory freshness: SalesBinder empty-response guard + Logiwa stale flag)

    • SalesBinder can't wipe good stock data on a glitch. If SalesBinder returned 200 with an empty list (a transient upstream hiccup), the cron used to cache 0 items over the good snapshot — and then serve "0 stock" as real until the next 4h refresh. It now keeps the prior non-empty snapshot instead (flagged staleFallback).
    • Logiwa 3PL uploads now carry an age + stale flag. Logiwa is upload-only with no automatic refresh, so a weeks-old export kept reading as live inventory. The API now returns ageMs + stale (past 7 days) so a stale 3PL count can be flagged rather than silently fed into the stock-on-hand comparison.

    v2.41 — 18 July 2026 (Xero invoices: paginate + filter by invoice date)

    • Xero invoice list no longer silently drops the oldest invoices. The endpoint fetched only the first page (max 100), so once more than 100 ACCREC invoices fell in the window the older-dated ones just vanished from the finance view. It now paginates through all pages (like the purchase-orders path). It also filters on the invoice Date via a where clause instead of If-Modified-Since (which filtered on last-updated date) — so "last 6 months" now means invoices dated in the window, not merely ones touched in it.

    v2.40 — 18 July 2026 (Woo refunds/cancellations stop counting as revenue — modified-date safety-net)

    • Refunded and cancelled orders no longer count as revenue forever. The incremental Woo sync advanced its watermark on create date and pulled each order only once, at creation — so an order that later flipped completed/processing → refunded stayed counted in D1 (revenue overcounted), and an unpaid → paid upgrade never landed (undercounted). A new weekly modified-date safety-net re-fetches every order modified in the last 30 days (CA + US) and upserts it, correcting status + line items in place. Mirrors the CIN7 safety-net already used on the AU side. A shared buildWooOrderStatements helper keeps the incremental + safety-net paths from drifting. Manual trigger: POST /api/admin/woo/safetynet.

    v2.39.1 — 18 July 2026 (Remove stale "(×48)" note from the totals row)

    • Dropped the "(×48)" label on the Xero column's "Total single tins" footer. It was left over from before v2.39, when only the footer converted cartons to tins — now every Xero cell already shows tins (with a "(N cart)" note on carton rows), so the footer is just their sum and the label was misleading (most of that total was never multiplied).

    v2.39 — 18 July 2026 (Monthly SKU Sales: wholesale cartons counted in single tins everywhere)

    • Wholesale carton volume is no longer understated. A Xero CTN carton = 48 tins, but the Total column, the "units" summary card, the % column and the CSV all counted a carton as 1 — while the "Total single tins" footer counted it as 48. So the summary card and the footer never matched (e.g. 660 vs 1,130) and a big wholesale line showed as ~1.5% and sorted last. Now cartons are normalized to single tins at the source, so every figure shares one unit and reconciles: the summary card equals the footer, the % reflects real tin volume, the CSV matches the screen, and carton lines sort by actual volume. The Xero cell shows the tin figure with a small "(N cart)" note so the carton count is still visible.

    v2.38 — 18 July 2026 (Weekly view removed from Monthly SKU Sales)

    • Removed the Monthly/Weekly toggle and week picker from the SKU Sales tab — the view is monthly-only now, per Mel (weekly wasn't needed). This also retires a weekly-view date off-by-one that showed NA users a phantom empty first period. The underlying weekly data plumbing is now unused and will be stripped in a follow-up cleanup (left in place here to avoid touching the shared monthly data-loaders on a live deploy).

    v2.37 — 18 July 2026 (Data Health de-alarmed + FBA sellable-stock + NA colour-coding fixes)

    • "Amazon rows missing line id" no longer cries wolf. The v2.36 guard counted every pre-update row across the last 6 months — including frozen historical months that never re-fetch — so it showed a scary amber warning that could never fully clear. It now looks only at the active ranges (this + last month, which re-fetch within ~24h) and reads as a calm green "these rows predate the update, clearing automatically." The real dedup invariant is still the "Amazon duplicate order lines" check, which stays green with no cleanup.
    • FBA "sellable" stock no longer overstated during restocks. fulfillable read fulfillableQuantity || totalQuantity, so a genuine 0 sellable (stock all inbound/reserved) fell through to the total and counted inbound units as sellable — under-flagging reorders. Now a real 0 stays 0 (?? 0), only falling back to the total when the detail block is truly absent.
    • NA Forecast & Packaging colour-coding restored. Those tabs referenced CSS variables that don't exist here (--danger, --amber, --pink-bg, --pink), so critical/overdue reorder cells rendered near-black and the To-Order column lost its pink tint. Aliased to the real tokens (the AU side was fixed back in v2.2.70; NA was missed).

    v2.36 — 18 July 2026 (Amazon SKU dedup made structural — no more manual cleanup)

    • The Amazon "Run cleanup" chore is gone. Amazon SKU quantities no longer creep upward with no real sales, and the Data Health dedup check now stays green on its own — no periodic cleaning. Root cause: the report window filters on last-updated/shipment date, but rows were stored by purchase date and cleared by a date-window DELETE that keyed on purchase date — three mismatched axes, so re-fetching an active range left stale copies behind that the SKU totals then summed.
    • Fix: a stable per-line key. amazon_items now captures shipment_item_id (migration 0008) with a UNIQUE index; ingest supersedes the whole re-fetched range_label and upserts on the line id, so exactly one row survives per real order line — even if a line migrates between report windows. Duplicates are now structurally impossible, not cleaned up after the fact.
    • New Data Health checks: "Amazon duplicate order lines" (the true 0-or-bust invariant, counted by shipment_item_id) and a "rows missing line id" guard that trips if the report ever stops delivering the column.
    • One-time: legacy pre-0008 rows carry no line id and clear as active ranges re-fetch; the existing "Run cleanup" button still handles any older residue.

    v2.35.2 — 11 May 2026 (NA changelog cleanup)

    • Removed inadvertent v2.35 + v2.35.1 entries from this NA panel. Those bumps were AU-only changes (Phase 2b CIN7 work) that should have stayed in the AU changelog only per the per-region versioning rule. NA dashboard behaviour is unchanged since v2.34.2 — the AU work didn't touch any NA code paths. Cleanup logged on the AU side as v2.2.5.

    v2.34.2 — 10 May 2026 (AU Inventory: FBA branch-name fix + warehouse vs FBA split)

    • FBA branch-name match was broken silently. v2.34's fetchStockBySku matched the FBA branch with strict equality against 'amazonfba' (no space). CIN7's actual branch name is "Amazon FBA" (with a space) — confirmed from the CIN7 UI — so the equality always failed, leaving fba_cin7: 0 for every SKU. Spot-checks: AU-OG-NPO-35 should report 484 at FBA, AU-CL-BCF-35 152, AU-FF-VLB-35 309, all of which were reading 0. Switched to a whitespace-insensitive case-insensitive substring (/amazon\s*fba/i) so we tolerate Amazon FBA, AmazonFBA, Amazon-FBA, and any future region-tagged variants like Amazon FBA AU.
    • Warehouse vs FBA split — the Inventory tab now treats them as separate buckets. Previously v2.34 reported soh as the sum of every CIN7 branch, conflating physical-on-site stock with Amazon FBA stock that you can't redirect to fulfil Woo / wholesale / EDI orders. Now each row carries three buckets: soh (total, kept for backwards compat), warehouse_soh (everything not at FBA — Ingleburn + any other non-FBA branch), and fba_cin7 (the FBA branch alone). Mirror fields exist for avail and incoming. Aggregation lives in fetchStockBySku in src/cin7.js; SRT-tray rollup contributions land on the warehouse bucket only since FBA never holds trays.
    • Inventory table column relabel. The CIN7-prefixed columns are renamed to make the bucket explicit:
      • CIN7 on hand → Warehouse on hand
      • CIN7 available → Warehouse available
      • CIN7 on order → Warehouse on order
      • FBA AU (CIN7 mirror) — same column, but now actually populated. Gets read of the FBA-only bucket.
      • FBA AU (Amazon · truth) — unchanged Phase 3 placeholder; fills in once AU-side SP-API credentials are configured.
      Tooltips updated to spell out which bucket each column shows. The table sorts by warehouse SOH (descending) since that's the operationally relevant ordering — biggest reorderable stock at the top.
    • Status badge thresholds run on warehouse SOH. A SKU with 200 at FBA and 0 in the warehouse is operationally out of stock for everything except Amazon, so the “Low stock < 100” / “Out of stock” / “Negative stock” checks now fire off warehouse_soh rather than total. FBA sync drift detection (|fba_amz - fba_cin7| > 10) is unchanged.
    • KPI cards rebalanced — new “Tins at Amazon FBA” card. The card row goes from 5 to 6 tiles. Loose tins on hand now shows the warehouse-only number (with “available · warehouse only” in the sub-line so it’s explicit). New Tins at Amazon FBA tile next to it surfaces the FBA bucket separately so it’s visible at a glance without clicking through. Cartons on hand and Discontinued tins are also warehouse-only now.
    • CSV export updated to match. The au-inventory.csv now exports Warehouse on hand, Warehouse available, Warehouse on order alongside the existing FBA Amazon (truth) and FBA CIN7 (mirror) columns, plus a CIN7 total on hand sanity column for any downstream sheet that wants to reconcile against the legacy total. Header row matches the on-screen labels.
    • Static-fallback compatibility preserved. When the live /api/au/inventory fetch fails and the dashboard falls back to window.AU_DATA from /au-data.js, the static rows don’t carry the new warehouse_* fields. Three small accessor helpers (auWhSoh, auWhAvail, auWhIncoming) read warehouse fields when present and otherwise derive them as total − FBA from the static soh + fba_cin7 — so the warehouse-vs-FBA split still renders correctly under fallback.

    v2.34.1 — 10 May 2026 (CIN7 rate-limit handling: pacing + backoff + serial fetch)

    • First load of /api/au/inventory failed with HTTP 500 wrapping CIN7’s 429 Too Many Requests. CIN7 Omni rate-limits to 3 calls/sec, 60/min, 5,000/day. The v2.34 implementation paginated /Stock and /Products with no inter-page delay AND ran them in parallel via Promise.all, which roughly doubled the rate and tripped the per-second cap immediately on a cold cache.
    • Three-layer fix in src/cin7.js:
      • Inter-page pacing. cin7FetchAll now sleeps PAGE_DELAY_MS = 400 between pagination calls (after the first), targeting ~2.5 calls/sec to stay safely under the 3/sec cap.
      • Serial Stock + Products. buildAuInventoryPayload awaits Stock then Products instead of running them in parallel via Promise.all. Roughly halves peak rate at the cost of a couple of extra seconds wall-time on a cold cache — still well within Worker CPU/duration budgets.
      • 429 retry with exponential backoff. cin7Fetch retries up to 3 times on a 429 response (1s → 2s → 4s) before bubbling. Other 4xx/5xx remain fatal because retrying a malformed query or an upstream bug just wastes the daily quota. Network-level fetch() throws also retry once at the first delay so a transient TCP blip doesn’t fail the build.
    • Defence-in-depth. The three layers stack: pacing keeps us under the limit, serialising halves the burst rate, and retry covers the case where CIN7’s minute or daily counter is already partially spent from another consumer (cron, manual smoke tests, etc.). On a warm KV cache (15-min TTL from v2.34) we don’t hit CIN7 at all, so this only matters on the first request after deploy or after the cache expires.
    • Frontend graceful-degradation worked perfectly under failure. Inventory tab showed CIN7 fallback (static) chip with the full 429 error in the tooltip, and rendered the static rows from /au-data.js behind it — exactly the design intent. No diagnostic guesswork was needed; the UI surfaced the upstream message directly.

    v2.34 — 10 May 2026 (AU Phase 2a: live CIN7 inventory)

    • AU Inventory tab now reads live from CIN7 Omni. The Inventory rows on the AU dashboard are no longer pulled from the static /au-data.js snapshot — they're fetched from a new GET /api/au/inventory endpoint that builds them in real time from CIN7's /Products + /Stock. Sales by Channel, Monthly SKU Sales, Purchase Orders, Refunds and the placeholders still ship from /au-data.js for now and migrate in subsequent PRs.
    • New src/cin7.js module. Ports the parked Express CIN7 Omni client (backend/server.js in the Drive folder) to the Worker: btoa() for base64, c.env for secrets, paginated cin7Fetch + cin7FetchAll with a 200-page safety cap. Mounted at app.route('/api/au', auRoutes). Two endpoints today: GET /api/au/cin7/status (smallest proof-of-life — pings /Branches with rows=1) and GET /api/au/inventory.
    • Server-side AU SKU normalisation. The same rules that AU Dashboard/scripts/build_data.py applies (CTN ×48, WC- ×54, SRT ×12, MIX ×72, B-prefix distinct, Spicy Chai discontinued) are mirrored in JS so the live inventory rows match the shape and roll-up the static dataset has, byte-for-byte. Keep normalize_sku in build_data.py and normalizeAuSku in src/cin7.js in sync — canonical doc at AU Dashboard/au-sku-rules.md.
    • Per-branch stock aggregation. CIN7 Omni returns per-(productOption × branch) rows; we sum stockOnHand, available, and openPurchaseOrders across branches per SKU, while pulling out the AmazonFBA branch separately as fba_cin7 so it can be compared against the SP-API truth (Phase 3) and any drift >10 units flags a CIN7 sync issue.
    • KV-cached 15 min, with stale-fallback on upstream error. Cache key au:inventory:v1; pass ?refresh=1 to force a fresh upstream pull. If CIN7 errors and a cached snapshot exists, the endpoint returns the stale snapshot with staleFallback: true + an error field instead of failing the request. Same pattern as SalesBinder's /inventory.
    • Frontend graceful degradation. initAuDashboard still renders static rows immediately so there's no perceived lag; a background fetch of /api/au/inventory then swaps AU_INVENTORY_DATA for the live rows and re-renders the Inventory tab. A new source-chip in the Inventory table header shows CIN7 live / CIN7 cached / CIN7 fallback (static) so it's visible at a glance which dataset you're looking at. Failed fetch keeps the static rows — no broken view.
    • Wrangler secrets: CIN7_USERNAME + CIN7_CONNECTION_KEY (HTTP Basic auth, values from CIN7 → Integrations → API). Set with npx wrangler secret put; visible in npx wrangler secret list. Same shape the parked Express server consumed, so the values lift cleanly out of the legacy backend/.env.
    • Phase 2 plan. Phase 2a = inventory live (this PR). Phase 2b = Sales by Channel, Refunds, Purchase Orders also live from CIN7 (SalesOrders, PurchaseOrders endpoints, channel attribution per au-sku-rules.md). Phase 3 = Amazon AU SP-API + Metorik (Woo) live; the fba_amz column on the Inventory tab populates then and unlocks drift detection.

    v2.33 — 10 May 2026 (AU dashboard goes live: Phase 1 static-data port from prototype)

    • Australia dashboard is now live. The full six-tab layout from AU Dashboard/au-dashboard-prototype.html has been ported into public/index.html behind the existing region toggle. Tabs: Sales by Channel (default landing), Monthly SKU Sales, Inventory SKUs, Purchase Orders, Forecasting (placeholder), Packaging (placeholder). The previous Overview / SKUs / inventory-only placeholder shell is replaced.
    • Real April 2026 data shipped. The AU Dashboard/au-data.js file (generated by scripts/build_data.py from CIN7, Metorik, and Amazon SP-API exports) is copied into public/au-data.js and loaded as a static asset at page load. Headline numbers: Woo 38,223 tins · Amazon 1,866 · Coles 78,336 · Woolies 73,728 · Distributors 1,648 · refunds −110 · total ~193,800 tins / ~$1.05M AUD after refunds.
    • Five-channel split surfaces supermarket lumpiness. Coles & Woolworths stay separate (rather than merged into "Wholesale") because their order cycles are independent and lumpy. Channel colours: Woo pink, Amazon amber, Coles red, Woolies green, Distributors blue.
    • Global month picker drives Sales by Channel and Monthly SKU Sales together. Trailing-6-month chart re-anchors to the selected month. Currently only April 2026 is loaded; pipeline trivially extends as more monthly exports drop into raw/.
    • Inventory tab shows base tins + cartons (×48) as separate physical rows per Melanie's design (the team packs cartons; SRTs roll into base SKUs). FBA Amazon column is a placeholder pending an SP-API export; CIN7 mirror column is live.
    • Purchase Orders tab: 26 open POs from CIN7 with type badges (Finished / Soap / Tins / Packaging / Other) plus Type and Supplier filters. A second table mirrors the CA dashboard's per-SKU drilldown with risk badges (critical / low / monitor / stable / no-sales / inactive, with -incoming suffix when a PO is on the way).
    • This is Phase 1 of the AU rollout — static-data first, mirroring how the CA dashboard was built. Phase 2 will replace /au-data.js with a /api/au/data endpoint backed by KV/D1, wiring CIN7 Omni first then Amazon AU SP-API and Metorik (Woo). The existing /api/cin7/* endpoints in src/index.js are untouched and ready to be re-pointed at the new UI.
    • CSV exports on every AU table (Sales, Monthly SKU, Inventory, Purchase Orders, Incoming-by-SKU). Helpers escapeHtml and toCsvField from the original AU shell are kept; the legacy CIN7-fetch scaffolding (fetchJson, auState, refreshAuOverview, etc.) is removed and will be re-introduced in Phase 2 against the new shape.
    • AU dashboard footer carries its own version label — bumped from v2.0 to v2.1 with a per-region changelog. The main app version (top of changelog) is bumped from v2.32 → v2.33 since the change touches public/index.html.

    v2.32 — 8 May 2026 (Packaging tab: Envelopes, SRT trays, PCIs, Watchlist)

    • Packaging tab now covers paper packaging, not just tins. Four new sections appear below the existing tin table: SRT Packaging Trays (per scent), Envelopes, Postcard Inserts (PCIs), and an Inventory Watchlist (blank cartons, blank envelopes, and Purolator boxes for well.ca). Each demand-modelled section pulls live On Hand / Incoming / Unit Cost from SalesBinder and surfaces a runway + Order By date so reorders aren’t reactive.
    • Demand derivation per section:
      • SRT trays (P-CA-SRT-{scent}-{form}): 3-month trailing average of Woo SRT bundles sold for that scent (CA + US, raw count) + Xero CTN cartons sold for that scent × 4 SRTs per carton. The Woo SRT/mo and CTN×4/mo columns show the breakdown so the demand is auditable. Captures raw SRT bundle quantities into a new WOO_SRT_BUNDLES_BY_MARKET store during loadWooSales (the existing SKU_CHANNEL_DATA per-base-SKU bucket multiplies by 12 since v2.30, so it can’t be used here).
      • Envelopes (P-ENV-A1): 1 per Woo order (CA + US), 3-month trailing average of OC.wooCA + OC.wooUS. Only the printed envelope is in active rotation; P-BLANK-ENV-A1 is emergency backup stock and lives in the watchlist.
      • PCIs (NP_INSERTS): 1 per Woo order (CA + US), same calc as envelopes.
      • Watchlist (P-CA-BLANK-CTN-2SRT/-4SRT, P-BLANK-ENV-A1, NOPONG-PURO-BOXES): no demand math — flagged for inventory visibility only (status reads “Below threshold” or “Negative stock” for at-a-glance attention). Demand for these doesn’t trace cleanly through Woo/Xero so we eyeball stock when Logiwa reconciles.
    • Lead time defaults to 4 months and is overridable per SKU. Until Lauren confirms supplier-specific timings for paper packaging, runway / Order By math uses a 4-month lead time (matches Guanqiao tins). Per-SKU overrides live in the Worker at src/salesbinder.js → PACKAGING_OVERRIDES.leadTimes (e.g. { ‘P-ENV-A1’: 3, ‘NP_INSERTS’: 2 }) — edit + redeploy when the actual lead times are nailed down.
    • SalesBinder packaging endpoint now returns five categories instead of one. /api/salesbinder/packaging now classifies items as tins (T-*), srt (P-CA-SRT-*), envelopes (P-ENV-*), inserts (NP_INSERTS), or blankCartons (P-CA-BLANK-CTN-*SRT, P-BLANK-ENV-*, NOPONG-PURO-BOXES — the inventory-watchlist bucket). Cache key bumped to salesbinder:packaging:v2 so the new schema and reclassification take effect on the first request after deploy — no manual ?refresh=true needed.
    • Per-section CSV exports. SRT, Envelopes, and PCI tables each have their own Export CSV button so reorder spreadsheets can be pulled independently.
    • Source of truth note. SalesBinder is treated as the live estimate; Logiwa’s end-of-month physical count is authoritative. Surfaced in the table hint so the dashboard doesn’t pretend SalesBinder numbers are perfect mid-month.

    v2.31 — 8 May 2026 (Close workers.dev side door, drop ADMIN_KEY, self-serve cleanup button)

    • Disabled the unauthenticated logistics-dashboard.no-pong.workers.dev URL. Cloudflare gives every Worker a default <name>.workers.dev hostname; ours had been live since the Step 3 hello-world and sat outside Cloudflare Access — meaning anyone who knew or guessed the URL could read company sales data without signing in. Set workers_dev: false in wrangler.jsonc so the Worker is reachable only at logistics.apps.nopong.com, which is fully behind Google Workspace SSO. The deploy-time URL list will now show only the custom hostname.
    • Removed the X-Admin-Key middleware from /api/admin/*. The ADMIN_KEY Wrangler secret existed solely as an application-layer fence around the workers.dev exposure; with that hostname disabled, the key was friction with no security benefit, and it was actively blocking dashboard-driven admin workflows (the new "Run cleanup" button below couldn't have worked without this change). Cloudflare Access on logistics.apps.nopong.com is now the single auth layer for every route, mirroring the precedent already set by /api/logiwa/upload. The old Wrangler secret can stay set or be deleted with npx wrangler secret delete ADMIN_KEY — nothing in the code consumes it anymore.
    • "Run cleanup" button on the Amazon dedupe Data Health check. When the check fails, an inline pink button now appears next to the detail text. Clicking it POSTs to /api/admin/amazon/dedupe-items using the user's existing SSO session (no key needed), shows progress in-button ("Cleaning…" → "Cleaned N rows"), busts the Amazon sales cache, and re-runs the health checks so the badge flips green without a manual refresh. Closes the loop the v2.29 detail text opened ("Run POST /api/admin/amazon/dedupe-items to clean") — previously the only way to act on that instruction was a terminal curl with a key only Chris could read back.

    v2.30 — 4 May 2026 (Monthly SKU Sales now in tins, not Woo line items)

    • Monthly SKU Sales tab + CSV export now show tin-equivalent counts for SRT bundles and WC- wholesale cartons. Previously the Woo per-SKU bucket (powering the Monthly/Weekly SKU Sales tab and downloadMonthlyCSV) collapsed variant SKUs into their base SKU (e.g. CA-SRT-OG-NPO-35x12 → CA-OG-NPO-35) but added the raw line quantity — so a 12-pack bundle counted as 1 tin, not 12, and a WC- wholesale carton counted as 1 tin, not 48. The TINS data structure (run rates, forecasting, top-level "tins shipped" cards) was already applying the multiplier correctly — the bug was confined to the per-SKU view layer. For April 2026 CA Woo this meant CA-OG-NPO-35 displayed as 1,239 tins when 6 SRT bundles bumped it to 1,305 (a 66-tin gap), with similar shortfalls on every base SKU that had SRT or WC- sales in the period. Total April CA Woo "tins" was under-reported by 187 (17 SRT bundles × 11 missed-tin-each).
    • Fix is one-line-per-bucket in loadWooSales — multiply info.monthlyQty and info.weeklyQty by norm.multiplier when populating SKU_CHANNEL_DATA[wooBucketKey] / SKU_WEEKLY_DATA[wooBucketKey]. Revenue (monthly) stays un-multiplied because a 12-pack is one transaction with one revenue line.
    • CTN cartons (Xero CA-CTN-*-48) intentionally NOT rolled into base tin SKU. Per Melanie's forecasting model, cartons are tracked as their own SKUs in the Monthly SKU Sales view and forecast separately. The existing forecasting code (around cartonTinDemand) already accounts for tin-equivalent demand from carton sales when computing inventory burn rates — that path is unchanged.
    • Validated against Seller Central + Xero exports for April 2026 CA after deploy: per-SKU Amazon tins matched validation file exactly (1,478 total). Per-SKU Woo tins now match expected post-multiplier values. Per-SKU Xero values match validation file exactly (672/11/3/1).

    v2.29 — 4 May 2026 (Amazon SKU monthly doubling fix)

    • Fixed Monthly SKU Sales over-reporting Amazon by 2x or more. The Reports ingest path (ingestReadyJobs in src/amazon.js) scoped its pre-insert DELETE by report_job_id only. Every ~24h, the Reports cron re-fetched each active range (this month + last month) and the new fetch got a new report_jobs row with a fresh id — so the DELETE only wiped its own (empty) rows and the prior job's rows for the same date range stayed in amazon_items. handleAmazonSalesRequest aggregates across job_ids, so per-SKU monthly quantities scaled by the number of cron cycles that had touched that range. Older months (frozen ranges, never re-fetched) reported correctly; the most recent two months drifted further from reality every day. Both CA and US affected. The DELETE is now scoped by the job's date window (data_start_time / data_end_time) instead of by job id, so the new fetch always replaces the old data for that window.
    • One-time cleanup endpoint for the duplicate rows already in D1. POST /api/admin/amazon/dedupe-items walks every range_label per market, keeps only rows from the most-recent ingested job, deletes the rest, and busts the sales cache. Idempotent — safe to re-run. Returns a per-(market, range_label) summary of kept vs deleted counts. The fix above stops new duplicates accumulating; this endpoint clears the existing ones so the dashboard reflects clean numbers immediately rather than waiting for old rows to age out of the 6-month window.
    • New Data Health check: "Amazon SKU rows duplicated across jobs." The reason this bug shipped green for weeks is that none of the existing checks could see it — sum reconciliation, duplicate order IDs, market separation, and SKU mapping coverage all stayed happy because the duplication was structural at the row level, not at the SKU or order level. New check counts amazon_items rows tied to superseded (non-latest) ingested jobs per range_label and flags red if any exist. /api/audit exposes the counts as amazon.caSupersededRowCount / amazon.usSupersededRowCount; the badge will catch this class of regression on the next data refresh going forward.

    v2.28.1 — 30 April 2026 (Reports activity log + chip order)

    • Reports rows in the activity log are now labelled amazon-reports instead of inheriting amazon from the underlying sync_logs row. Previously a heartbeat tick read as "amazon CA — idle (all ranges fresh)" sitting next to "amazon CA — +1 order", which was correct underneath but visually indistinguishable. Now reads as "amazon-reports CA" and groups with the existing report_jobs entries.
    • Sync bar chips now follow an explicit order instead of alphabetical: Woo CA/US → Amz CA/US → Amz Reports CA/US → FBA Inv CA/US → SalesBinder → Logiwa → Xero. Reads left-to-right by category instead of mixing Reports between Orders. Unknown chips fall to the end and sort alphabetically among themselves so a new connector chip appears predictably.

    v2.28 — 30 April 2026 (Amazon Reports visibility)

    • Amazon Reports now have their own freshness chips. Amz Reports CA and Amz Reports US joined the per-source row. Previously the only Amazon chip was Orders, so when Reports was the connector that mattered (it drives Monthly SKU Sales) there was no direct way to see whether it was running.
    • Activity log now sees the Reports cron tick every 15 min. Previously runAmazonReportsTick only left a trace via report_jobs.updated_at — once all 24 ranges were stably ingested, no row's timestamp moved and the activity dropdown went silent for hours even though cron was firing every 15 min. The function now writes a heartbeat row to sync_logs on every tick (action='amazon-reports') regardless of whether work happened. Idle ticks render as "idle (all ranges fresh)"; busy ticks render as e.g. "+1 job ingested, +2 polls advanced"; rate-limited or errored ticks surface the upstream error inline.
    • Reports chip tooltip shows ingested/total ranges. Hover Amz Reports CA → "Ranges: 12/12 ingested" alongside the cron schedule, so it's immediately obvious whether a backfill is still in flight (e.g. "9/12 ingested") or steady state.
    • Reports tooltip explains the "mostly idle" cadence. The schedule hint reads "Cron: every 15 min … Most ticks are no-ops; active ranges (this/last month) refresh every 24h, historical ranges never re-run." — pre-empting the "why does it look stale?" question that comes up when nothing's ingested for 15+ hours during normal operation.

    v2.27.2 — 29 April 2026 (favicon)

    • Added the No Pong brand favicon at /favicon.png and linked from the document head.

    v2.27.1 — 29 April 2026 (changelog reveal fix)

    • Clicking the version number now actually shows the changelog. The panel was opening fine but landed below the fold from a footer-visible scroll position — viewer saw only the top ~18px and reasonably concluded the link did nothing. Toggling now scrolls the panel into view.

    v2.27 — 29 April 2026 (FBA inventory + SalesBinder crons)

    • FBA inventory now cron-driven. Previously the only refresh path was stale-while-revalidate — a user request to /api/amazon/inventory served the stale snapshot and spawned a background refresh via ctx.waitUntil. That works fine when someone has the dashboard open daily; it doesn't work if nobody opens it for 48h. runAmazonInventoryCronSync now runs from the scheduled handler every 15 min and refreshes either market when its snapshot is > 1h old. Worst-case staleness is now ~1h regardless of dashboard usage. Self-gates on the freshness threshold so 14 of every 16 ticks are free no-ops.
    • SalesBinder now has a cron. Previously it had no automatic refresh path at all — the cache only updated when someone hit /api/salesbinder/inventory?refresh=true, which meant the chip just told you when one of us last clicked refresh by hand. runSalesBinderCronSync runs every 15 min and refreshes either inventory or packaging when their snapshot is > 4h old. Worst-case staleness ~4h, which is plenty fresh for "what's in the warehouse" and keeps the SalesBinder API call rate negligible.
    • FBA Inv CA / FBA Inv US chips added to the per-source row. They were already in /api/sync-status output but the frontend wasn't reading them.
    • Tooltip schedules updated to match the new behaviour: SalesBinder no longer says "on-demand only", FBA Inv now says "refreshed when snapshot is older than 1 hour. Checked every 15 min by the cron — upstream pull only fires when stale."

    v2.26 — 29 April 2026 (chip + refresh fixes, sub-header layout, all 7 sources, schedule tooltips)

    • Sub-header row. The brand row (logo + region switch) now stands alone; the data status & controls live on a thin sticky sub-header below it (Live / Data Health / ··· / Activity / Newest sync / Refresh). Less crowded, controls still pinned to the absolute right.
    • Per-source chips finally show real timing. loadWooSales, loadAmazonSales, and loadXeroInvoices no longer overwrite sourceSyncTimes from their own data.lastSync field — those endpoints are KV-cached, so a fresh deploy left the chips clobbered with stale timestamps from the cached payload. /api/sync-status (uncached, reads sync_state + KV markers + xero_tokens.updated_at directly) is now the only writer.
    • All 7 sources in the chip row. Added Logiwa, SalesBinder, and Xero alongside the existing Woo CA / Woo US / Amz CA / Amz US chips. Logiwa's chip shows the last CSV upload time; SalesBinder's shows the last on-demand pull (it has no cron); Xero's shows the most recent OAuth token refresh.
    • Sync-schedule tooltips. Hover any chip to see what it is and when the next pull is due — e.g. "Cron: every 15 min (:00, :15, :30, :45). Next pull due ~10:45 pm (7m)." for Woo / Amazon. SalesBinder's tooltip is honest: "On-demand pull only — no cron." Logiwa's: "Manual CSV upload — no cron. Re-upload the export to refresh." Xero's: "OAuth token auto-refreshes on use when expired (~30 min lifespan, rotated)."
    • "Last updated" now shows the newest upstream sync instead of the browser's clock. Reads MAX(sourceSyncTimes) — "Newest sync: 29 Apr 2026, 10:30 pm" means the dashboard data is at most as fresh as the most-recently-synced source. Falls back to "Loaded: <time>" if no source has reported yet.
    • Refresh button actually refreshes — what it CAN refresh. Clicking it now sets a __forceRefresh flag that causes loadWooSales/loadAmazonSales to pass ?refresh=1, busting the 15-min KV cache and re-aggregating from D1. Refresh re-renders from cached data; it does not pull from upstream — the cron is what does that, every 15 min. Tooltip on the button explains this.
    • Header layout split. Left cluster (Live badge, Data Health) describes data state; right cluster (Activity, Newest sync, Refresh) is grouped controls and is pinned to the absolute right edge with margin-left: auto. Visual gap between them grows with the viewport.

    v2.25 — 29 April 2026 (data freshness & activity log)

    • Per-source "Last sync" chips now reflect every cron tick, not just ticks that found new data. runBackfillChunk (Woo) and runAmazonOrdersChunk (Amazon Orders) now update sync_state.last_synced_at on every successful run, with explicit ISO-8601 UTC formatting (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', 'now')) so JS new Date() parses as UTC, not as local time on the viewer's machine. Watermark column still tracks "last NEW data" for the cases that need it.
    • Data Health badge no longer stuck on "Checking…". Check 7 (daily-data-flow) was throwing RangeError: Invalid time value on monthWindowStart + '-01' because that field is already YYYY-MM-DD; fixed to use it directly. The thrown error was silently aborting the whole runDataHealthChecks function before renderHealthBadge could run.
    • New /api/import-log endpoint & "Activity" dropdown in the header. Click the chip to see every cron tick (including the quiet ones that found 0 new orders — those PROVE the cron ran), every Amazon Reports state-machine transition, every Logiwa/SalesBinder/FBA inventory snapshot, and the latest Xero token refresh. Defaults to last 24h, can switch to last 7 days. "Hide quiet ticks" toggle filters out the 0-orders rows once you've confirmed the system is alive. Re-fetches on every panel-open — the point is to see what's true RIGHT NOW.
    • Diagnostic timestamps surfaced from D1 are now normalised to ISO-Z at read time (toIsoUtc helper in src/diagnostics.js) — older rows written before this release still display correctly without waiting for an overwrite.

    v2.24 — 29 April 2026 (Logiwa ported)

    • Logiwa connector ported to the Worker. The dashboard's Logiwa upload tile now talks to /api/logiwa/inventory on the Worker instead of the legacy Express server — same client-side SheetJS parse, same POST shape ({ inventory, uploadedAt, fileName }), no frontend change.
    • Snapshot lives in KV under logiwa:inventory:current — one key, overwrite-on-upload. Deliberately no R2, no D1, no migration: Logiwa stock is point-in-time, not append-only, and the parsed JSON is small enough to live in a single KV value.
    • New /api/logiwa/test mirrors the connectivity-check pattern from SalesBinder/Amazon/Xero — returns { connected, count, fileName, uploadedAt } when a snapshot exists.
    • /api/status.logiwa.connected now flips automatically based on whether a snapshot is in KV. The dashboard's loadAllData dispatches loadLogiwaInventory() after an upload without a manual refresh.
    • No new secrets, no new bindings, no new migrations — this connector reuses the existing CACHE KV namespace. Auth is Cloudflare Access at the edge; if curl-upload becomes a real workflow later we can add a parallel /api/admin/logiwa/upload route.
    • Defensive whitespace strip in scripts/load-secrets.sh: leading/trailing spaces on values are now trimmed after the existing quote-strip step. Caught last session when a stray leading space on XERO_CLIENT_ID produced a +-encoded space in the OAuth URL and Xero rejected with unauthorized_client.

    v2.23 — 29 April 2026 (Xero ported)

    • Xero connector ported to the Worker — OAuth2 auth-code flow with lazy refresh, plus the three legacy API endpoints. Replaces the local xero-node SDK (Node-only) with direct REST against identity.xero.com and api.xero.com.
    • Migration 0004 adds xero_tokens — single-row table (CHECK id = 1) holding access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, tenant_id, tenant_name, scope. Single-tenant by design (No Pong has one Xero org).
    • Auth routes at /auth/xero (consent), /auth/xero/callback (token exchange + tenant capture), and /auth/xero/disconnect (delete tokens). CSRF state in KV (10-min TTL, single-use). Refresh tokens rotate on every refresh — the new one is persisted, defensively falling back to the prior one if Xero ever omits it.
    • /api/xero/purchase-orders — paginated, filtered to deliveryDate ≥ start of current month, excludes BILLED/DELETED, strips tin packaging line items (T- prefix). KV-cached 6h with offline fallback.
    • /api/xero/invoices — last 6 months ACCREC, ordered Date DESC, excludes Metorik + Amazon weekly-summary contacts (those come from the Woo + Amazon connectors directly). KV-cached 6h with offline fallback.
    • /api/xero/org — first organisation info. KV-cached 24h.
    • /api/status auto-flips xero.connected from the xero_tokens row, with a cached sub-flag so the dashboard can show "connected (cached)" when the token is gone but a snapshot is still serving.
    • redactSecrets grew Xero-aware patterns — bearer JWTs (eyJ….….…) plus query-param forms oauth_token=, code=, id_token=. One edit, applied at every layer per v2.16 convention.

    v2.22 — 29 April 2026 (FBA SKU map gap fill)

    • Seven seller SKUs that FBA Inventory was returning unmatched are now in AMZ_SKU_MAP: CA-OG-BCF-35, EX-DHDC-UJK7 (Flower Power FNSKU alias), SC-NPO-35-GS1 (Spicy Chai Original GS1-barcode variant), US-OG-NPO-35, US-OG-NPO-35-AM, US-OG-BCF-35, US-SC-NPO-35. US-only codes follow the existing convention of normalising to the CA dashboard SKU for combined reporting.
    • Effect: cross-channel SKU rollups will pick up these rows next time the Amazon Reports state machine ingests a range that includes them. The legacy frontend's own AMZ_INV_MAP_CA/AMZ_INV_MAP_US already covered most of these for the FBA tile, so this closes the silent-drop gap on the Worker side.

    v2.21 — 29 April 2026 (D1-read sweep)

    • Four legacy endpoints ported from the Express backend in one pass — the dashboard's recent-orders rail, sync-status bar, Data Health panel, and buying-tool sparklines now all serve from the Worker.
    • /api/woo/recent-orders?market=CA|US — last 24h of completed/processing orders, top 50, ordered newest first. Reads directly from D1 (no KV layer — the data's small, the rail's a "live" surface).
    • /api/sync-status — per-source freshness map (wooCA/wooUS/amzCA/amzUS with lastSync+orderCount, plus amzInvCA/amzInvUS from the FBA inventory KV snapshot). Powers the source-time chips in the status bar.
    • /api/audit — read-only data audit. Per-market Woo monthly revenue + per-SKU rollups + daily order counts; per-market Amazon SKU counts + filter-signal coverage + unmatched/wrong-market counts from report_jobs. The legacy "CA vs US byte-identical caches" check is structurally impossible under the per-source D1 schema, so caUsIdentical is hard-false and the frontend's Data Health panel reads divergence from the filter-signal fields instead.
    • /api/buying-tool-history — 18+ months of per-SKU monthly sales plus manually curated allocation buffers, baked into the Worker bundle as src/buying-tool-history.js. Edit + redeploy when the spreadsheet refreshes.

    v2.20 — 29 April 2026 (Amazon FBA Inventory)

    • Amazon FBA Inventory ported to the Worker — /api/amazon/inventory?market=CA|US serves a KV-cached snapshot of FBA stock per seller SKU (asin, fnSku, fulfillable, inbound, reserved, unfulfillable). The dashboard's FBA CA / FBA US stock tiles now populate again.
    • Stale-while-revalidate caching — KV entry lives 24h but is treated as fresh only for 1h. After 1h the route serves the stale snapshot immediately and spawns a background refresh via ctx.waitUntil, so users never block on a paginated cold fetch (~10–15s) except on an empty cache. ?refresh=true forces a synchronous refresh. Falls back to cache on Amazon rate-limit.
    • Each inventory row now carries a dashboardSku (mapped via matchAmzItemToSku) so cross-channel SKU rollups (Woo + Amazon Orders + FBA stock) can join cleanly. Top-level response surfaces matched/unmatched counts so silent SKU drops are visible.

    v2.19 — 29 April 2026 (rate-limit error visibility)

    • Amazon SP-API rate-limit errors hitting postNewReportJobs are now persisted to report_jobs.error and surfaced in the cron tick response (postError field) instead of being silently swallowed. Caught when v2.18's POST-all-jobs-in-one-tick pattern hit Amazon's /reports rate cap and the bail-out had no audit trail — jobs just sat in pending with no amazon_report_id and no error message.

    v2.18 — 29 April 2026 (Amazon Reports parallelism)

    • Reports state machine now POSTs all un-POSTed pending jobs in one tick (cheap, ~500ms each) instead of being capped at maxJobsPerPhase=3. Previously the SELECT+LIMIT meant lower-id jobs hogged the polling budget and higher-id ones (e.g. 2026-02-1h, 2026-03-1h, etc.) never got POSTed to Amazon at all — they starved indefinitely behind in-flight lower-id jobs that hadn’t completed yet.
    • Cron tick now: Phase A (seed) → Phase B0 (POST all un-POSTed) → Phase B1 (poll up to N=3 in-flight) → Phase C (ingest up to N=3 ready). All under the same 25s wall-clock budget. Result: all 12 ranges per market enter Amazon’s queue within 1-2 cron ticks (15-30 min), processed in parallel by Amazon, then ingested as they complete.
    • Tick response shape gains a posted field alongside seeded/polled/ingested so backfill progress is visible at every stage.

    v2.17 — 29 April 2026 (Amazon hardening)

    • SP-API rate-limit handling — spApiRequest + spApiPost now retry once on 429 with the server’s Retry-After (capped at 10s). If still 429, the Orders chunk converts to a graceful error: rate_limited response with watermark unchanged and more: true so curl loops back off rather than aborting.
    • Reports state machine: minimum 90s between polls of the same job (last_polled_at SQL filter). Without this guard, cron + manual admin loops racing each other could burn through MAX_ATTEMPTS in minutes and prematurely mark a still-processing report as failed.
    • REPORT_POLL_MAX_ATTEMPTS bumped 15 → 30. With the 90s spacing guard, this gives Amazon ~45 min of real processing time before we give up — comfortably above the typical 5–15 min generation window.
    • sync_logs gains a 'rate_limited' status value (in addition to 'ok', 'error', 'partial') so audit can distinguish backoff events from real failures.

    v2.16 — 29 April 2026

    • Amazon SP-API ported to the Worker — /api/amazon/sales serves D1-backed monthly revenue + per-SKU breakdowns, same payload shape as /api/woo/sales. KV-cached for 15 min; cron invalidates after each ingest.
    • LWA token cached in KV under amazon:lwa-token with TTL = expires_in − 60s. Each Worker invocation reads from KV first, refreshes only on miss/expiry. Survives the stateless-Worker boundary the legacy in-memory cache couldn’t.
    • Orders flow (monthly revenue): one chunk per cron tick per market, NextToken-paginated, watermark on amazon_orders.purchase_date. Manual catch-up: POST /api/admin/backfill?source=amazon&market=CA.
    • Reports flow (per-SKU): async state machine in report_jobs — pending → ready → ingested | failed. Cron advances up to N=3 jobs per phase per tick with a 25s wall-clock guard. Fallback report type GET_FBA_FULFILLMENT_CUSTOMER_SHIPMENT_SALES_DATA auto-tried when the primary GET_AMAZON_FULFILLED_SHIPMENTS_DATA_GENERAL returns CANCELLED/FATAL.
    • SKU mapping table lifted verbatim from legacy backend/server.js (CA canonical + FNSKU aliases + US-only SKUs that normalise to CA dashboard codes for combined reporting). Unmatched seller SKUs surface in /api/amazon/sales output as rowsUnmatched so silent drops are visible.
    • redactSecrets extracted into src/redact.js — new patterns added for Amazon LWA tokens (Atzr|…, Atza|…) and LWA client secrets (amzn1.oa2-cs.v1.…). One edit, applied at every layer.
    • /api/status auto-flips amazon.connected: true when AMAZON_REFRESH_TOKEN, AMAZON_LWA_CLIENT_ID, and AMAZON_LWA_CLIENT_SECRET are all set.

    v2.15 — 29 April 2026

    • Canonical timezone for all order bucketing — America/Toronto (Eastern). New local_date TEXT column on every order/items table, populated at insert time and used for every monthly/weekly bucket query. Both Woo stores are configured to Eastern in wp-admin (Toronto for CA, New York for US — same DST), so existing 22k+1.9k rows backfill cleanly via substr(date_created, 1, 10) in migration 0003.
    • Rolling 6-month window now flips at midnight Toronto, not midnight UTC — fixes a 4-hour daily drift at month boundaries.
    • Amazon SP-API schema staged: amazon_orders, amazon_items, report_jobs tables (migration 0002), plus pre-seeded sync_state watermarks for CA/US at 2025-10-29 (6-month initial window). Amazon ingestion code lands next session.
    • New src/timezone.js module is the single source of truth for BUSINESS_TZ, toBusinessLocalDate(), buildMonthWindow(), and getWeekKey(). src/woo.js imports from it instead of carrying local copies.

    v2.14 — 29 April 2026

    • SalesBinder ported to the Worker — /api/salesbinder/inventory (finished-goods SOH for the discrepancy check) and /api/salesbinder/packaging (tin runway + blank-pool flags) now serve from KV-cached snapshots, with ?refresh=true to force a re-fetch. Fall-through to cached data when SalesBinder is unreachable.
    • Packaging overrides (excluded SKUs, leBlank flags, retired-SKU re-purposing) baked into the Worker code — edit src/salesbinder.js + redeploy when they change. Frontend still derives committedToPetra from in-transit Petra POs.
    • /api/status auto-flips salesBinder.connected: true when both SALESBINDER_SUBDOMAIN and SALESBINDER_API_KEY secrets are set.

    v2.13 — 29 April 2026 (security fix)

    • Sanitize error responses — wooFetch now catches malformed-URL and unreachable-host fetch failures and emits redacted error messages (no consumer_key/consumer_secret in the response body). Global onError handler also scrubs credential-shaped query params from any err.message before returning. Caught when a paste typo on WOO_US_URL dropped the leading h in https and the ensuing fetch error leaked the Woo US API key & secret into a 500 response (rotated).

    v2.12 — 29 April 2026

    • Cron incremental sync — every 15 minutes the Worker pulls any new Woo orders since the watermark, writes them into D1, and invalidates the relevant /api/woo/sales KV cache key. Dashboard data refreshes itself without manual intervention.
    • Cron silently skips markets without configured secrets, so once WOO_US_* secrets are set US sync starts on the next tick — no code change needed.
    • Backfill chunk runner extracted from the admin route and reused by the cron; sync_logs now records action: 'incremental' vs 'backfill' so manual and scheduled runs are distinguishable in audit.
    • Default chunk size for /api/admin/backfill lowered from 10 to 1 to match the safe ceiling on Workers Free.

    v2.11 — 29 April 2026

    • /api/woo/sales now reads from D1 instead of a 30-day Woo live-fetch — the rolling 6-month revenue chart finally renders all six months of CA history (20,119 orders, validated against Woo).
    • Per-SKU monthly + weekly breakdowns aggregated from orders & order_items via two D1 queries; KV layer kept in front (15-min TTL).
    • Payload now includes dataSource: 'd1', windowStart, ordersInWindow, and watermark; lastSync now reflects sync_state.last_synced_at (true data freshness) rather than request time.

    v2.10 — 23 April 2026

    • Project moved to shared Google Drive folder — dashboard is now team-accessible instead of living on one Mac.
    • Cache relocated from ~/.nopong-cache/ to ../cache/ (project-root cache/ folder), so cached data travels with the project. NOPONG_CACHE_DIR env var can override (used by the packaged app build).
    • Cloud-sync interval disabled by default — redundant now that the cache itself lives on Google Drive. Re-enable with NOPONG_SYNC_TO_CLOUD=true.
    • CACHE_FORMAT_VERSION bumped 2 → 3. Treated as a location-only change: existing v2 caches are stamped to v3 in place on startup — no SP-API rebuild, no data loss.
    • health-check.js now resolves the same cache path as the server (with legacy fallbacks so old installs still work).

    v2.9 — 22 April 2026

    • Fix weekly/monthly mismatch — _parseLocalDate now handles all date formats: WooCommerce dates (no timezone) extract the date directly, Amazon dates (with UTC offset like +00:00 or Z) convert to local time first. Orders near midnight UTC at month boundaries now land in the correct month and week.
    • Targeted cache patching on startup — WooCommerce fetches just March 1 orders (1 API call). Amazon SKU caches auto-refresh in the background with corrected date parsing (takes ~10 min, runs automatically).

    v2.6 — 22 April 2026

    • Add daily data flow health check — detects days with no orders flowing in. Woo CA flags as an error (orders expected every day), Woo US warns only if many gaps (occasional zero-order days are normal)

    v2.5 — 22 April 2026

    • Weekly data now starts from March 1 (was March 2) — captures all orders at start of month
    • Cache auto-detects when WEEKLY_START_DATE changes and triggers full rebuild — prevents stale week keys

    v2.4 — 22 April 2026

    • Fix 24-unit weekly/monthly mismatch — Amazon dates with UTC offsets (e.g. 2026-04-01T03:00:00+00:00) now use local timezone for month extraction, matching the weekly path

    v2.3 — 21 April 2026

    • Fix “Current week” showing future dates with no data — reverted to correct logic where week periods stop at today

    v2.2 — 21 April 2026

    • Fix Amazon SKU data disappearing after server restart — cache migration no longer deletes Amazon reports (they aren't auto-fetched on startup)
    • Fix Data Health Checks stuck on “Checking” — Amazon SKU fetch now has 30s timeout so it doesn't block health checks
    • /api/rebuild-weekly now also rebuilds Amazon SKU reports alongside WooCommerce

    v2.1 — 21 April 2026

    • Weekly view on Monthly SKU Sales tab: toggle between Monthly/Weekly with dedicated dropdown
    • Week periods run Mon–Sun, split at month boundaries so weekly totals align with monthly totals
    • Weekly data available from March 2026 onward across all channels (WooCommerce, Amazon, Xero)
    • Timezone-safe date parsing (_parseLocalDate) — fixes date-only strings shifting across month boundaries in EDT
    • Cache auto-migration: server detects and rebuilds cache files missing weekly data on startup
    • New /api/rebuild-weekly endpoint for manual cache rebuild

    v2.0 — 18 April 2026

    • Forecasting/Buying Tool: conservative rate = MAX(5mo avg, 18mo trimmed avg), growth rate includes YoY compound
    • Allocated buffer auto-calculated (3 weeks of growth rate), net stock = SOH − allocated
    • Progressive coverage: Current Cover, +in transit, +on order (−2mo lead), after new order (−3mo lead)
    • PO suggestions with Order By / Arrives By dates; 12-month planning horizon
    • Historical sales from spreadsheet (Feb 2026 and earlier), API for March 2026+
    • Carton (CTN) SKUs inline with product families; each carton = 48 tins off PO total
    • Flower Power VLB marked as discontinuing — no reorder, sell-through only
    • Excel export with live formulas (growth rate, coverage, costs all recalculate)
    • Product colours updated to match actual tin artwork across all tabs
    • Colour-tinted rows per product family; opaque sticky SKU column
    • Packaging tab: colour-coded rows matching product tins
    • Tab reorder: Monthly SKU Sales moved after Sales by Channel
    • Removed broken Petra Forecast export

    v1.0 — March 2026

    • Initial dashboard: Overview, Sales by Channel, Inventory, SKUs, Purchase Orders, Monthly SKU Sales
    • Live API integrations: WooCommerce, Amazon SP-API, Xero, SalesBinder, Logiwa
    • Stock discrepancy checking (SalesBinder vs Logiwa + FBA)
    • PO tracking with days-to-arrival and stock-out risk flags
    • Packaging tab with tin runway, blank pool management, pallet suggestions
    CIN7 Woo AU Amazon AU Metorik (backup) Xero AU
    Month:
    Source of truth. For Woo and Amazon sales, the channel APIs are the truth — pulled directly from WooCommerce REST (Metorik on backup) and Amazon SP-API. CIN7 mirrors those orders for shipping & fulfilment, so if CIN7's Woo/Amazon totals don't match the channel API, that's a sync problem in CIN7, not a real difference. Coles and Woolworths come into CIN7 directly via EDI (no other source — CIN7 IS the truth for those). Distributors are CIN7 sales orders that aren't EDI.
    Revenue by channel — 6 months ending May 2026 (AUD)
    Selected month breakdown — May 2026
    Channel ⓘ Orders ⓘ Tins (multipliers applied) ⓘ Revenue (AUD) ⓘ Avg order value ⓘ vs prev. month ⓘ Source ⓘ
    Rows are SKUs at the base level (No Pong SKU normalization rules apply — variants like -M, -SRT-x12, WC-, -CTN-48 roll up to base SKUs). Columns are the 5 channels for the selected month, with a total. Order counts and unit counts both shown.
    Units sold by SKU — May 2026
    SKU ⓘ Name ⓘ Woo ⓘ Amazon ⓘ Coles ⓘ Woolworths ⓘ Distributors ⓘ Total tins ⓘ Revenue (AUD) ⓘ
    Inventory sources. CIN7 is the operational ledger for AU stock. Base tins and cartons (×48) appear as separate rows since the team physically manages them (cartons come in via PO and get packed). Shelf-ready trays (×12) are NOT inventoried separately — any tray stock is rolled into the matching base SKU as ×12 tins. (Trays will reappear in the Packaging tab when we build it.) For Amazon FBA stock, the truth is Amazon SP-API itself — CIN7's AmazonFBA branch column is a mirror, so any drift between the two = CIN7 didn't sync. Woo doesn't track its own inventory (it pulls from CIN7). Discontinued lines (currently Spicy Chai) are kept as rows so we can run down stock, tagged and sunk to the bottom.
    Inventory by source
    Carton (×48) and tray (×12) SKUs shown as separate physical items. Flag if |FBA Amazon − FBA CIN7| > 10 units = CIN7 sync issue.
    SKU ⓘ Name Warehouse on hand ⓘ Warehouse available ⓘ FBA AU (Amazon · truth) ⓘ FBA AU (CIN7 mirror) ⓘ Warehouse on order ⓘ Status ⓘ
    Open POs from CIN7. Five flavours of PO show up here: Finished (manufactured tins from Health Focus etc.), Soap — new product family launching with AU-BD-NPS-100 (Specialty Soap Pty Ltd making the bars, No More Plastic doing the packaging), Tins (raw empty tins from Guanqiao Tinbox — T-AU-…), Packaging (boxes / outer cartons / envelopes — P-…), and Other.

    Live from CIN7 (v2.35). Phase 2b wires the /PurchaseOrders endpoint live, so created-date, expected-date, and status fields are now populated (the manual CSV export was missing those). Days-until-arrival and late-PO detection follow in the next polish pass — the data is here, just needs the column wiring on the table.
    Update ETDs from HFM schedule

    Upload the HFM production schedule as an Excel or CSV file (columns: Cust PO#, Description, Size, Quantity, Due Date, Fill Date). It reads each PO's fill date, matches it to CIN7 (PO-<number>), previews current → new ETD, and on confirm writes estimatedDeliveryDate back to CIN7. Date ranges and split fills use the earliest date.

    📄
    Drop the HFM Excel/CSV here, or browse
    .xlsx, .xls or .csv
    Open purchase orders
    Hover any row to see the full line-item detail with quantities. Type badge (Finished / Soap / Tins / Packaging / Other) shown next to the PO number.
    PO # ⓘ Supplier ⓘ Expected date ⓘ Days until arrival ⓘ Total units ⓘ SKUs ⓘ Status ⓘ
    Incoming stock by SKU
    Stock = base SKU SOH + carton tins (×48) · Run rate = trailing 3 complete months ÷ days · Days left = stock ÷ run rate · Next PO (days) = earliest open-PO expected_date − today · Discontinued SKUs excluded · Run rate covers all 5 channels (Coles + Woolies + Distributors + Woo + Amazon). Incoming qty = fully-filled tins only — raw tins (T-AU-...) and packaging (P-...) excluded. Size filter (All / 35 g / 85 g) persists across reloads; CSV export respects the active filter.
    SKU ⓘ Current stock ⓘ Run rate (units/day) ⓘ Days left ⓘ Next PO (days) ⓘ Incoming qty ⓘ PO ref ⓘ Risk status ⓘ
    Carton SKUs — wholesale / retail packing
    48 tins per carton (4 × 12 SRT trays) · Sold via Coles / Woolworths / Distributor channels · Equivalent tins = cartons in stock × 48
    SKU ⓘ Contents ⓘ Cartons in stock ⓘ Equivalent tins ⓘ
    Growth applied: % on monthly demand
    Order lead: mo Min per run: mo Monthly manufacturing budget: $ cash-flow guardrail — spend cards turn red over this Demand = trimmed average of live CIN7 tins
    Products to order now
    0
    PO due this month (or overdue)
    Manufacturing run-rate /mo
    $0
    steady replacement cost
    Suggested POs — total
    $0
    to top everything up now
    To order — your plan
    0
    $0 · enter quantities below
    35g tins — all formats combined
    Every 35g tin sold counts — single tins, wholesale cartons (Coles/Woolies), and SRTs — rolled into one demand rate per scent.
    Buffer = ⅓ month of demand · Cover includes stock + incoming POs · Order-by = cover − order lead (default 2 months, editable above) · each row shows its projected run-out month
    Products On hand Buffer
    ⅓ mo    		 Incoming Empty tins
    on hand + on order    		 Growth
    ± override    		 Demand
    /mo (plan)    		 3-mo actual
    vs base    		 Cover Suggested
    PO ($)    		 Order
    every    		 Order by To order Status
    85g tins
    85g is a different physical tin, forecast on its own.
    Products On hand Buffer
    ⅓ mo    		 Incoming Empty tins
    on hand + on order    		 Growth
    ± override    		 Demand
    /mo (plan)    		 3-mo actual
    vs base    		 Cover Suggested
    PO ($)    		 Order
    every    		 Order by To order Status
    Wholesale cartons on hand
    Coles / Woolworths retail cartons (48 tins each). These tins are already counted in the 35g demand above — shown here just for the packed-stock view. SRTs are handled in Packaging.
    SKU Contents Cartons on hand Equivalent tins
    Lead times: SRT trays mo Cartons mo Envelopes mo Blank tins mo
    Reorder floors: Blank tins Generic outers PCIs both are placeholders — set them to what the team actually uses
    To order in 30 days
    0
    listed in the action table below
    Tightest tray cover
    —
    months of tray stock
    Envelope cover
    —
    D2C orders/mo
    Order in the next 30 days
    Pulled from every table below — forecast lines whose order-by date falls within 30 days (or has passed), plus watch-list items already under their floor. Empty means nothing needs ordering this month.
    SKU Type Stock Incoming Demand/mo Cover Order by Status
    SRT trays
    Demand = trays shipped to Coles / Woolworths / distributors plus trays sold direct on Woo, plus 4 trays for every retailer carton. Counted as actual tray units off the order lines, not tins ÷ 12.
    Tray SKU Scent Stock Incoming Demand/mo Cover Order by Status
    Printed outer cartons
    One outer per 48-tin retailer carton sold. Wholesale cartons (WC-AU, ×54) are excluded — they ship direct from the manufacturer with no tray or outer.
    Carton SKU Scent Stock Incoming Demand/mo Cover Order by Status
    Envelopes & mailers
    One mailer per D2C Woo order (orders containing a tray or wholesale carton are excluded — they ship in their own packaging). The three are not interchangeable, so each is forecast on the orders it actually serves: P-BOX-12x85 for >6 × 85g, >12 × 35g, or a mix over 10 tins · P-ENV-A1 for any remaining order with 85g, or 7–12 × 35g · ENVELOPES for 6 or fewer 35g.
    SKU Item Stock Incoming Demand/mo Cover Order by Status
    Watch list — no forecast
    Generic outers, PCIs and blank engraving tins have no forecastable demand, so these are flagged on stock level alone rather than runway — at or below the floor means reorder. Blank tins are engraving stock (AU doesn't sticker blanks onto branded runs); PCIs are a marketing expense rather than a per-order consumable.
    SKU Item Stock Incoming Floor Status

    Last updated: — Auto-refresh: 60 s
    No Pong Natural Products · Australia Dashboard · Data from CIN7 · Metorik (Woo) · Amazon SP-API
    v2.2.89 · July 2026 ·

    v2.2.89 — 20 July 2026 (🔧 Stock diagnostic reported the pre-fix field)

    • The diagnostic was lying about itself. /api/au/stock/debug shipped in v2.2.85 with dashboardReads.field hardcoded to openPurchaseOrders. v2.2.86 changed the real code to read incoming, but the diagnostic kept reporting the old name — so it returned incoming: 0 for every SKU and read as though the bug were still live. It now mirrors fetchStockBySku exactly, fallback included.
    • Added a byBranch summary to the same response, matching the tab's Stock tooltip, so a “that stock isn't where you say it is” question can be settled from one call rather than by reading raw rows.
    • Correction, again, to the PCI figures. v2.2.85 and v2.2.87 both quoted PCI stock from a 10 May CSV export as if current: “31,072 on hand, 5,000 incoming, 20,000 of it at Health Focus”. Live CIN7 says 25,903, all at No Pong Warehouse NSW, nothing incoming, and zero at Health Focus — as Mel said. The tab has always read live and was never affected; only the changelog commentary was wrong. Anything sourced from AU Dashboard/raw/ is a May snapshot and should be treated as history, not fact.
    • Noted for later: CIN7 returns an EDI Test branch (id 23899). It holds zero across the catalogue today, so totals are unaffected, but it is not filtered — if it ever carries stock it will count toward on-hand. Left visible in the branch breakdown rather than silently excluded.

    v2.2.88 — 20 July 2026 (✏️ “Inserts” renamed to PCIs)

    • The reorder-floor control, its tooltip and the watch-list hint now say PCIs rather than “Inserts” — matching what the team actually calls them. The NA tab already used “Postcard Inserts (PCIs)”; AU now agrees.
    • Correction to v2.2.87. That entry cited PCI stock as “20,000 at Health Focus and 11,072 in our warehouse”. Those figures came from a 10 May CSV export, not live CIN7, and were quoted as though current — Mel flagged that there are no PCIs at Health Focus. The tab itself reads live and always has, so whatever it shows is current; only the example in that note was stale. If the live data does still attribute PCI stock to Health Focus, that's a CIN7 attribution issue at source rather than a dashboard one — /api/au/stock/debug?code=PCI shows the raw per-branch rows.

    v2.2.87 — 20 July 2026 (📍 Stock now shows which location it's in)

    • The totals were already right; the label wasn't. Mel asked whether stock at Brisbane Logistics and Health Focus was being counted. It always has been — fetchStockBySku sums stockOnHand across every branch row CIN7 returns, filtering nothing, and only also tracks Amazon FBA separately so a warehouse-only figure can be derived where that's useful. Verified with a multi-branch test: Main Branch + No Pong Warehouse + Health Focus + Brisbane + FBA all land in the total. But the Stock column tooltip read “our warehouse + Health Focus”, which named two locations and silently omitted Brisbane — a fair reason to doubt the number.
    • Stock cells now carry a per-location breakdown. Hover any Stock figure for the split by branch, with a sub-label showing either the single location or “N locations”. This matters more than it sounds: across the catalogue Health Focus holds 416,359 units and Brisbane 120,000, against 359,676 in our own warehouse — so for empty tins and packaging the majority is routinely not with us. A bare total reads as “on hand” when much of it is sitting at the filler.
    • Concrete examples now visible on the tab: PCI's 31,072 is 20,000 at Health Focus and 11,072 in our warehouse. T-AU-BLANK-BASE-35's 182 blank bases are entirely at My Skincare Manufacturer — none in our warehouse at all.
    • Backend carries a branches map per SKU (branch name → soh / incoming). Empty branch rows are skipped, since CIN7 returns a row per product × branch whether or not it holds anything.

    v2.2.86 — 20 July 2026 (🐛 Incoming stock was 0 on every AU SKU — wrong CIN7 field name)

    • The bug. fetchStockBySku requested a field list naming openPurchaseOrders and openSalesOrders. CIN7's /Stock response doesn't use those names — it returns incoming and openSales. So every row read back undefined, and incoming computed as 0 for every AU SKU. Nothing errored; the numbers were just quietly wrong. Confirmed from the raw response: P-ENV-A1 at the NSW warehouse carries "incoming": 24000 while the dashboard was reading openPurchaseOrders and getting nothing.
    • Three tabs were affected, not just Packaging. Inventory — the Incoming column has been 0 for everything since the Worker rewrite. Forecasting — the “+N on order” figure under empty tins has read 0 since v2.2.71 (2 July), so a suggested manufacturing run could look tin-short when empties were already on the way. Packaging — incoming blank across the board, which understated cover and pulled every order-by date earlier than reality. Spotted by Mel, who noticed a 24,000 PO on the POs tab that Packaging wasn't showing.
    • The fix. Read incoming, with openPurchaseOrders kept as a fallback so a future CIN7 rename can't silently zero it again. The fields restriction is dropped entirely — requesting no field list is exactly what the diagnostic does, and it returns every field CIN7 has (verified across 619 rows). Re-restricting would mean guessing which names CIN7 accepts as inputs versus what it emits as outputs, which is the assumption that caused this.
    • Verified against the real response, not a mock: the two raw P-ENV-A1 rows from /api/au/stock/debug now accumulate to soh 6,980 and incoming 24,000, with the Main Branch row correctly contributing nothing and FBA attribution unchanged.
    • Cache keys bumped to au:inventory:v3 and au:pos:v10 — warm entries have the zeros baked in and would keep serving them for up to 15 minutes.
    • Why the diagnostic stays. /api/au/stock/debug?code=<SKU> is what turned a guess into an answer in one round trip, and it's the right first stop for any future “CIN7 says X, dashboard says Y” discrepancy.

    v2.2.85 — 20 July 2026 (🔍 Stock-field diagnostic + PCI on the watch list)

    • Incoming stock reads 0 when CIN7 says otherwise, and this adds the tool to find out why. P-ENV-A1 shows no incoming on the tab while CIN7's UI shows a 24,000 PO against it. We read incoming from openPurchaseOrders on /Stock, which is what CIN7's documented field list gives — but the UI column is “Incoming Stock” and a CSV export of the same data calls it IncomingStock, and those may not be the same number. New GET /api/au/stock/debug?code=P-ENV-A1 fetches /Stock with no field restriction and dumps the raw rows, so the true field names and values are visible rather than inferred. It reports what the dashboard currently computes alongside every non-zero numeric field on the row — if the real incoming figure lives under another name, it'll be in that list.
    • Deliberately diagnostic-only, no speculative fix. Adding a guessed field name to the main fields request risks CIN7 rejecting the whole call and taking the inventory endpoint down with it. This bug came from an assumption about a field; the fix shouldn't be another one. Incoming is understated on every packaging row until this is resolved, which means cover is understated and order-by dates are earlier than reality.
    • PCI added to the watch list. Postcard inserts (31,072 on hand, 5,000 incoming) now appear alongside generic outers and blank tins, with their own editable floor. No forecast attached — they're a marketing expense rather than a per-order consumable. Note the bare PCI code was the same trap as ENVELOPES: no prefix, so prefix-based matching missed it.
    • Third reorder floor added for inserts, defaulting to 5,000. Like the other two, a placeholder — set it to whatever the team works to.

    v2.2.84 — 20 July 2026 (✉️ Mailer rules complete — 7–12 × 35g assigned)

    • The unassigned band is closed. Orders of 7–12 × 35g with no 85g use P-ENV-A1 (Mel, 20 Jul), so they now fold into that SKU's rate instead of sitting in a reported gap. P-ENV-A1's forecast rises accordingly — it was previously under-counted by roughly the volume of that band.
    • Every D2C order now maps to exactly one mailer. Verified exhaustively across 960 order shapes (0–30 tins of each size): no shape falls in two buckets, none falls in none. All of Mel's stated boundaries check out — 6 × 35g → ENVELOPES, 7 × 35g → P-ENV-A1, 13 × 35g → P-BOX, 6 × 85g → P-ENV-A1, 7 × 85g → P-BOX, and a 6+5 mix → P-BOX on the over-10 rule.
    • The gap note becomes a tripwire. The query keeps counting orders that match no rule, but that count should now always be zero. It only surfaces on the tab if it isn't — so a future rule change that leaves a hole is loud, rather than quietly under-forecasting whichever mailer those orders really use.

    v2.2.83 — 20 July 2026 (📦 Packaging — action table, per-mailer demand, editable floors)

    • New “Order in the next 30 days” table at the top. Pulls from every table below — forecast lines whose order-by date falls inside 30 days or has passed, plus watch-list items at or under their floor — sorted most urgent first. This is the tab’s answer to “what do I actually need to do this month”. The Dead packaging stock card is gone; discontinued lines still render greyed in their own tables, they just don’t warrant a headline number.
    • The three mailer SKUs are forecast separately, because they are not interchangeable. Which one an order consumes depends on its composition (Mel, 20 Jul): P-BOX-12x85 for more than 6 × 85g, more than 12 × 35g, or a mix over 10 tins; P-ENV-A1 for any remaining order containing 85g; ENVELOPES for 6 or fewer 35g. The demand query now counts 35g and 85g tins per order and buckets each order to the mailer it actually uses, rather than treating envelopes as one pooled line. The KPI card reports the tightest mailer, since an average would hide a shortage in one size.
    • ENVELOPES was missing entirely. The bare code carries no P- prefix, so the prefix-based classifier dropped it silently — understating the envelope position by 22,680 units. Mailer SKUs now match an explicit list rather than a prefix, so a similarly odd code can’t slip through unnoticed again. Found by Mel, who knew there were two envelope SKUs.
    • Reorder floors are editable. The 300 used for generic outers was a placeholder I picked with no basis; it now sits in the controls next to the blank-tin floor, both labelled as placeholders. Set them to whatever the team actually works to.
    • At the floor now counts as low. Was strictly-below, so a line sitting exactly on its reorder point read as OK — P-AU-BLANK-3CTN-OUTERS at 300/300 didn’t appear in the action table when it should have.
    • Unassigned-mailer volume is surfaced. Orders of 7–12 × 35g with no 85g fall between the stated rules — ENVELOPES covers 6 or fewer, P-BOX starts above 12. Those orders are counted into their own bucket and reported under the tables rather than guessed into a SKU, so whichever mailer they use is visibly under-forecast until the rule is confirmed.
    • Correction to v2.2.82: that entry said PCIs aren’t stock-tracked in CIN7. They are — PCI (“Post Card Insert”) holds 31,072 on hand with 5,000 incoming. Still excluded from the tab pending a decision on whether to forecast them, but the stated reason was wrong.

    v2.2.82 — 20 July 2026 (📦 Packaging tab — built)

    • The Packaging tab is live — SRT trays, printed outer cartons, envelopes, and a watch list for generic outers and blank engraving tins. It replaces the “to design” placeholder, which had assumed we might need a separate spreadsheet upload like Logiwa on the CA side. We don’t: CIN7 already carries every packaging SKU with real stock and incoming figures. The rows were being fetched on every sync and then discarded — buildStockBySku() applies no category filter, but buildInventory() narrows the payload to Finished Products / Bicarb Based before the front end sees it. New buildAuPackagingBySku() reads the unfiltered map, so the whole tab costs zero extra API calls.
    • Not a port of the NA tab. Two of NA’s defining assumptions don’t hold here. NA pools blank 85g tins and stickers them at Petra run time — all of buildPackagingRows’ PASS 2 / PASS 4 allocation machinery exists to model that. AU doesn’t sticker at all: blanks are engraving stock, so they’re a standalone watch-list line with a reorder floor and none of that logic ports. NA also derives paper demand from a single driver (“1 per Woo order”); AU needs two, below.
    • Channel-split demand. Envelopes count D2C Woo orders excluding any order containing a tray or wholesale carton — those ship in their own packaging, so NA’s flat per-order rule would over-order AU envelopes. Trays come from supermarket + distributor volume plus direct Woo tray sales, plus 4 trays per retailer carton. All counted as real pack-format units off the order lines (order_items.sku for Woo, cin7_sales_order_items.code for CIN7), not tins ÷ 12 — skuSales can’t serve this because it rolls every line up to base SKU with the multiplier already applied.
    • Lead times: entered drives, observed nudges. Each family has an editable lead-time field seeded from the lead-time doc. Beside it sits the median createdDate → fullyReceivedDate from CIN7’s own PO history, which turns amber when the two diverge by more than about a week. It never feeds a calculation — a supplier changing pace shows up as a prompt, not as silently moved order dates. First finding: TC Printing’s observed median is 27 days across 5 received POs (range 24–40, the 40 being a Christmas run), against the 5 weeks in the doc. Median, not mean, precisely so one Christmas run can’t drag it.
    • Dead-stock detection. A derived link only counts if that finished SKU still exists in CIN7. Retired lines keep their packaging on the shelf long after delisting — Spicy Chai is the live case: P-OC-AU-CTN-SC-BCF-48 and -SC-NPO-48 point at carton SKUs that are gone. Those rows render greyed as Discontinued rather than as demand-less rows that read like a supply problem.
    • Deliberate exclusions. Wholesale cartons (WC-AU-, ×54) ship direct from the manufacturer with no tray or outer, so they consume nothing on this tab — though they still suppress an envelope on the Woo side. Lids (T-L-AU-) are discontinued. PCIs are a marketing expense and aren’t stock-tracked in CIN7, so there’s no SOH to report. Mixed mega-trays hold 6 trays of assorted scents, so their tray demand can’t be attributed to any single SKU — surfaced in a note under the tables rather than silently dropped.
