    • Cache keys bumped — au:inventory:v2 and au:pos:v9, so a warm entry can’t serve a payload missing packaging or observedLeadTimes for up to 15 minutes after deploy.
    • Known gap: classifyPoLineCode()’s T- catch-all still counts lid SKUs as raw tins on the PO tab. Harmless while that stock runs out, but it does inflate raw-tin figures today.

    v2.2.81 — 18 July 2026 (🚚 Logistics — Amazon orders no longer show as distributors)

    • The Logistics “Other distributors” panel was listing individual Amazon retail orders (e.g. “Amazon Seller No Pong Natural Products”, order refs like 249-8715696-3264625, 1 unit each). Root cause: the distributor classifier reuses attributeCin7Order(), which since v2.2.47/v2.2.52 returns 'amz' for Amazon orders (added to power the Monthly SKU Sales tab). The Logistics classifier only special-cased Coles and Woolies, then treated everything else as a distributor — so 'amz' orders fell straight through into the “Other distributors” column. The classifier docstring always said Amazon mirrors should be excluded; the code just no longer matched it. Fix: drop 'amz' orders explicitly, and only bucket genuine 'dist' orders (fail-closed on any future channel). Amazon fulfilment still flows through the ShipStation open-queue KPIs above, where it belongs.

    v2.2.80 — 12 July 2026 (📅 Forecasting CSV — Run-out date added)

    • The Forecasting CSV export now includes the projected Run out date (e.g. “5 Sep 2026”) — the same “runs out ~” date shown under the Order by cell on screen. New Run out (date) column sits right after Order by (date). It’s today + Cover months, so it nets off the ⅓-month safety buffer and counts incoming POs, matching the dashboard exactly.

    v2.2.79 — 9 July 2026 (📅 Forecasting CSV — Order-by date added)

    • The Forecasting CSV export now includes the actual Order by date (e.g. “5 Aug 2026”, or “… (overdue)”), not just the months-from-now number. New Order by (date) column sits next to Order by (mo) and matches the date shown on screen.

    v2.2.78 — 9 July 2026 (⏰ Incoming by SKU — overdue POs now surface)

    • The “Next PO (days)” column now shows an overdue PO instead of hiding it behind a later one. The rule used to be “a future PO always wins,” so a SKU with an overdue PO and a far-off future PO (e.g. AU-EF-BCF-35) only showed the future one — the overdue supply was invisible. Now it shows the earliest-dated open PO for the SKU, whether overdue (negative days, red) or upcoming, so late arrivals are visible where they matter most. (Cache key au:pos bumped v7→v8.)

    v2.2.77 — 9 July 2026 (🔢 Purchase Orders — finished POs no longer double-count empty tins)

    • Total units on a Finished PO now counts only the finished product, not the empty tins. HFM finished POs list the finished good and its empty-tin component (e.g. AU-OG-BCF-35 + T-AU-OG-BCF-35) — the same physical run in two forms — so the old “sum every line” total double-counted (e.g. showed 30,000 for a 15,000-unit run). Total units now reflects the PO’s primary product by type. Genuine raw-tin and packaging POs still count their own units. (Cache key au:pos bumped v6→v7.)

    v2.2.76 — 9 July 2026 (🩹 fix: ETD "Write" button was invisible)

    • The “Write selected ETDs to CIN7” button used a --pink CSS variable this dashboard doesn’t define, so it rendered white-on-white (invisible). Switched to --accent so the button shows.

    v2.2.75 — 9 July 2026 (📄 Purchase Orders — update ETDs from the HFM schedule)

    • New on the Purchase Orders tab: “Update ETDs from HFM schedule”. Upload the HFM (Health Focus Manufacturers) production schedule as an Excel or CSV file; the dashboard reads each row, matches the Cust PO# to its CIN7 purchase order (PO-<number>), and shows a preview of the current → new ETD. Tick the rows you want and it writes estimatedDeliveryDate straight back to CIN7 (PUT /PurchaseOrders).
    • Reads the spreadsheet directly in the browser (SheetJS) — no AI involved, nothing to configure. Auto-detects the Cust PO# and Fill Date columns from the header row.
    • Earliest-date rule for ranges / split fills — 17 & 24 Jul → 17 Jul, 30-31 Jul → 30 Jul; the year is inferred. Rows where the fill date lands after the due date are flagged (⚑) for a second look, and PO numbers not found in CIN7 are listed rather than silently skipped.
    • New endpoints POST /api/au/etd/scan (parse + preview, no writes) and POST /api/au/etd/apply (gated write, logged to KV au:etd:audit:*). Nothing is written until you confirm.

    v2.2.73 — 2 July 2026 (🥫 AU Forecasting — empty tins ON ORDER now count toward the fill ceiling)

    • Tins on order are now kept in mind for upcoming POs. The fill ceiling for a run is now empties on hand + empties already on order (not just on hand). Two states now: a run is only flagged ⚠ short if it can’t be filled even counting the tins on order (that’s when you genuinely need to order more empties); if on-hand alone won’t fill it but tins already on order will, it shows a softer blue ℹ “tins on order cover this” note instead — no new tin order needed, just mind the ~4-month lead vs when you fill. The Empty-tins column header now reads “on hand + on order”, and the shortage banner only lists products short after counting inbound tins.

    v2.2.72 — 2 July 2026 (🧹 AU Forecasting — 85g parity, top-card cleanup, tin-info fixes)

    • 85g section brought to full parity with 35g. The 85g table shares the exact same engine as 35g (one rowHtml / auSuggestPO) — but its column headers had lost all their hover explanations, so it looked like it wasn’t using the new logic. Restored every tooltip (Empty tins, the re-thought Suggested-PO sizing for the 85g 5k/10k/15k runs, etc.). No behaviour change — 85g always ran the same maths.
    • Top cards cleaned up. “Scents to order now” → “Products to order now”, and it now counts the products whose PO must actually be placed this month (order-by date overdue or in the current calendar month) instead of a vague “cover ≤ lead” number. Removed the two meaningless headline cards (“Tins needed — next 12 mo” and “POs to place — next 12 mo”) and the now-unused Plan-horizon control. “Scent” → “product” wording throughout the cards + banners.
    • Suggested-PO hover no longer mentions Health Focus. Dropped the “no Health Focus PO cost on file” note (cost is moving to CIN7 Cost AUD — next up).

    v2.2.71 — 2 July 2026 (🥫 AU Forecasting — empty-tin fill ceiling + re-thought Suggested PO)

    • Empty tins now feed the forecast. New Empty tins column shows the unfilled tins we hold for each scent (CIN7 T-AU-… stock, pulled live), plus any empties on order. It’s the ceiling on how many finished tins a run can fill right now — you can’t manufacture more than you have empties for. When a suggested (or entered) run exceeds the empties on hand, the row goes amber with a “⚠ short N tins” flag and a banner lists the affected scents. Warn-only: the finished PO stays as-is, but you’re told to order the empty tins first (~4-month lead) — full tin ordering will live in the AU Packaging forecast.
    • Suggested PO re-thought as a standard run size. It used to be a “top up to ~8 months, capped at one batch” number that jittered with stock and silently under-sized the fast movers. It’s now the smallest run that lasts at least the “Min per run” months (new control, default 2), capped at the largest batch. Slow scents land on the smallest run (which naturally lasts many months); fast/carton lines (OG) hit the cap and are simply reordered more often. Cadence (“lasts ~X mo”) sits beside it, and when to order stays on the Order-by date.
    • CSV export gains Empty tins, Empty tins on order, and Tin shortfall columns.
    • Backend: incomingBySku rows now carry empty_tins_soh / empty_tins_incoming (POs cache bumped au:pos:v5→v6).
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.70 — 2 July 2026 (🗓️ AU Forecasting — order-date emphasis + fixed red/amber colours)

    • Order-by dates now emphasise only what’s actionable this cycle. Ordering is done monthly, so only overdue dates (red bold) and dates in the current calendar month (amber bold) stand out; any future-month date is now plain. Previously everything within ~2 months was bold, which over-emphasised dates a later monthly run will catch.
    • Fixed the red/amber colour coding. The forecast referenced CSS variables that don’t exist in this dashboard (--danger, --amber, --pink-bg), so Cover, Order-by and the spend cards were silently rendering near-black instead of red/amber, and the “To order” column lost its pink tint. Now uses the real tokens (--red-text, --amber-text, --woo-bg).
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.69 — 2 July 2026 (🔍 AU Forecasting — baseline behind the 3-mo %, + easing-off alert)

    • The “3-mo actual” % now shows what it’s measured against. The ▲/▼ % compares the recent 3-month rate to the smoothed baseline — but for a climbing product the recent rate equals the plan Demand, so the two columns showed the same number with a % that looked like it compared a value to itself. The cell now shows “vs base X · ▲/▼ %” beneath the 3-mo figure, so the % always has a visible reference. (Numbers were correct all along; this is a display fix.)
    • Easing-off alert (decline flag). Plan demand deliberately keeps ordering to the higher of baseline vs recent rate so we never run out — but that can over-manufacture a declining product. A new blue banner now lists products whose recent rate is ≥25% below baseline, prompting the forecaster to trim those next runs (via the per-product growth override) to protect cash flow. Mirrors the ramp-up alert, the other direction.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.68 — 2 July 2026 (🔁 AU Forecasting — refresh on-hand breakdown when live POs land)

    • The Forecasting tab now re-renders when the live PO fetch completes, so the On-hand warehouse / FBA / carton split (from v2.2.67) shows up reliably even if you’re already sitting on the tab — no need to switch away and back.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.67 — 2 July 2026 (📦 AU Forecasting — On-hand now includes Amazon FBA + shows its make-up)

    • On-hand now includes Amazon FBA stock. It was warehouse-only, which understated on-hand — FBA tins are sellable, just held at Amazon’s warehouse. Backend current_stock for base SKUs now = warehouse singles + FBA (cartons stay warehouse-only, since Amazon doesn’t hold cartons). KV cache bumped au:pos:v4→v5.
    • On-hand shows its breakdown. Each value now shows a “X whse · Y FBA · Z ctn” split beneath it, with a full hover — so it reconciles against CIN7 (the warehouse figure matches CIN7’s base SKU; the rest is FBA + tins packed in cartons/SRTs). Dropped the “loose” jargon.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.66 — 2 July 2026 (🎯 AU Forecasting — demand now plans for the higher recent rate)

    • Cover no longer overstates when a product is ramping. Planning demand is now the higher of the smoothed baseline and the recent 3-month run-rate (median-of-3, so a single load-in still can’t inflate it). Cover, Buffer, Order-by and Suggested-PO all use it. Fixes the Midnight-Jazz case (baseline 8,358 said 2.4 mo cover while the real 3-month rate of ~11,800 meant <2 mo) — the trap that caught Flower Power / Fragrance Free out.
    • New “3-mo actual vs base” column beside Demand shows the recent run-rate and its ▲/▼ % change vs baseline — the pluses and minuses, so the variance is visible at a glance (not just the ramp-up flag).
    • The ramp-up badge moved into that column (informational now that the forecast auto-uses the higher rate); the alert banner still lists surging products.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.65 — 2 July 2026 (📈 AU Forecasting — ramp-up / surge flag)

    • Surge detection. When a product’s recent run-rate (median of the last 3 months) is ≥25% above its smoothed baseline — i.e. it’s ramping up the way Flower Power did in Coles/Woolies — a ▲ +X% badge appears under its Demand, and a ramp-up alert banner lists the affected products above the table. Guards against false flags on tiny/low-volume SKUs.
    • One click to act. Because the trimmed baseline deliberately smooths spikes, it can hide real growth. Clicking the ▲ badge folds that surge % into the product’s growth override, so cover shrinks and the order-by date pulls in — prompting you to secure tins early. The badge turns to a green ✓ once the growth covers it.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.64 — 2 July 2026 (📅 AU Forecasting — Order-by shows a specific date)

    • Order-by is now a specific calendar date (e.g. “18 Jul 2026”) — the latest day to place the PO = projected run-out date − the order lead — like the North America dashboard, with the projected run-out date beneath.
    • Less trigger-happy urgency. The date (and Status) only turns red “Order now” once that order-by date has actually passed; amber “Order soon” within ~2 months; green otherwise. No more “Order now” for a tin that doesn’t run out for months.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.63 — 2 July 2026 (📐 AU Forecasting — smaller product names, centred cells)

    • Product name shrunk (12 → 10px, tighter line-height) under the SKU so rows aren’t so tall — the SKU still leads.
    • All cells centred (headers, values, and the growth / to-order inputs) for even spacing; the Products column stays left-aligned.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.62 — 2 July 2026 (🗓️ AU Forecasting — clearer Order-by + narrower Products column)

    • Order-by now reads as urgency, not a confusing current month. Status + colours key off when you must place the PO (cover − lead): under 1 month → red “Order now” and the cell shows “This month” (or “Now” if overdue) instead of printing the current month name; 1–3 months → amber with the deadline month; beyond that → green. Future deadlines floor to the safe (earlier) month.
    • Products column narrowed + renamed. The SKU/“Scent” column is now capped in width (was expanding to fit long names) and relabelled Products.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.61 — 2 July 2026 (👓 AU Forecasting — readability pass)

    • Lighter row tints (tin-colour wash dialled from 14% → 8%) so text reads clearly on top.
    • Bigger, clearer type. Table base up to 14px; the faint sub-lines (Order-by “runs out”, Suggested-PO cost, product name) enlarged and darkened from the near-invisible light grey to the readable secondary tone.
    • Narrower SKU column (min-width 160 → 112px).
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.60 — 2 July 2026 (🎨 AU Forecasting — rows tinted by tin colour)

    • Each row is tinted by its product’s canonical tin colour (No Pong Brand Kit 2026 §4) — Original pink, Cool Lavender dusty lavender, Luscious Lime teal, Enchanted Forest navy, the black-tin editions by their accent-stripe colour, etc. — as a light wash, with a matching colour dot by each SKU, for product-level line-by-line differentiation. Frozen SKU column picks up the tint so it stays clean during horizontal scroll; the editable To-order column keeps its own pink.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.59 — 1 July 2026 (🚫 AU Forecasting — exclude Secret Scenta + Spicy Chai)

    • Secret Scenta (AU-SS…) hidden from the forecast — it’s an annual limited edition, not a steady line to reorder against.
    • Spicy Chai (AU-SC…) hidden — discontinued for now. If it comes back, remove the SC test in auExcludedFromForecast to re-enable.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.58 — 1 July 2026 (⏱️ AU Forecasting — fixed the confusing “Order now” logic)

    • Lead time 4 → 2 months, and now editable. The old 4-month figure was the empty-tin (China) lead; manufacturing POs to Health Focus need ~2 months’ notice. New Order lead field in the controls (default 2 mo) drives every order-by / status calc, so the basis is explicit and adjustable.
    • Status, Cover and Order-by now use ONE number. The Status badge previously judged stock-on-hand only (ignoring incoming POs), so a scent with 3 months’ cover could still say “Order now”. All three now read the same cover-incl-incoming figure vs the lead: red Order now when cover ≤ lead, amber Order soon when ≤ lead + 2 months, else green.
    • Specific dates, not a bare “Order now”. Order-by shows the month to place the PO (rounded to the month, since you order at the start of a month) and, beneath it, the projected run-out month. “Now” only appears when you’re genuinely inside the lead window, with a tooltip explaining why.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.57 — 1 July 2026 (💲 AU Forecasting — manufacturing cost keyed to Health Focus POs)

    • Manufacturing cost now comes from Health Focus (manufacturer) POs. auUnitCosts keys each tin's cost to the per-line price on Health Focus purchase orders — that IS the cost to manufacture each tin — falling back to any finished-goods PO line only if a SKU has no Health Focus PO on file. Feeds the run-rate / suggested-PO / to-order spend cards and the $300k budget guardrail.
    • NA dashboard untouched. NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.56 — 1 July 2026 (🧮 AU Forecasting — PO cadence, run sizes & cash-flow guardrail)

    • Rows now lead with the tin number (SKU). Product names were being pulled from the sales feed, whose name is the first line that hit each base SKU — often a carton/SRT description (Original showed as a “shelf-ready tray”). Names now come from the inventory feed only, and each row leads with the SKU with a clean name beneath.
    • PO run sizes. Suggested runs snap to the real manufacturing sequence — 35g: 15k / 30k / 45k (45k Original only), 85g: 5k / 10k (15k available). New Suggested PO column (with its $ cost) and Order every ~X mo cadence column so you can see how often each tin needs a PO.
    • 6–12 month horizon. A plan-horizon toggle plus summary cards for total tins needed and number of POs to place over the next 6 or 12 months — the rough forward total.
    • Cash-flow guardrail. Per-tin manufacturing cost is read from AU finished-goods POs. New cards for monthly manufacturing run-rate, the total cost to top everything up now, and your live “to order” spend — all compared to an editable $300k/month budget (cards turn red over target) so you can pace orders across months.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.55 — 1 July 2026 (🔮 AU Forecasting tab — concise all-tins-together buying tool)

    • New AU Forecasting tab (was a placeholder). A concise buying tool modelled on the CA one but trimmed from 25 columns to a lean set: Scent, On hand, Buffer, Incoming, Growth± override, Demand/mo, Cover, Order by, To order, Status. 35g and 85g tins forecast in separate sections.
    • All 35g tins together. Demand per scent is the live CIN7 total_tins, which already folds every format into base tins server-side (normalizeAuSku: single tins ×1, retail cartons ×48, wholesale ×54, SRTs ×12). No per-format rows — one combined “tins needed” number per scent, exactly how AU is ordered.
    • Load-in smoothing. Rate = trimmed (exclude min & max) average of the recent live months, so a one-off Coles/Woolies stocking month (e.g. Apr's ~45k) doesn’t inflate the ongoing run rate. Buffer = ⅓ month of demand; cover includes stock + incoming POs; order-by assumes a 4-month tin lead time.
    • Smarter growth. A global growth % (positive or negative) with a Suggest from trend button (recent 3-month median momentum vs the trimmed baseline — median keeps a load-in spike from hijacking the suggestion), plus per-scent growth overrides. Same-month-last-year reference switches on automatically once 12+ months of live CIN7 history accumulate.
    • Wholesale cartons on hand shown as a separate informational table (tins already counted in the 35g totals). SRT tins are counted in demand but not forecast as their own line — that’s a Packaging-tab concern.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.54 — 30 June 2026 (🎉 Cutover flipped to May — Amazon AU live from CIN7, monthly CSV import retired)

    • AU_AMAZON_CIN7_CUTOVER_MONTH '2026-06' → '2026-05'. May 2026 onward now reads the Amazon column from CIN7's cin7_sales_orders (channel_attr='amz') instead of the CSV-imported amazon_orders table. This completes the work the whole v2.2.50–53 chain set up: a reliable, complete CIN7 Amazon feed. No more monthly Amazon CSV imports for AU. April and earlier still read the CSV (CIN7's pre-May Amazon data is incomplete), so the hybrid is unchanged below the cutover.
    • Reconciliation that justified the flip: after the cluster-aware re-walk (v2.2.50), the order-ID attribution fix (v2.2.52, +895 redacted Amazon orders) and the CreatedDate early-May backfill (v2.2.53), D1 May Amazon = 1,458 orders / 1,886 tins vs CIN7's own pivot 1,463 / 1,900 — a 99.7% / 99.3% match (the small residual is VOID/refund/snapshot noise). June = 1,379 vs 1,326. Both well inside the ±tolerance the April CSV cross-check (0.7%) established.
    • KV cache key bumped v8 → v9 (kvSalesKey) so the changed May payload refreshes immediately rather than serving a stale pre-cutover value for up to the 15-min TTL.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.53 — 30 June 2026 (🩹 CreatedDate safety-net trigger — fill under-synced day ranges)

    • New GET /api/admin/cin7-safetynet?since=YYYY-MM-DD&until=YYYY-MM-DD — clickable (SSO'd) trigger for the CreatedDate safety-net backfill, now with explicit date-range bounds. Re-fetches SalesOrders by CreatedDate (sidestepping the ModifiedDate noise walls) to fill historical holes where whole days were never synced. Used to backfill early May (1–8), which had only ~10–20 orders/day in D1 vs ~300/day mid-month — a genuine under-sync, not attribution. runCin7SafetyNetBackfill gained since/until params so a sweep can be bound to one gap and finish in a single call. Auth: Cloudflare Access SSO.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.52 — 30 June 2026 (🎯 Amazon attribution by order-ID reference — catches CIN7-redacted Amazon sales)

    • Real attribution miss found + fixed. After the v2.2.50 cluster-aware re-walk filled June fully (D1 1,361 vs CIN7's 1,326 — matches), May stayed ~600 short (D1 856 vs CIN7 1,463). Read-only diagnosis showed the late-May days are fully synced (~300 orders/day) but Amazon orders were landing with company [Redacted] (CIN7 redacts the customer on some Amazon orders) and being dropped to NULL by the company rule. Proof: 273 May NULL-tagged orders carry Amazon order-ID references (NNN-NNNNNNN-NNNNNNN) + AU SKUs. June had zero such drops.
    • Fix (src/cin7.js, attributeCin7Order): attribute by the Amazon order-ID reference (/^\d{3}-\d{7}-\d{7}$/) as the primary Amazon signal, ahead of the company rules, so a redacted Amazon sale is caught regardless of customer label. Cross-checked against all June Amazon-ref orders — zero false positives (no Coles/Woolies/distributor order uses this reference shape). The company.startsWith('amazon seller') rule stays as a fallback.
    • Backfill: existing NULL orders with Amazon-format references re-tagged to amz (one-time idempotent UPDATE).
    • Still open (not this release): a residual early-May gap (~334) where Amazon orders aren't in D1 at all (early-May under-sync) — to be resolved by a CreatedDate re-sync before the cutover is flipped to '2026-05'. AU_AMAZON_CIN7_CUTOVER_MONTH stays '2026-06' for now.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.51 — 30 June 2026 (⚡ One-click CIN7 catch-up accelerator)

    • New GET /api/admin/cin7-catchup — a browser-clickable accelerator over the 15-min cron for the CIN7 sales-orders sync. Opens from an SSO'd tab (no POST tooling), runs the same cluster-aware chunk in a loop advancing the shared watermark, and returns a plain-English message + nextStep (“click again” vs “caught up”) so a recovery doesn't mean waiting hours of cron ticks. Used to backfill the 24–31 May + 7–29 June holes after the 6 June stall. Credit notes excluded (low volume, not behind). Auth: Cloudflare Access SSO, same as every /api/admin/* route.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.50 — 29 June 2026 (🔁 Cluster-aware CIN7 cron + weekly safety-net — stalls can't freeze the sync anymore)

    • Root cause confirmed (and it was NOT an attribution bug). The AU Amazon undercount was diagnosed read-only against D1: every Amazon order CIN7 sends arrives as customer "Amazon Sales" / company "amazon seller no pong natural products" and is already tagged channel_attr='amz' correctly — there is no pile of mis-attributed Amazon sales hiding in the NULL bucket, and CIN7's /SalesOrders API does not even return a branch field, so a branch-based attribution rebuild was unbuildable and unnecessary. The undercount is purely sync coverage: the cron had wedged again, this time on a 275-row cluster all stamped 2026-06-06T14:00:02Z (page size is 250, so a single page can never clear it), leaving 7–29 June unsynced; plus the old 23 May stall left 24–31 May thin.
    • Cluster-aware pagination (the durable fix). runCin7SalesOrdersChunk + runCin7CreditNotesChunk in src/cin7-sync.js now track (watermark, clusterPage) — re-purposing the dormant sync_state.watermark_id column. When a full page sits entirely on one ModifiedDate, the watermark holds and the page advances, so a same-timestamp cluster of any size is walked in ceil(size/250) ticks instead of looping forever. New pure helper decideNextCursor isolates the advance logic. This is the cron rebuild queued in v2.2.49 — CIN7 still coerces > to >= and drops Id filters, so the page param is the only lever, and this uses it.
    • Weekly safety-net backfill (the insurance). New runCin7SafetyNetBackfill + a second cron trigger (0 2 * * 0, Sunday 02:00 UTC) re-fetches the last 60 days of SalesOrders ordered by CreatedDate (which sidesteps ModifiedDate clusters entirely) and INSERT OR REPLACEs them — idempotent, bounded, stateless. Catches anything the incremental path structurally missed, including the 24–31 May hole left by the 2 June manual watermark jump. The per-order upsert was extracted to a shared buildSalesOrderStatements so incremental + safety-net can never drift on attribution.
    • Immediate recovery. The stuck 6 June watermark was advanced one second past the cluster (commands/unstick-cin7-sales-watermark.command, updated for this recurrence) so the now cluster-aware cron resumes forward through June.
    • No dashboard read-path or cutover change in this release — AU_AMAZON_CIN7_CUTOVER_MONTH stays '2026-06' until D1 May is verified complete against CIN7's own May total (1,463 Amazon orders), at which point a follow-up flips it back to '2026-05' to retire the monthly CSV import.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.49 — 2 June 2026 (📦 Cutover shifted to June after CIN7 cron stalled mid-May)

    • Cutover constant moved May → June. Same-day follow-up to v2.2.48. The May cutover assumed CIN7's `cin7_sales_orders` table had complete May Amazon data, but a CIN7 cron stall (watermark stuck at 2026-05-23T14:00:0XZ on a bulk-modification cluster of ~1,000 records-per-second) left May 24-31 Amazon orders unreached, so CIN7 May numbers under-counted (e.g. AU-OG-NPO-35 read 269 tins vs amazon_orders' CSV-imported 309). Moving AU_AMAZON_CIN7_CUTOVER_MONTH from '2026-05' to '2026-06' keeps May on the accurate CSV source and lets June onwards live on CIN7.
    • Watermark jump command: commands/jump-cin7-watermark-to-june.command pushes sync_state.watermark for cin7_sales_orders straight to 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z, skipping the stuck May 23 cluster entirely. Two cron ticks later (~30 min) June's 40+ Amazon Seller orders land in D1 with channel_attr='amz' (set by the v2.2.48 attributeCin7Order change). May orders that the cron missed (May 24-31) are intentionally skipped — they're already on the amazon_orders CSV side, so no data loss.
    • Long-term cron fix queued for next session. The 1-second-per-tick crawl on the May 23 cluster proved the current cron design can't survive CIN7's bulk-modification events at scale. Plan: cluster-aware pagination (track (watermark, cluster_page) instead of just watermark; paginate through same-timestamp clusters via CIN7's page param) plus a weekly safety-net backfill that re-fetches the last 60 days by CreatedDate ASC. Notes in Melanie's Context/DECISIONS.md 2026-06-02.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.48 — 2 June 2026 (📦 Amazon AU live from CIN7 + honest fallback chip)

    • Amazon AU column now live from CIN7 for May 2026 onwards. Per Melanie 2026-06-02, the Monthly SKU table's Amazon column was empty for June because the old data path read from amazon_orders (CSV-imported from Amazon Seller Central) and no June CSV had been imported yet — even though the same orders were already syncing into cin7_sales_orders via the 15-min CIN7 cron. Per a side-by-side comparison (May: CIN7 695 orders vs CSV 730 = ~95% match), CIN7 is now the source of truth from AU_AMAZON_CIN7_CUTOVER_MONTH = '2026-05' onwards. Pre-cutover months (March, April, earlier) still read from amazon_orders because CIN7's sync only started capturing Amazon Seller orders mid-April (0 in March, 14 in April) and historical numbers would crater if we cut over fully without a backfill.
    • Backend (src/cin7.js): attributeCin7Order now returns 'amz' for company names starting with "amazon seller" instead of dropping them to null; "amazon fba" still returns null (replenishment, not a sale). buildAuSalesPayloadFromD1 picks the channel filter (('col','woo2','dist') vs ('col','woo2','dist','amz')) based on the month-vs-cutover check, and only calls getAuAmazonSalesFromD1 on the pre-cutover branch to avoid double-counting. Per-SKU rollup gains amz + amz_revenue SUM-CASE pivots. counts.ordersConsidered includes Amazon orders post-cutover. source field now disambiguates: 'cin7-d1+amz-from-cin7' vs 'cin7-d1+amz-from-csv'.
    • KV cache key bumped v7 → v8 (kvSalesKey) so any pre-v2.2.48 cached month payloads with the old shape get ignored and the new aggregation re-runs.
    • Backfill required for legacy rows. Existing Amazon Seller rows in cin7_sales_orders still have channel_attr = NULL from the old attribution code. New double-clickable commands/backfill-amazon-channel-attr.command runs the one-time UPDATE. Safe to re-run (no-op once tagged). Run this once after the deploy.
    • Frontend: honest fallback chip + one-shot auto-retry (public/index.html). The old "CIN7 fallback (static) — Showing static April snapshot" copy was misleading whenever a previous fetch had successfully populated the month bucket — the dashboard kept rendering the live data while claiming it was static. loadLiveAuSales's catch block now distinguishes "have prior data → CIN7 retry pending; showing previously-loaded data" vs "no prior data → CIN7 live fetch failed; no data available". Both states trigger a one-shot auto-retry after 2 seconds (guarded by a per-month _retrying sentinel so we don't loop) to ride out transient Cloudflare Access SSO blips at page-load.
    • Comparison + backfill scripts shipped in commands/: compare-amazon-au-sources.command (read-only, used to validate the cutover decision) + backfill-amazon-channel-attr.command (one-time UPDATE described above).
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.47 — 2 June 2026 (🧼 Inventory — SOAP SKUs now surface)

    • Soap stock now visible on the AU Inventory tab. Per Melanie 2026-06-02 the soap product family (AU-{scent}-NPS-100, e.g. AU-BD-NPS-100 from the 11th birthday launch) wasn't appearing in the inventory table at all. Root cause: CIN7 has soap under its own category, and buildInventory in src/cin7.js was only letting Finished Products, Bicarb Based, and uncategorised SKUs through — soap was being silently dropped before the data ever reached the dashboard.
    • Fix: special-case the *-NPS-100 suffix. New helper isAuSoapSku(code) matches the soap SKU pattern by suffix (scent-agnostic, future-proof for whatever scents launch next year). Soap SKUs bypass the category whitelist and are tagged kind:'soap', mult:1 so they stay separate from tin counts.
    • New KPI card: "Soap bars on hand" — purple accent (matches the existing PO "Soap (new)" badge palette). Subtitle shows the count of soap SKUs + "100 g bars". Soap is excluded from the tin KPIs (Loose tins on hand, Tins at Amazon FBA, Cartons on hand, Discontinued tins) so the tin numbers stay clean, and excluded from Units on order with a subtitle clarification (tins only) — birthday SKU isn't coming back until next year so any soap PO would be misleading there.
    • New row badge: Soap (100 g) in the SKU column of the inventory table, same purple palette. Low-stock badge (< 100) deliberately does NOT trigger on soap rows since the threshold is tuned for tin runway.
    • Deliberately NOT changed: the Incoming stock by SKU table on the PO tab still excludes soap. Per Melanie 2026-06-02 the only soap SKU live today is birthday-only and won't return until next year, so a run-rate / days-left / next-PO view would be misleading. Filter is automatic — buildIncomingBySku only includes kind:'base' + kind:'base-b'.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.46 — 1 June 2026 (🎂 11th Birthday tab archived & removed)

    • Tab + endpoint retired now that the 21 May 2026 launch is over. Per Melanie 2026-06-01 the polling (live Woo REST + ShipStation every 2-5 min via /api/au/birthday) was running unnecessarily. Removed: the 🎂 11th Birthday tab button, the #tab-au-birthday panel + all bday-* CSS, every bday*/_bday* JS function (countdown, refresh scheduler, render, sell-out + milestone celebration, hourly chart), the bdayOnTabActivate/Deactivate hooks in switchAuTab(), the birthdayRoutes import + app.route('/api/au', birthdayRoutes) mount in src/index.js, and the src/birthday.js module itself.
    • Full implementation preserved at archive/11th-birthday-tab/ with a README that explains what was on the tab and how to re-integrate it for future launches. Files: tab-panel.html (HTML + CSS), tab.js (all JS), birthday.backend.js (the Hono routes), plus README.md with reintegration steps + SKU constants to update.
    • ~1,100 lines lighter on the deployed bundle. The shared deps (buildShipStationSnapshot in src/shipstation.js, redactSecrets in src/redact.js) stay in place — src/logistics.js uses them.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.45 — 1 June 2026 (🚚 Logistics tab: typography dialled halfway to fit without scrolling)

    • v2.2.44 was too big — Coles column needed to scroll. Per Melanie 2026-06-01, dial back to roughly halfway between the v2.2.43 tight sizes and the v2.2.44 large sizes so all three columns fit at 1920×1080 without scroll bars.
    • Order-card text: labels 18 → 15px, SKU 16 → 13px, line text 16 → 13px, date 14 → 12px, ref + qty 13-14 → 12px. Section titles 17 → 15px, count pills 14 → 13px.
    • Tick badge 20 → 17px square; card padding 8/10 → 6/9px; margin between orders 6 → 5px. Net effect: ~25% less vertical space per order.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.44 — 1 June 2026 (🚚 Logistics tab: bigger typography for warehouse-TV readability)

    • Order-card text resized for ~3-5m viewing distance. Distributor labels 12 → 18px, must-ship-by dates 10 → 14px, SKU codes 11 → 16px, qty + reference 10 → 13-14px. Section titles 13 → 17px, count pills 11 → 14px. Empty-state copy 12 → 15px.
    • Tick badges + padding scaled to match. Green ✓ tick 14 → 20px square; red "X / Y cartons" gap pill resized accordingly. Order-card padding 5/8 → 8/10px so the bigger text has room to breathe.
    • Applies to all three columns (Coles, Woolworths, Other distributors) for visual consistency — selectors are unchanged, just the font-size declarations.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.43 — 1 June 2026 (🚚 Logistics tab: split Coles + Woolies into thirds, Waiva Clark preview mode)

    • Main row is now three equal columns — Coles | Woolworths | Other distributors. Was previously a 2fr / 1fr split with Coles + Woolies merged into a single "CW" section. Each retailer now has its own column on the warehouse TV with the channel colour stripe (Coles red, Woolies green, Distributors blue) matching the AU Sales palette. New IDs: log-coles-list, log-woolies-list, log-other-list (plus -count pills).
    • Backend: aggregateDistributorOrders() in src/logistics.js now returns three arrays (coles, woolies, otherDistributors) instead of two. classifyDistributor() stamps group: 'coles' | 'woolies' | 'otherDistributors'. Totals expose coles_count + woolies_count separately (the old coles_woolies_count aggregate is gone).
    • Tablet breakpoint added. At ≤ 1400px the layout collapses to two columns; at ≤ 1200px it stacks to one. Warehouse TV at 1920×1080 gets the full three-up.
    • Waiva Clark preview mode — ?test_waiva=1. Hit logistics.apps.nopong.com/?test_waiva=1 and the Wholesale KPI card paints red, pulses, and the sub-line shows "⚠ Waiva Clark order open" (or "⚠ Waiva Clark — N orders open" with ?test_waiva=3). Detector unchanged — the real flag fires when ShipStation's billTo.company/shipTo.company matches /waiva\s*clark/i. Preview mode bypasses the 60s KV cache and never writes the forged payload back, so removing the query param clears it immediately.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.42 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — Incoming stock by SKU: loose-vs-carton breakdown tooltip on Current stock)

    • Hover any Current stock value in the Incoming stock by SKU table to see the breakdown of loose base-SKU tins vs cartons rolled up at × 48 — e.g. on No Pong Original, the tooltip reads "4,815 loose tins + 551 cartons × 48 = 26,448 tins = 31,263 total". Helps Mel sanity-check what's behind the headline number without scrolling down to the Carton SKUs table.
    • Backend (buildIncomingBySku in src/cin7.js): baseStock map changed from a flat number to { loose, fromCartons, cartonCount } per base SKU. Three new fields on each output row: stock_loose_tins, stock_from_cartons, stock_carton_count. Total current_stock is the sum (unchanged behaviour).
    • Frontend: Current stock cell wrapped in a <span> with a dotted underline (cursor:help) so the affordance is visible; tooltip text only renders the parts that have non-zero values (a SKU with no cartons just says "X loose tins"; a SKU with only carton stock just says "Y cartons × 48 = Z tins"). Graceful fallback to a plain number when the breakdown fields aren't present (older cached responses).
    • CSV export: three new drill-down columns (Loose tins, Carton count, Tins from cartons) so the spreadsheet view matches the tooltip.
    • Column header tooltip updated to mention the per-row hover behaviour.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.41 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — Carton SKUs table: actually fix the column spacing this time)

    • v2.2.40 didn't actually fix the spacing — the numeric columns were still slammed against the right edge of the table. Root cause: AU's CIN7 product names are 50–60 chars long and all start with the same boilerplate ("CARTON OF 4 SRT'S - 48 X NO PONG …"). The Contents column was flexing to absorb that, soaking up the full ~1300 px of remaining table width on a wide monitor and pushing the two numeric columns into a thin ribbon at the far right.
    • Real fix, two parts:
      1. Strip the boilerplate in the renderer via regex /NO\s*PONG\s+(.+)$/i. So "CARTON OF 4 SRT'S - 48 X NO PONG ORIGINAL 35G" displays as just "ORIGINAL 35G" — matches the visual rhythm of the CA tab's "Original — Original formula" entries. Full CIN7 name lives in the cell's title tooltip for anyone who needs to verify.
      2. Rebalance the colgroup widths: SKU 200 px, Contents 340 px (fixed — no longer flexes), numeric columns share whatever's left. Numeric headers and values now sit at roughly the middle-right of the table rather than the far edge.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.40 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — Carton SKUs table: column spacing fix)

    • Numeric columns no longer slam against the right edge. v2.2.39's Carton SKUs table inherited the default td.num right-alignment with only 6 px right padding, so on AU (which has fewer carton SKUs than CA) the Contents column absorbed all the slack and the "Cartons in stock" / "Equivalent tins" numbers ended up as a tiny ribbon hugging the right edge of the table.
    • Fix: <colgroup> on the table with explicit widths (SKU 180 px fixed, Contents flexes, two numeric columns 170 px / 180 px). New .au-cartons-num class applied to header + data + footer cells bumps the right padding to 24 px, so the headers and values sit at a comfortable distance from the edge instead of being slammed against it. Proportions now match the CA carton table at the same screen width.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.39 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — new "Carton SKUs" table at the bottom, mirroring the CA tab)

    • New "Carton SKUs — wholesale / retail packing" table at the bottom of the AU PO tab, sitting under "Incoming stock by SKU". Same 4-column shape as the CA equivalent: SKU / Contents / Cartons in stock / Equivalent tins.
    • Why: when Lauren is looking at total tins on a PO and wants to sanity-check carton stock × 48 = tin equivalents, she can scroll to the bottom of the tab and see it in one place rather than mentally multiplying. The previously-implicit relationship between carton SKU rows and base-SKU rolled-up tins is now explicit.
    • Data source: reads from AU_INVENTORY_DATA filtered to kind === 'carton' (AU-CTN-...-48 rows). All AU cartons are ×48 multiplier; SRT trays (×12) are already folded into the base SKU's stock upstream, and mixed mega-trays (×72) are filtered out at buildInventory. So this table is 1:1 with the CA pattern — no behaviour difference between regions.
    • Footer total row for total cartons in stock + total tin equivalents, so Lauren has the headline number on the same view.
    • Live re-renders on the live /api/au/inventory response landing — no static-vs-live lag.
    • CSV export via the same pattern as the other AU tables. Includes the footer total row so spreadsheet readers don't have to recompute.
    • Discontinued cartons excluded (matches the rest of the AU dashboard's behaviour).
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.38 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — revert raw-tin rollup: Incoming qty is now fully-filled tins only)

    • Reversing the v2.2.36 raw-tin rollup decision. Mel's reasoning: the "Incoming qty" column means "ready-to-sell stock arriving" — raw tins (T-AU-...) still need filling before they belong there, and the runway from current stock is already captured by Days left. Same logic for "Next PO (days)" — that should be when the next batch of finished tins arrives, not when the next raw-tin batch lands.
    • Backend (buildIncomingBySku in src/cin7.js): dropped the T-AU-... branch from the line-classification loop. Removed raw_qty from the per-SKU accumulator. Removed raw_incoming_qty and has_raw_only from the output row shape. deriveNextPo now only sees finished-tin POs, so the "Next PO (days)" value reflects ready-to-sell arrivals only.
    • Frontend (public/index.html): dropped the yellow raw badge and the mixed-row asterisk + tooltip in the Incoming qty cell. Updated the SKU column tooltip and the Incoming qty column tooltip to reflect the new scope. Table-hint refreshed.
    • CSV export: dropped the Raw incoming qty and Raw only columns. All open POs remains as the right-edge drill-down column.
    • Packaging (P-...) still excluded — same reason as before. If a packaging fold-in becomes useful later, it'll need a separate SKU-mapping conversation with Mel.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.37 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — size filter on the per-SKU table: All / 35 g / 85 g)

    • Segmented control above the "Incoming stock by SKU" table: All / 35 g / 85 g. Lets Mel narrow the bottom table to just the standard finished-tin sizes when she wants to scan the full-tin pipeline without 100 g birthday soaps / other formats getting in the way.
    • Filter applies to size derived from the SKU's trailing digits (e.g. AU-OG-NPO-35 → '35', AU-OG-NPO-85 → '85'). Anything without a clean trailing -35 / -85 (e.g. AU-BD-NPS-100 birthday soap, or any future format) shows only when the filter is on "All". No backend change — size is already encoded in the SKU string from CIN7.
    • Choice persists in localStorage under au_incoming_size_filter, so reload-and-resume keeps your last view. Falls back to 'All' silently if localStorage is unavailable (e.g. private-browsing edge case).
    • CSV export honours the active filter. Filename gains a -35g / -85g suffix when filtered so you don't accidentally overwrite the previous export.
    • Empty-state copy for "no SKUs match the 35 g filter" so the table isn't silent when the dataset has no matches.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.36 — 27 May 2026 (AU PO — per-SKU table reshaped to mirror CA + Next PO (days) column + raw-tin rollup)

    • "Incoming stock by SKU" table now matches the CA layout. Columns: SKU / Current stock / Run rate (units/day) / Days left / Next PO (days) / Incoming qty / PO ref / Risk status. Previously AU was missing the per-SKU days-until-arrival readout entirely — the only "when" signal was the risk badge's "-incoming" suffix, which doesn't tell you whether the next PO is 3 days away or 3 months. Mel called this out as not useful versus the CA equivalent.
    • New "Next PO (days)" column. Backend (buildIncomingBySku in src/cin7.js) now joins each base SKU to its open POs and picks the earliest future expected_date. The value is days from today. Overdue POs (negative days) surface in red — "the PO you were expecting hasn't landed yet" is still information Mel needs to see. If no PO for this SKU has any date set in CIN7, the cell is blank rather than a misleading zero. Future-dated POs always win over overdue ones if both exist.
    • Raw tins (T-AU-...) now fold into the matching finished SKU row. Previously raw-tin POs were excluded from the per-SKU view entirely — if the only thing on order was raw tins, the SKU showed "Incoming: —" even though supply was genuinely en route. Now T-AU-OG-NPO-35 contributes to the AU-OG-NPO-35 row's incoming_qty and next_po_days. To keep the distinction honest, rows where ALL incoming is still raw get a yellow raw badge next to the qty (those tins still need filling before they're sellable). Mixed rows (some finished + some raw) get a subtle asterisk + tooltip.
    • Packaging (P-...) deferred. CIN7 packaging SKUs don't have a clean machine-readable mapping to a single finished SKU — they're named by human-readable description ("SRTs for CL-VLB"). Folding them in needs Mel's mapping rules; tracking as a separate follow-up so we don't ship a half-mapping.
    • New backend fields on each row: next_po_days, next_po_ref, raw_incoming_qty, has_raw_only. next_po_refs kept for back-compat (now an array of ref strings, not objects).
    • Column rename: "Run rate (tins/day)" → "Run rate (units/day)" for CA parity. Tooltip still notes it's tins (AU only ships tins).
    • CSV export updated to match. 8 on-screen columns plus 3 drill-down columns on the right edge: Raw incoming qty, Raw only, All open POs (semicolon-joined).
    • Table-hint and tooltips refreshed to explain the new column + the raw-tin behaviour.
    • NA dashboard untouched (per the per-region versioning rule). NA stays at v2.35.2.

    v2.2.35 — 21 May 2026 (Mic-drop milestone bumped 2,000 → 2,100)

    • 2,000 mic-drop milestone never fired because the All-orders-today count had already crossed 2,000 by the time v2.2.34 deployed (~14:13 AEST). The first-load suppression logic correctly marked it as already-fired silently — the celebration was lost. Per Melanie: rather than retroactively replay it, bump the threshold so we get a fresh crossing to celebrate.
    • Fix. _BDAY_MILESTONES entry #4 (mic-drop) threshold 2,000 → 2,100; banner text updated to "🎤 2,100 ORDERS — MIC DROP! 🎤". No other state changes — the localStorage flag for "2000" is now harmless dead data and will be ignored.
    • Now reads: 500 🔥 → 1,000 🔨 → 1,500 ⚡ → 1,733 🎯 → 2,100 🎤 → 2,500 🚀.

    v2.2.34 — 21 May 2026 (Birthday tab: two more milestones beyond the goal 🎤🚀)

    • Two new milestone celebrations on the 🎂 11th Birthday tab, sitting above the 1,733 bullseye for when we blow past last year's total.
    • 2,000 orders — 🎤 "MIC DROP!" Microphone falls in from above-left, lands centre-stage with a bounce, settles, then exits stage right with a 360° spin. Deep purple → gold gradient banner. Purple + gold confetti. 5s loop (slightly slower than the others — the drop-and-settle reads better at this cadence).
    • 2,500 orders — 🚀 "WE'RE ROCKETING!" Rocket flies left→right with a slight upward arc (top: 65% → 25%). Pink → gold → blue gradient banner. Pink + gold + blue confetti. 3s loop (faster than the others for that "blasting past" energy).
    • Same machinery as the existing four. No changes to _bdayCheckMilestoneTransitions() or _bdayCelebrateMilestone() — the detector walks _BDAY_MILESTONES low-to-high and picks up the two new entries automatically. Per-day localStorage persistence + first-load suppression + highest-threshold-wins all behave identically.
    • Now reads: 500 🔥 → 1,000 🔨 → 1,500 ⚡ → 1,733 🎯 (last year's goal) → 2,000 🎤 → 2,500 🚀.

    v2.2.33 — 21 May 2026 (🚨 Launch-day hotfix: birthday endpoint was capped at 1,000 orders)

    • Bug. fetchOrdersSince() in src/birthday.js had a hardcoded maxPages: 10 safety belt — combined with per_page: 100 this capped total_orders_today at exactly 1,000. The dashboard froze at 1,000 mid-morning during the 11th-birthday launch while real Woo orders kept climbing past it. Confirmed via DevTools: Fresh total_orders_today: 1000 on a ?refresh=1 bypass of the KV cache, with no error in the payload — the endpoint was happily returning a truncated count as if it were complete.
    • Fix. Bumped maxPages from 10 to 100 (10,000-order ceiling). The Woo x-wp-totalpages header already drives the actual loop exit (if (page >= totalPages) break), so the cap is purely a runaway-loop backstop — raising it has zero impact on cost when daily volume is below the new ceiling.
    • Why keep a cap at all. Defence against an upstream bug where Woo reports a wrong totalPages count and we end up fetching forever. 100 is high enough to cover any conceivable No Pong day and low enough to surface as a runaway loop rather than burning the worker's full CPU budget.
    • Knock-on effect. The 🎂 11th Birthday tab's milestone celebrations (🔥 500 / 🔨 1,000 / ⚡ 1,500 / 🎯 1,733) couldn't fire above 1,000 with the bug in place. Now unblocked — 1,500 and 1,733 will trigger correctly as the day continues.

    v2.2.32 — 19 May 2026 (🚚 Logistics tab: TV-density rework + Waiva Clark flag)

    • Removed the left column from the Logistics tab. The two ShipStation alert sections (Express/Intl still open, Wholesale still open) were duplicating counts already shown on the KPI strip and eating ~42% of the horizontal space. Per Melanie 2026-05-19: "I just want a number of orders. I don't need the actual order numbers." Both order lists deleted; the KPI strip retains the counts.
    • Distributor row now Coles+Woolies (2/3) + Other Distributors (1/3) side-by-side. The full TV width is reclaimed for distributor orders so 4-6 orders can fit per section without scrolling (the warehouse TV can't scroll). .log-main-row grid moves from 1fr 1.4fr (split between alerts and distributors) to 2fr 1fr (CW:Other).
    • Line-item SKU display stripped to fragrance+formula. Per Melanie: "I really just need the variant, so the fragrance and the formula part of it." New _logShortSku() helper drops the AU- prefix and the trailing -CTN-NN / -xNN / -NN pack-size suffix. So AU-FF-NPV-CTN-48 → FF-NPV, AU-OG-NPO-35 → OG-NPO, AU-BD-NBF-35 → BD-NBF. WC- prefix is preserved (wholesale signal). The wordy product-name field on each line is gone — the short SKU carries the variant info on its own.
    • Order-card density tightened. Card padding 8px/10px → 5px/8px, card margin 6px → 5px, line padding 2px → 1px, line height shrunk via 14px ticks (down from 16px), order-label 13px → 12px, date line 11px → 10px. Roughly 50% denser per order — 4-6 orders per section now fits 1080p comfortably.
    • New right-aligned qty column on each line. Each line now reads ✓ FF-NPV ........ 100 cartons (fulfillable) or 60/100 FF-NPV ........ cartons (short, with the gap inside the red pill). The qty pushes to the right via margin-left: auto so the SKU column lines up cleanly across multiple lines.
    • Waiva Clark wholesale flag. Per Melanie: "if it's an order from Waiva Clark... put an extra asterisk or something, a flag." Backend buildShipStationSnapshot() now emits company on each wholesale summary (from billTo.company with shipTo.company fallback); the logistics endpoint runs a case-insensitive /waiva\s*clark/i match across them and sets shipstation.waiva_clark_open + shipstation.waiva_clark_open_count on the payload. Frontend paints the Wholesale KPI card red with a pulsing box-shadow and swaps the sub-line to ⚠ WAIVA CLARK ORDER OPEN (or ⚠ Waiva Clark — N orders open if there's more than one) so it pops from across the warehouse floor.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit /api/au/logistics?refresh=1 — payload's shipstation block should now include waiva_clark_open + waiva_clark_open_count fields, and each entry in wholesaleOpenOrders should include a company field. On the dashboard, open the 🚚 Logistics tab and confirm: no left column, Coles+Woolies on the left 2/3, Other on the right 1/3, line items show short SKUs only (e.g. OG-NPO not AU-OG-NPO-CTN-48).

    v2.2.31 — 19 May 2026 (Flip hammer + bullseye horizontally so they face the direction of travel)

    • Hammer + bullseye now face forwards. v2.2.29's sweep animations had the 🔨 hammer and 🎯 bullseye facing left while the sweep travels left→right, so they looked like they were flying backwards. Added scaleX(-1) to every keyframe in @keyframes bday-hammer-swing and @keyframes bday-bullseye-hit so the hammer head + the bullseye both face the direction of travel. Flame and lightning bolt sweeps untouched (they're already symmetric enough that the flip wasn't needed).

    v2.2.30 — 19 May 2026 (Milestone sweeps now render in front of the banner)

    • Z-index fix. The 🔥 / 🔨 / ⚡ / 🎯 emoji sweeps were rendering behind the milestone banner in v2.2.29 (sweep at z-index: 99998, banner at 99999). Bumped .bday-milestone-sweep to z-index: 100000 so the emoji flies across the front of the banner, not hidden behind it. The banner stays at 99999 so it still sits above everything else.

    v2.2.29 — 19 May 2026 (Birthday tab: order-count milestone celebrations 🔥🔨⚡🎯)

    • Four new 15-second milestone celebrations on the 🎂 11th Birthday tab, triggered off the All orders today counter (data.woo.total_orders_today). When the count crosses each threshold for the first time today, a giant emoji sweeps across the screen, a centred banner bounce-ins, and themed confetti fires from both bottom corners for 15 seconds (same family of effects as the sell-out celebration).
    • 500 orders — 🔥 "You're on fire!" Flame sweeps across with a gentle rotation. Orange → gold gradient banner. Warm-coloured confetti.
    • 1,000 orders — 🔨 "You're nailing it!" Hammer swings back and forth as it sweeps. Purple gradient banner. Purple + gold + silver confetti.
    • 1,500 orders — ⚡ "You're electrifying!" Lightning bolt strobes across with a flash effect. Gold → electric-blue → indigo gradient banner. Cyan + gold confetti.
    • 1,733 orders (last year's total) — 🎯 "You've hit the bull's-eye!" Bullseye flies in from the left, lands centre-stage with a satisfying scale-bounce, then exits stage right. Red → gold → red gradient banner (slightly larger than the others — this is THE moment). Red + gold + pink confetti.
    • Per-day localStorage persistence keyed by bday-milestones-fired-YYYY-MM-DD (Sydney-local date). If a browser tab reload happens after a milestone fires, it won't re-fire on the next refresh. Date key resets at Sydney midnight so tomorrow starts fresh.
    • First-load suppression. Same pattern as the sell-out detector — on the very first load (where _bdayPrevOrderCount === undefined), no milestones fire even if the count is already past one. Otherwise opening the dashboard mid-day at 1,200 orders would fire 500 + 1,000 instantly. Already-crossed thresholds get silently marked as "fired today" so they don't fire later either.
    • Highest-threshold-wins on a surge. If a single refresh crosses two thresholds at once (e.g. count jumps from 480 to 1,050 between ticks), only the higher one fires — the lower one is silently marked fired. Keeps the screen sane and the warehouse TV from playing back-to-back celebrations.
    • Independent of sell-out confetti. Both can fire on the same refresh cycle; banners stack vertically (milestone at 38%, sell-out at 32% / 50%). Confetti from both events overlap — intentional, more party.

    v2.2.28 — 19 May 2026 (🚚 Logistics tab UI — Ship 2 of 2)

    • New 🚚 Logistics tab in the AU tab bar (purple, between 🎂 11th Birthday and the existing tabs). TV-fit 16:9 no-scroll layout sized for the warehouse TV.
    • Hero strip with a big open-queue counter on the right.
    • ShipStation KPI strip (5 cards): Open queue · Shipped today (orders) · Shipped today (items) · Express/Intl open · Wholesale open.
    • Two-column main row. Left: Express/Intl still open (pink stripe) + Wholesale still open (purple stripe), both with the order list inline. Right: Coles + Woolworths (green stripe) + Other distributors (blue stripe), each with order cards showing distributor label · reference · must-ship-by date · per-line tick (green ✓) or gap (red "X / Y cartons").
    • Past-due rows go red (light red background + red border + "PAST DUE" suffix on the distributor label + red bolded date line).
    • 60-second auto-refresh while the tab is active; pauses when you switch away.
    • Refresh-now button in the meta bar for immediate updates.
    • Backend was Ship 1 (v2.2.27 through v2.2.27i — see entries below for that iteration). This is Ship 2 closing out the workstream.

    v2.2.27i — 19 May 2026 (Hotfix: CIN7 where clause needs full ISO 8601 timestamp)

    • Fixed CIN7 400 "not a valid date time" error in v2.2.27h's EstimatedDeliveryDate>='2026-05-19' where clause. CIN7 Omni v1 requires yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ format, not bare YYYY-MM-DD. Now passes ${todayLocalDate}T00:00:00Z (Sydney midnight today, expressed in UTC).

    v2.2.27h — 19 May 2026 (Logistics: filter by EstimatedDeliveryDate >= today)

    • Per Melanie: "just look for orders with an estimated due date of today or in the future." Server-side where in fetchOpenSalesOrdersLive() simplified from CreatedDate>='<30 days ago>' to EstimatedDeliveryDate>='<today Sydney>'. Much cleaner than chasing status + stage + dispatchedDate semantics — the warehouse only acts on orders whose ETD is upcoming, anything overdue is a separate ops conversation. Client-side dispatchedDate IS NULL filter retained to hide in-transit orders that no longer need warehouse action. OPEN_ORDER_WINDOW_DAYS constant dropped — no longer needed.

    v2.2.27g — 19 May 2026 (Logistics: pivot SalesOrders fetch from D1 to live CIN7)

    • Bypass D1 for SalesOrders entirely. v2.2.27f's D1-based filter worked correctly for orders modified today, but missed real open Coles+Woolies EDI orders (e.g. 0111020225 + 0111015771 from May 18-19) whose modifiedDate was before today's watermark. Trying to lower the watermark hit the v2.2.10 strict-greater-than tie-group bug (5000+ row ties at 2026-04-11T14:00:06Z and 2026-05-16T14:00:03Z) that we'd have to fix in the sync infrastructure to resolve cleanly. For the warehouse-TV use case, going LIVE against CIN7 is simpler and more reliable: typical open-order count is <20, so it's one CIN7 call per dashboard refresh.
    • New fetchOpenSalesOrdersLive() in src/logistics.js. Server-side where filter: CreatedDate>='<30 days ago>' AND Status='APPROVED'. Client-side: drop rows with dispatchedDate set, drop voided. Field whitelist mirrors the cron's SALES_FIELDS so estimatedDeliveryDate + stage are captured. deliveryDate on the returned shape is sourced from estimatedDeliveryDate.
    • Rate-limit math. Two CIN7 calls per request (SalesOrders + Stock), serialized. KV cache 60s caps absolute volume at ~60 dashboard refreshes/hour = 120 CIN7 calls/hour = 2,880/day. CIN7 limits: 3/sec, 60/min, 5,000/day — comfortably within all three.
    • Migration 0007 columns stay in place. The cron continues to populate stage / dispatched_date / delivery_date on every tick; other endpoints can use them. This endpoint just doesn't read them.

    v2.2.27f — 19 May 2026 (Logistics: promote stage / dispatched_date / delivery_date to first-class D1 columns)

    • Migration 0007 adds three columns to cin7_sales_orders: stage, dispatched_date, delivery_date. v2.2.27e's live-CIN7 probe on order 76910 (Coles QLD RedBank) revealed: status: "APPROVED" but stage: "Dispatched", with a real dispatchedDate: "2026-05-08T01:44:00Z" — the order shipped 11 days ago. The reason it was showing as "open" is the raw_json column is empty for many backfill-era rows, so the json_extract(raw_json, '$.dispatchedDate') IS NULL filter passed for every row regardless. First-class columns fix this for good.
    • Corrected the ETD field. v2.2.27d theory that createdDate == ETD for EDI orders was wrong. The probe showed order 76910 has createdDate: "2026-05-03" but estimatedDeliveryDate: "2026-05-10" — 7 days apart. estimatedDeliveryDate is the CIN7 API field for the "Delivery Date (ETD)" shown in the UI; the cron now writes it to the new delivery_date column.
    • Logistics endpoint filter rewritten using the new columns: stage = 'Processing' AND dispatched_date IS NULL AND status NOT IN (VOID/VOIDED/CANCELLED) AND created_date >= today − 30 days. No more json_extract dependency.
    • Cron updates. SALES_FIELDS in src/cin7-sync.js now includes estimatedDeliveryDate and stage. Write path populates the three new columns on every upsert.
    • One-time backfill needed to repopulate existing rows. After deploy: trigger /api/admin/cin7-backfill (or run the existing admin backfill loop) so the cron re-fetches recent orders and writes the new column values. Without this, only orders modified after the cron's first post-migration tick will have stage / dispatched_date / delivery_date set, and the warehouse TV will under-report until those propagate.
    • Removed the v2.2.27e ?debug-live branch — job done.

    v2.2.27e — 19 May 2026 (Logistics endpoint: live-CIN7 status probe + zero-qty filter)

    • Diagnosed why we're seeing weeks-old "open" orders. The json_extract(raw_json, '$.dispatchedDate') IS NULL filter relies on raw_json being populated — but raw_json is empty in D1 for most rows, so json_extract(NULL, ...) returns NULL, which IS NULL, which passes the filter for every row. So we're effectively showing all APPROVED orders regardless of dispatch state. Also: Melanie's expected open filter is status = 'Open' AND stage = 'Processing', but the current SQL has status = 'APPROVED' — likely reading the wrong CIN7 field. A proper fix needs to promote dispatchedDate + stage to first-class D1 columns (migration + cron change + backfill), but first we need to see exactly what CIN7 returns.
    • Added ?debug-live=<orderId> probe. One-shot live CIN7 SalesOrders fetch by id with no fields filter (returns ALL fields). Surfaces all top-level keys plus the status-ish and date-ish ones explicitly. Lets us inspect a single order to find the right filter fields without a full migration churn. Costs 1 CIN7 call, well within rate limits.
    • Zero-quantity line items now filtered out. Two of the "stale" orders had qty: 0 line items (zeroed by the retailer post-confirmation) that rendered as "0 / 0 cartons" — visual noise. New filter drops any line item where qty <= 0 at the aggregator level.

    v2.2.27d — 19 May 2026 (Logistics endpoint: EDI delivery date = createdDate)

    • Use createdDate as the delivery date for Coles + Woolies orders. The "Delivery Date (ETD)" Melanie pointed to in CIN7's UI lives there because EDI intake maps the EDI's "start date" (which IS the requested delivery date) into the order's createdDate field. No separate API field to chase — we already have it. Per-retailer logic: cls.group === 'colesWoolies' uses o.createdDate as the delivery source; other distributors keep the prior deliveryDate path (still null, but sorted by reference so it doesn't matter for them).
    • Must-ship-by-business-day-before now works. Coles QLD (RedBank) + Coles VIC (Somerton) orders correctly compute must_ship_by = previousBusinessDay(delivery_date) for the first time. Coles NSW (Kemps Creek) + both Woolies DCs use the raw delivery date as the must-ship-by.
    • Removed ?debug=1 branch. Did its job in v2.2.27c — we now know createdDate is the ETD source.

    v2.2.27c — 19 May 2026 (Logistics endpoint: actually-open filter + date-field debug)

    • Filter to actually-open orders. v2.2.27b's "open" definition was too loose — dispatchedDate IS NULL caught 95 orders including some from November 2025 that were despatched outside CIN7 and never closed. Tightened the SQL filter: status = 'APPROVED' AND dispatchedDate IS NULL AND created_date >= today − 30 days. The 30-day window matches real warehouse ops cadence (the older "Coles" / "Woolworths" rows without a DC suffix were all legacy data pre-current-naming-convention — they fall out automatically).
    • Added ?debug=1 query param. Surfaces date-looking field names + values from one recent Coles/Woolies order's raw_json, plus the full top-level key list. v2.2.27b's json_extract(raw_json, '$.deliveryDate') returned null on every order — the manually-entered delivery date Melanie referenced lives under a different field name. This branch finds it without a deploy-eyeball-deploy loop. Hit /api/au/logistics?debug=1.

    v2.2.27b — 19 May 2026 (Hotfix #2: read SalesOrders from D1 instead of live CIN7)

    • Switched /api/au/logistics SalesOrders fetch to D1. v2.2.27a's serialisation fixed the per-second 429 but the CIN7 5000-call daily budget was already crowded (existing cron + on-demand tabs + my two earlier smoke tests). New helper fetchOpenSalesOrdersFromD1() reads from cin7_sales_orders + cin7_sales_order_items (already kept fresh every 15 min by runCin7SalesOrdersChunk). dispatchedDate and deliveryDate aren't first-class columns — they live inside raw_json — so the query uses SQLite's json_extract to filter on them at read time. No migration needed.
    • Stock stays live. One single /Stock endpoint call with internal pagination — well within rate limits, and the warehouse needs current-moment stock for the green-tick fulfilment check. The 15-min D1 lag on SalesOrders is acceptable for permanent warehouse-TV ops (vs the launch-day birthday tab where we needed real-time).
    • Removed. fetchOpenSalesOrders (live CIN7 fetcher), unused OPEN_ORDER_LOOKBACK_DAYS + VOID_STATUSES constants, and the cin7FetchAll import — all replaced by the SQL path.

    v2.2.27a — 19 May 2026 (Hotfix: serialise CIN7 calls in /api/au/logistics)

    • Fixed CIN7 429 on the new logistics endpoint. v2.2.27 ran fetchOpenSalesOrders and fetchStockBySku concurrently via Promise.allSettled, which trips CIN7 Omni's 3-calls-per-second cap once internal pagination kicks in (Stock alone returns several pages). Serialised the two CIN7 calls: SalesOrders first, then Stock only if SalesOrders succeeded (back-to-back failures usually mean a wider outage; skipping the second call avoids burning rate budget for nothing). ShipStation still runs in parallel with the CIN7 work — it's a separate API and doesn't share a rate-limit bucket. Matches the existing memory project_cin7_omni_constraints.md: "don't run endpoints concurrently".

    v2.2.27 — 19 May 2026 (Logistics tab backend — Ship 1 of 2)

    • New /api/au/logistics endpoint (src/logistics.js) for the upcoming warehouse-TV Logistics tab. Surfaces a ShipStation snapshot (reused unchanged from the Birthday tab via buildShipStationSnapshot()) plus open distributor orders from CIN7 Omni v1, split into two groups: Coles + Woolworths (sorted by must-ship-by ascending) and Other distributors (Momentum, AVO, etc., sorted by CIN7 reference ascending). KV-cached 60s under au:logistics:v1; bypasses D1 for the same real-time reasoning as the birthday tab.
    • DC → state/label mapping for Coles + Woolies. Coles RedBank → "Coles QLD", Coles Somerton → "Coles VIC", Coles Kemps Creek → "Coles NSW"; Woolworths Moorebank + Erskine Park labelled per-DC. Retailer attribution runs first via the existing attributeCin7Order(), so the shared "Redbank" DC name disambiguates correctly between Coles and Woolies (the latter doesn't actually use Redbank; safety belt remains in place if that changes).
    • Must-ship-by-business-day-before rule. For Coles QLD (RedBank) + Coles VIC (Somerton) the warehouse needs to ship the business day BEFORE the customer's delivery date. New previousBusinessDay() helper skips Saturdays and Sundays; AU public holidays are NOT yet skipped (state-specific holiday calendars left as a follow-up). Coles NSW + all Woolies + every other distributor: must-ship-by = delivery date itself.
    • Per-line carton math + stock comparison. Each open distributor line item is evaluated for cartons_needed (handles Alt UOM lines via uomSize, and carton SKUs via normalizeAuSku() multipliers) versus cartons_available (warehouse-only stock = avail − fba_avail; Amazon FBA stock can't fulfil distributor orders). Fulfillable lines get an is_fulfillable: true flag; short lines get a "60 / 100 cartons" gap display. Past-due rows (must-ship-by strictly before today) carry is_past_due: true.
    • Backend only — no tab UI yet. This is Ship 1 of a two-ship plan: get the endpoint live and verify the numbers against real CIN7 data first, then build the TV-fit 16:9 frontend in Ship 2. Smoke test by hitting /api/au/logistics?refresh=1 after deploy.
    • Minor. Added export to cin7FetchAll and fetchStockBySku in src/cin7.js (additive only — both helpers were already designed for reuse, just hadn't crossed a module boundary yet).

    v2.2.26 — 19 May 2026 (Birthday tab: Woo stock-quantity readout + sell-out confetti 🎉)

    • Woo stock-quantity readout on the Soap + Tin KPI cards. New blue line ("Stock left: 47") sits between the units-sold value and the SKU sub-line. Backend pulls stock_quantity, stock_status, and manage_stock from the same Woo /products?sku=... call we were already making — no new API hits. When Woo's stock management is off for a SKU, the readout shows "Stock: not tracked in Woo" in grey so the team knows it's a Woo config thing, not a dashboard bug.
    • Sell-out confetti 🎉. When stock transitions from positive to zero between refreshes, the tab fires a 15-second canvas-confetti shower from both bottom corners plus a centred "SOLD OUT!" banner with the product name. Lazy-loads canvas-confetti@1.9.3 from jsdelivr (~5 KB minified) the first time the bday tab is activated, so it's pre-warmed by the time the first drop sells out. If the CDN load fails (offline / CSP) the banner still renders without particles — graceful degrade.
    • Per-SKU transition tracking. Soap and tin track independently in _bdayPrevStock. On the first load (where prev === undefined), no confetti even if stock is at 0 — otherwise opening the dashboard mid-launch when a drop has already sold out would auto-fire celebrations on every refresh. Confetti only fires on the actual >0 → 0 transition. Subsequent refreshes while still at 0 do nothing, and the next drop's 0 → 50 transition (refilling stock for the next hour) also doesn't fire — that's a drop, not a sell-out. When tin and soap sell out in the same refresh, both banners stack vertically and one confetti shower covers both.
    • "SOLD OUT" stock-line state. When stock hits 0, the inline readout switches from blue "Stock left: N" to red "🎉 SOLD OUT 🎉" with a slow pulse animation. Acts as a persistent indicator after the banner fades, so the warehouse team can see at a glance which drops are still active vs gone.
    • Image-card layout bumped to 4 rows. Card was 3 rows (label / value / sub); v2.2.26 adds the stock line as row 3, pushing sub to row 4. grid-row: span 4 on the thumbnail keeps it centred vertically across all four rows.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit /api/au/birthday-launch?refresh=1 — payload should now include woo.soap_stock_quantity, woo.soap_manage_stock, woo.tin_stock_quantity, woo.tin_manage_stock. On the dashboard, soap + tin cards show a blue stock line; once Woo has stock management turned on for those SKUs and stock is loaded for the first drop, refreshes will tick the number down and the sell-out moment will trigger the celebration.

    v2.2.25 — 19 May 2026 (Birthday tab: TV-fit 16:9 no-scroll layout + product images + envelope icons)

    • TV-fit no-scroll layout. The tab now goes on a warehouse TV (1920×1080, 16:9) and needs to fit a single viewport — no one's scrolling on a TV from across the warehouse floor. Panel restructured as a 4-row flex column: hero / 7-card KPI strip / chart-and-alerts main row (flex:1 to fill remaining vertical space) / compact meta bar. Below 1200px wide the layout falls back to scrollable so it still works on a laptop.
    • One KPI row instead of two separated sections. The pre-v2.2.25 layout had a Woo section (4 cards) and a ShipStation section (3 cards) on separate rows with section-title dividers. v2.2.25 folds all seven cards into a single 7-column grid — pink left-stripe (border-left: 3px solid #FF578A) on Woo cards, purple left-stripe on ShipStation cards so the source is still legible at a glance. Saves a chunk of vertical space and frees the chart to grow.
    • Product images on the soap + tin cards. Backend now fires a second Woo /products?sku=AU-BD-NBF-35 call in parallel with the existing soap fetch and the orders fetch — both product responses' images[0].src URLs are surfaced as woo.soap_image_url and woo.tin_image_url on the payload. Frontend renders them as 64×64 thumbnails inside the relevant cards, with a pink-gradient placeholder when the URL is missing or 404s. Image loads fall back to the placeholder via img.onerror rather than showing a broken-image icon.
    • Envelope mini-icons on the order-count cards (All orders / Launch-product orders) for visual rhythm with the SKU-image cards. Inline SVGs — no external assets, no extra HTTP requests.
    • Chart sized via flex:1, not fixed pixels. The chart canvas now grows to fill whatever vertical space remains after the hero / KPI / meta rows take their natural heights. On a 1080p TV that's ~480px of chart, big enough to read drop-hour bars from across the room. Chart.js's maintainAspectRatio: false handles the resize.
    • SS-disconnected dimming refactored. Previous wrapper-based dim is gone (couldn't survive the cards being flattened into one row). New: a bday-ss-down class toggles on the panel root, CSS rule #tab-au-birthday.bday-ss-down .bday-card--ss { opacity: 0.5 } dims all three SS cards. Identical UX, cleaner CSS.
    • Verify after deploy. Open the dashboard on the warehouse TV (or any 16:9 fullscreen browser): bday tab should fit one viewport, no scrollbar. Soap + tin cards should render product thumbnails (provided the SKUs exist in Woo). Hit /api/au/birthday-launch?refresh=1 and the JSON should now include woo.soap_image_url + woo.tin_image_url.

    v2.2.24 — 19 May 2026 (Birthday tab: side-by-side Express/Intl + Wholesale alert cards)

    • New wholesale alert card. Sits next to the existing Express/International card — same shape, same "open orders" call-out, different colour (purple/violet). Surfaces open ShipStation orders the fulfilment team should flag before the launch rush, so a wholesale-carton order doesn't get buried under hundreds of consumer orders. List shows order number, the wholesale SKUs that triggered the match, and the shipping service.
    • Wholesale detection: SKU pattern, not Woo category. ShipStation's WC integration doesn't reliably expose Woo's "wholesale" product category, so we match by SKU instead. An order is wholesale if any line item matches:
      • WC-* — wholesale custom (e.g. WC-OG-NPO-35)
      • *-SRT-* — sleeve / tray packs (e.g. AU-SRT-x12)
      • *-CTN-* — cartons (e.g. AU-CTN-48, where most AU wholesale volume lives)
      Case-insensitive. To add or remove a pattern, edit WHOLESALE_SKU_PATTERNS in src/shipstation.js — single source of truth.
    • Layout: two cards in a 2-col grid. On wide viewports the Express/Intl and Wholesale cards sit side-by-side (each 50% wide). Below 900px they stack to 1-col. The cards live below the ShipStation stat row, where the single full-width alert used to be.
    • Frontend refactor: the v2.2.21 inline alert rendering is now in a shared _bdayRenderAlertCard() helper that handles both cards' loading/empty/error/disconnected states. Each card's per-row markup is passed in as a rowFor callback. No backend behaviour change for express/intl — the JSON shape is identical to v2.2.23, just with new wholesaleOpen + wholesaleOpenOrders fields alongside the existing expressIntlOpen + expressIntlOpenOrders.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit /api/au/birthday-launch?refresh=1 and check the shipstation block has wholesaleOpen + wholesaleOpenOrders fields. On the dashboard the bday tab should now show two side-by-side cards under the SS row.

    v2.2.23 — 19 May 2026 (Birthday tab: strict-today Woo window + 12-bar 7am–7pm chart)

    • Woo after filter switched to naive Sydney-local. v2.2.22 passed a UTC-flavoured ISO (2026-05-19T00:00:00.000Z) to WC's after filter. WC reads after as naive store-local time even when a Z is appended — so the filter was actually starting from "May 18 at 14:00 Sydney" (yesterday afternoon) instead of Sydney midnight today. Net effect: total_orders_today: 230 at 11am Sydney with phantom counts in future hours — those were yesterday's late orders leaking through. Same naive-local convention is already in src/admin.js's backfill watermarks. Fix: pass ${localDate}T00:00:00 (naive) to fetchOrdersSince. UTC equivalent still surfaced in the response's window.from_iso block for debugging. KV TTL is 60s so cached buggy payloads age out within a minute.
