    • Hourly chart trimmed to the launch window (12 bars, 7am–7pm AEST). 24-bar 0–23 view was hard to read — lots of empty pre-7am noise and overnight tails. Chart now shows the launch window only: 12 bars labelled 7am, 8am, ..., 6pm, drop hours 7am–5pm in No Pong pink, post-drop bar (6pm) in grey. Backend still returns the full 24-bucket payload so we can widen the window later without a Worker redeploy — just a frontend slice change. total_orders_today on the cards continues to count every order since Sydney midnight regardless of the chart slice, so pre-7am stragglers are still in the headline number.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/birthday-launch?refresh=1 and look at orders_per_hour — hours past the current Sydney clock-time should be 0. On the dashboard, the bar chart should have 12 bars from 7am to 6pm.

    v2.2.22 — 19 May 2026 (ShipStation client swapped to v1 — basic auth + API Key/Secret pair)

    • Fix: ShipStation auth scheme corrected. v2.2.21 built the ShipStation client against API v2 (single API key, API-Key header, api.shipstation.com). Melanie confirmed on launch-eve that No Pong's ShipStation account uses v1 instead — HTTP Basic auth with both an API Key and an API Secret, against ssapi.shipstation.com. Without the fix, every ShipStation call would have 401'd on Thursday.
    • What changed in src/shipstation.js. Base URL api.shipstation.com/v2 → ssapi.shipstation.com. API-Key header → Authorization: Basic base64(key:secret). Field names v2 snake_case → v1 camelCase (shipTo.country instead of ship_to.country_code, orderNumber instead of order_number, shipmentItems instead of items). Shipments endpoint now uses shipDateStart/shipDateEnd (YYYY-MM-DD local) with voided=false and includeShipmentItems=true. 401s get a clear "re-run set-shipstation-key.command" message instead of leaking the v1 error body. Public aggregator interface unchanged — the birthday-launch route and the frontend didn't need to change.
    • New secret: SHIPSTATION_API_SECRET. v2.2.21 only needed SHIPSTATION_API_KEY. v1 needs both. Updated commands/set-shipstation-key.command prompts for both (hidden input each time) and pipes each into a separate wrangler secret put so neither value lives in shell history.

    v2.2.21 — 19 May 2026 (New 11th Birthday launch tab — live Woo + ShipStation v2 for Thursday 21 May)

    • New tab: 11th Birthday. Bright-pink button at the end of the AU tab bar, live for Thursday 21 May 2026's hourly-drop launch. Live data, real-time, doesn't touch D1 (cron lag would hurt). Tab activates only when clicked — the page does not poll the launch endpoint on every dashboard load.
    • Hero strip. A countdown to the next hourly drop (7:00, 8:00, ... 17:00 AEST), the next-drop wall-clock time, and the current count of orders awaiting shipment in ShipStation. Countdown ticks every second; outside the 7am–5pm window the countdown points to tomorrow's 7am.
    • WooCommerce zone (live REST). Four cards: All orders today / Orders containing launch products (soap or tin) / Soap units sold all-time (stock tracker — soap soft-launches Wednesday night) / 11th Birthday Tin units sold today. Below them: a per-hour bar chart of all orders today with the 7am–5pm AEST window highlighted in No Pong pink (rest of the day in grey). Launch SKUs: AU-BD-NPS-100 (soap), AU-BD-NBF-35 (tin). Belt-and-braces matching by SKU OR by line-item name containing "Birthday" so a mis-tagged line still gets counted.
    • ShipStation zone (live v2 API). Three cards: Orders awaiting shipment / Orders shipped today / Items shipped today. Below them a flagship alert card — Express / International — still open — with the inline list of order numbers, country, and service code so the fulfilment team knows what to grab first. Cards dim gracefully if the ShipStation key isn't yet configured.
    • Smart auto-refresh. 2-minute refresh for the first 10 minutes after each top-of-hour drop, then 5 minutes until the next drop. Stateless — derived from Sydney-local minutes-past-hour on the wall clock so a missed tick can't break the schedule. Tab is visibility-aware: refresh pauses when you click away to another tab, resumes when you come back. Manual Refresh now button always available.
    • New backend module src/birthday.js + src/shipstation.js. Combined endpoint GET /api/au/birthday-launch returns Woo + ShipStation in one shot. KV-cached at au:birthday-launch:v1 with a 60-second TTL (short enough for freshness, long enough to absorb a thundering herd). Soap lifetime sold pulls from Woo's /products?sku=... total_sales field — no D1 dependency.
    • Secrets. Reuses existing WOO_AU_URL / WOO_AU_KEY / WOO_AU_SECRET. One new secret needed: SHIPSTATION_API_KEY (single API key, ShipStation v2). Without it the ShipStation zone shows "not connected" but the Woo zone still works.
    • Verify after deploy. Click the pink 11th Birthday tab; the countdown should be ticking down to the next top-of-hour, and the Woo cards should populate within a couple of seconds (likely zeros until soap goes live Wednesday night). https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/birthday-launch?refresh=1 exercises the endpoint directly — useful for confirming both upstreams are healthy from the Worker side.

    v2.2.20 — 17 May 2026 (Phase 3b-a — Amazon AU live from D1; all 5 channels real)

    • Phase 3 complete on the read side. Amazon AU now sources from D1 alongside the other 4 channels. The dashboard is no longer dependent on static au-data.js for any channel — every number you see is from a live source (CIN7, Woo D1, Amazon D1). The static fallback path remains in the code for the cold-load case before /api/au/sales completes, but in steady state nothing reads from it.
    • New backend helper getAuAmazonSalesFromD1 in src/cin7.js. Queries amazon_orders for channel totals (orders count + revenue including shipping) and amazon_items for per-SKU breakdown (tins + units + line-item revenue). Returns the same { channelTotal, bySku } shape as getAuWooSalesFromD1 so the merge logic in buildAuSalesPayloadFromD1 stays symmetric.
    • buildAuSalesPayloadFromD1 extended. channels.amz populated from Amazon D1. Per-SKU merge now folds Amazon's bySku alongside Woo's — each skuSales row carries { sku, name, col, woo2, dist, woo, amz, total_tins, revenue }. total_tins sums all 5 channels; revenue sums CIN7 + Woo + Amazon revenue. SKUs that appear in any one channel get a row.
    • Front-end merge simplified. loadLiveAuSales dropped the static-amz fallback completely — no more reading from window.AU_DATA.skuSalesByMonth at merge time. Live data fully owns the per-SKU table. The Sales by Channel table source label for amz now flips to "Amazon · D1 (Fulfilled Shipments backfill)" once the live fetch lands, mirroring the Woo source label introduced in v2.2.18.
    • Run rate now covers all 5 channels. buildIncomingBySku's denominator uses skuSales.total_tins, which now includes Amazon. The Incoming stock by SKU table-hint, Run rate column tooltip, and runRateBasis.note all updated from "CIN7 + Woo (Amazon still pending)" to "all 5 channels — full demand picture". FBA-heavy SKUs (small-pack Originals) will see noticeably higher run rate values and correspondingly lower Days left.
    • Data source — CSV backfill, SP-API cron deferred. Phase 3b-a covers the historical CSV import only. New scripts/import-amazon-au-csv.py parses Seller Central's "Amazon Fulfilled Shipments" TSV; scripts/apply-amazon-au-import-chunked.py pushes to D1 via the chunked --command path (same OAuth workaround as the Woo + CIN7 importers). A sentinel report_jobs row anchors the AU CSV line items so re-runs CASCADE-clean cleanly. SP-API cron extension is Phase 3b-b — needs Amazon Developer Console authorization that hasn't been done yet.
    • SKU mapping. AU Amazon seller SKUs default to identity mapping (e.g. AU-OG-NPO-35 stays as-is). FNSKU-style aliases will need explicit entries in AMZ_SKU_MAP_AU in the importer once we see them in the data — the importer logs unmapped SKUs at the end of each run so we can add them and re-import.
    • KV cache key bumped au:sales:YYYY-MM:v6 → :v7. Payload shape gained channels.amz + each skuSales row gained an amz field.
    • Verify after deploy. Run Commands/import-amazon-au-csv.command with your Seller Central TSV. Then Commands/apply-amazon-au-import-chunked.command to push to D1. Then Commands/verify-amazon-au-import.command to spot-check row counts + unmapped SKU list. Finally hard-refresh the dashboard — Amazon channel cards, chart bars, per-SKU table columns, and the Incoming stock by SKU run rate should all reflect live Amazon data.

    v2.2.19 — 16 May 2026 (Monthly SKU table now sources product names from CIN7 inventory, not sales line items)

    • Most product names on the Monthly SKU Sales table were wrong after v2.2.18. The 35g SKUs that sell via Coles / Woolies / Distributors were showing carton-variant names like "CARTON OF 4 SRT'S - 48 X NO PONG ORIGINAL 35G" instead of the canonical "NO PONG ORIGINAL 35G". AU-OG-NPO-35, AU-LL-BCF-35, AU-OG-BCF-35, AU-FP-VLB-35, AU-B-BS-BCF-35, AU-CL-VLB-35, AU-EF-BCF-35, AU-FF-VLB-35, AU-B-MJ-BCF-35 all hit.
    • Root cause. Backend's per-SKU name comes from MAX(i.name) GROUP BY i.base_sku on cin7_sales_order_items. For SKUs sold in carton form, CIN7's line-item name field is the carton's name (e.g. "CARTON OF 4 SRT'S - 48 X NO PONG ORIGINAL 35G"). When MAX picks alphabetically, "CARTON OF..." beats "NO PONG..." (C < N). This was hidden in earlier versions because the front-end merge used static au-data.js names for April; v2.2.18 stopped using static, exposing the bad names for every month including April.
    • Fix — source names from inventory. The Inventory SKUs tab pulls product names from CIN7's /Stock + /Products endpoints, which return one canonical name per product. New helper _auCanonicalSkuName(sku, fallback) in public/index.html looks up the row's SKU in AU_INVENTORY_DATA and returns the inventory name. Falls back to the row's own name for SKUs not in inventory (monthly club, Re-Use Stickers, anything outside the Finished Products / Bicarb Based categories that's filtered out of inventory). loadLiveAuInventory now also triggers renderAuMonthlySkuTable() when it completes so canonical names appear without a tab switch.
    • Backend payload still has the carton-variant name bug. The /api/au/sales payload's skuSales[].name field still comes from the buggy SQL aggregation. Documented as a known issue. A follow-up backend ship can rebuild the name source from /Stock + /Products the same way buildAuInventoryPayload does. Frontend-only fix here keeps the ship small.
    • Verify after deploy. Hard-refresh, Monthly SKU Sales tab. Top rows like AU-OG-NPO-35 should now read "NO PONG ORIGINAL 35G" instead of "CARTON OF...". Names that already looked good in v2.2.18 (most 85g Woo-only SKUs) might change capitalisation but should still be recognisable since CIN7's master names are the source-of-truth. SKUs not in inventory (monthly club, stickers) keep their previous names.

    v2.2.18 — 16 May 2026 (Woo per-SKU live from D1 across all months; drop Amazon revenue estimate)

    • Per-SKU Monthly SKU Sales table was only showing Woo for April (from static). Spot-audit after v2.2.17 found that Woo columns on the Monthly SKU Sales tab read — for every month except April, even though D1 has the full Woo history Nov 2025 → May 2026. Root cause: v2.2.14 wired Woo at the channel level only (channels.woo on the sales payload), but the per-SKU skuSales array still only carried CIN7 columns (col / woo2 / dist). The front-end merge tried to fill the gap from au-data.js's static snapshot — which only has April. Other months read 0.
    • Backend now serves Woo per-SKU. buildAuSalesPayloadFromD1 in src/cin7.js folds getAuWooSalesFromD1.bySku into the skuSales array. Each row gains a woo field (tin count per base SKU) and revenue per row now includes Woo revenue alongside CIN7. SKUs with Woo sales but no CIN7 channel sales appear as new rows (Woo-only D2C SKUs). KV cache key bumped au:sales:YYYY-MM:v5 → :v6.
    • Front-end merge dropped the Woo+Amazon revenue approximation. Previously loadLiveAuSales approximated woo+amz revenue from static via static.revenue × (woo_amz_tins / total_tins) — that violated the "never fudge data" rule. With Woo now real from D1, the approximation is gone entirely. Amazon TINS still display for April from the static snapshot (real April export), but Amazon revenue now shows blank/0 until Phase 3b lands real SP-API data. This is the honest representation: CIN7 + Woo are real; Amazon is missing and we're transparent about it.
    • Channel total revenue will visibly drop for months that previously included the Woo+Amz approximation. April 2026 will see the biggest delta since the static approximation contributed most there. Per-SKU Woo numbers across non-April months will look new because they weren't being displayed before — check the totals at the bottom of the Monthly SKU Sales table against the channel-level Woo total at the top.
    • Sales by Channel table source label fixed. The Woo row's source column was still reading "Metorik / Woo · static (Phase 3)" even though v2.2.14 had it live. Now correctly reads "Woo · D1 (Metorik backfill + cron)" when the live fetch has landed, mirroring the CIN7 channels' live/static behaviour. Amazon row still reads "static — Phase 3b" because that's still true.
    • Run rate denominator now includes Woo. buildIncomingBySku's total_tins input now sums col + woo2 + dist + woo, so the trailing-3-month run rate on the Incoming stock by SKU table reflects ~95% of true demand (was ~80% on CIN7-only). The runRateBasis.note, the table-hint above the table, and the column tooltip all updated. FBA-heavy SKUs (small-pack Originals especially) will still under-count slightly until Phase 3b.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/sales?month=2026-03&refresh=1 — skuSales rows should now have a woo field with non-zero values. On the dashboard, navigate Monthly SKU Sales to any month other than April — the Woo column should now have data (was blank). Check the table TOTAL row matches the channel-level Woo total card at the top of the Sales by Channel tab. For April 2026: per-SKU revenue per row will be slightly lower than v2.2.17 (the Amazon revenue estimate is gone); total tins per row include the static Amazon tin count for April only.

    v2.2.17 — 16 May 2026 (AU numeric data cells now right-align to match their headers)

    • Numbers didn't sit under their column headings on AU tables. Headers like Current stock / Run rate / Days left / Incoming on the Incoming stock by SKU table (and the new Total units / SKUs / Days until arrival on the Open POs table) were right-aligned per the v2.35 prototype rule, but the data cells defaulted to left-aligned because the base td.num CSS only sets padding + tabular-nums, no alignment. Result: number columns visually drifted left while their headers drifted right, columns of numbers didn't stack against each other, and totals were hard to read at a glance.
    • Fix. One CSS rule in public/index.html: #region-au td.num { text-align: right; }. Mirrors the existing #region-au th.num { text-align: right; } rule one line above so AU's numeric headers and cells share the same alignment. NA tables aren't affected (they're outside #region-au and use class="num right" explicitly when needed).
    • Diagnostic confirms v2.2.16's fallback chain is doing its job. Aggregate diagnostic from /api/au/pos/debug shows estimatedDeliveryDate_set: 35 out of 36 open POs — the v2.2.16 fallback was the right call. Your team's PO data entry uses estimatedDeliveryDate, not deliveryDate. One PO has no date in any field (will show — on the table); two have invoiceDate populated as an extra signal we don't currently use.

    v2.2.16 — 16 May 2026 (PO expected-date fallback chain + diagnostic for date-field coverage)

    • Expected date + Days until arrival columns were blank for every open PO. Symptom showed up immediately after v2.2.15 deployed: the new CA-style table rendered the columns correctly but the data behind them was always null. Root cause: fetchAllPurchaseOrders in src/cin7.js only requested CIN7's deliveryDate field, and live AU data shows deliveryDate is never populated — No Pong's PO-entry process clearly uses a different date field (or none at all). CIN7's PurchaseOrder model has five candidate date fields besides deliveryDate.
    • Fix: fallback chain through all CIN7 date fields. fetchAllPurchaseOrders now also requests estimatedDeliveryDate, estimatedArrivalDate, supplierAcceptanceDate, port, and customFields. shapePoRows's expected_date is now derived: deliveryDate → estimatedDeliveryDate → estimatedArrivalDate → supplierAcceptanceDate → null. New field expected_date_source records which field actually supplied the value (for debugging + future tooltip). All raw date fields are also surfaced on the row payload (delivery_date, estimated_delivery_date, estimated_arrival_date, supplier_acceptance_date, invoice_date) so a future ship can render any of them on hover if useful.
    • Diagnostic endpoint extended. /api/au/pos/debug (aggregate mode) now returns openDateFieldCoverage — counts how many of the 36 currently-open POs have each date field populated (deliveryDate / estimatedDeliveryDate / estimatedArrivalDate / supplierAcceptanceDate / invoiceDate / customFields), plus a no_date_at_all count for POs with no date in any field. Tells us empirically which field your team uses. ?ref=<PO> mode now returns a full dateFields block plus the raw customFields on the PO, so we can see what's actually stored.
    • KV cache key bumped au:pos:v3 → :v4. Payload shape changed (new expected_date_source + raw date fields), so v3 caches need to drop cleanly.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/pos?refresh=1 — if any open PO has expected_date populated, the dashboard Expected date column will start showing dates. Then hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/pos/debug and look at openDateFieldCoverage:
      • If estimatedDeliveryDate_set or estimatedArrivalDate_set is high, the fallback is now picking those up — nothing else to do.
      • If everything is at zero and no_date_at_all: 36, then CIN7 simply doesn't have expected-date data for AU POs and someone needs to start entering it in the CIN7 UI (or we add a custom-field lookup).
      • If customFields_set is high, the data is in a CIN7 custom field — next ship would map the custom-field key to expected_date.

    v2.2.15 — 16 May 2026 (PO filter rework + PO table restyle to match CA columns)

    • PO filter — receive-but-never-Completed POs were leaking through. v2.2.14's 'void' hotfix dropped 13 truly-cancelled rows, but 143 remained — all APPROVED, many 6+ months old (PO-AU-T88 from Sept 2025, PO-313 from Oct, PO-4806 from Nov). Root cause: isOpenPo only checked status + completedDate, both of which require a human to manually mark the PO Completed in CIN7's UI. CIN7 doesn't auto-flip the status when stock is received; many orgs (including No Pong) just leave old POs sitting APPROVED indefinitely once the goods arrive.
    • Fix — use CIN7's auto-set closure signals + line-item rollup fallback. fetchAllPurchaseOrders in src/cin7.js now requests fullyReceivedDate, cancellationDate, isVoid, stage, and modifiedDate alongside the existing fields. isOpenPo drops a PO if any of those closure signals are set, AND falls back to a line-item rollup (every non-zero-qty line has qtyShipped >= qty) for legacy POs CIN7 didn't backfill fullyReceivedDate on. New helper: isFullyReceivedByLines. KV cache key bumped au:pos:v2 → :v3.
    • New diagnostic endpoint: GET /api/au/pos/debug. Aggregate mode returns frequency tables of CIN7 status values, counts of each closure signal across all POs, and a sample of the oldest APPROVED POs that the new filter drops (so you can confirm the right rows are getting filtered). Pass ?ref=PO-AU-T88 to dump one specific PO with raw fields plus line-by-line received-vs-ordered breakdown. Mirrors the /sales/debug pattern. Use this whenever a PO behaves unexpectedly — faster than re-deploying with extra logging.
    • PO table restyled to match the CA dashboard's PO tab. Columns are now: PO # (with Type badge inline) · Supplier · Expected date · Days until arrival · Total units · SKUs · Status. The Type column is removed (badge folded into the PO # cell to retain the info without spending a column on it); the Items column is removed (full SKU list still shows on row hover); the ETD column is split into Expected date + Days until arrival to mirror CA. Status column shows a derived Overdue / Arriving soon / Confirmed badge with CIN7's raw status (APPROVED / DRAFT) as a small sub-label.
    • Backend supports the new columns. shapePoRows in src/cin7.js now emits sku_count (distinct SKUs excluding freight / KittingFee / discount lines) plus the new closure-signal fields (fully_received_date, cancellation_date, is_void, stage). The Type and Items info is still available in the payload — the Type column is just no longer rendered as a separate column.
    • CSV export refreshed to match. downloadAuPosCSV emits the new column order: PO # / Supplier / Contact / Expected date / Days until arrival / Total units / SKUs / CIN7 status / Derived status / Type / Items. Type + Items are kept on the right edge of the spreadsheet for drill-down convenience even though they're not in the on-page table.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit /api/au/pos?refresh=1 — PO count should drop noticeably from 143 (was after v2.2.14). Spot-check by ref: /api/au/pos/debug?ref=PO-AU-T88 — should now report isOpenByCurrentFilter: false with the line-item rollup showing all lines fully received. For an aggregate view of how many POs changed bucket: /api/au/pos/debug returns delta_v2_2_14_to_v2_2_15 with the count.

    v2.2.14 — 15 May 2026 (PO cache bust + Woo channel read-side cutover from D1)

    • POs — VOID statuses were leaking through the open-filter. Right after the v2.2.13 deploy the AU Purchase Orders tab showed every PO instead of just open ones — not a cache issue as I first guessed, but an actual filter bug. fetchAllPurchaseOrders' isOpenPo helper closed on a set of {'completed', 'voided', 'cancelled', 'closed'} (the v2.35 comment claimed CIN7 returned Voided as the status), but Omni's /PurchaseOrders endpoint actually returns VOID (4 chars), not VOIDED/Voided (6 chars). Lowercased, 'void' !== 'voided', so 13+ VOID POs slipped through and surfaced. Fix: added 'void' to CLOSED_PO_STATUSES alongside 'voided' so the filter is resilient to either capitalisation. Also bumped the au:pos:v1 → au:pos:v2 KV key so the buggy cache snapshot doesn't keep serving for the next 15 min after deploy.
    • Woo channel now reads from D1. Phase 3a's read-side cutover lands the second half of the work v2.2.13 set up: D1 had the data, but /api/au/sales wasn't reading from it for the Woo channel. New helper getAuWooSalesFromD1 in src/cin7.js queries orders + order_items for market='AU', status IN ('completed','processing') in the named month, applies AU SKU normalisation in JS (WC-AU-...-WC × 54, AU-CTN-...-48 × 48, AU-SRT-...x12 × 12, etc.) to compute tins, and rolls up to channel totals + per-base-SKU.
    • Payload shape change. /api/au/sales's channels object now includes a woo key with { units, tins, revenue, orders } alongside the existing col / woo2 / dist. The Amazon channel (amz) is still absent — that's Phase 3b. KV cache key bumped au:sales:YYYY-MM:v4 → :v5 so v4 caches (no woo key) get invalidated cleanly.
    • Frontend now picks Woo from API instead of static. loadLiveAuSales in public/index.html extends its channel-merge loop from ['col', 'woo2', 'dist'] to include 'woo', so AU_SALES_BY_MONTH / AU_ORDERS_BY_MONTH / AU_TINS_BY_MONTH all pull Woo from D1 once the live fetch lands. Until the fetch completes for a given month the chart and channel cards still render from static au-data.js (which now only covers April 2026), same lazy-load pattern as the CIN7 channels.
    • April 2026 numbers will shift visibly. Static au-data.js Woo row for April: 18,562 "orders" / $313,417 revenue / 28,375 units / 38,223 tins. D1 returns: 8,980 distinct orders / $398,201 revenue / units + tins per the live normalisation. The 2x "orders" gap appears to be the static counting line items as orders (8,980 × 2.07 line-items-per-order ≈ 18,600); the ~$85K revenue gap is likely the static's refund-netting via Metorik's "Total After Refunds" column where this ship uses raw "Total" to match NA Woo's gross semantics. D1 numbers are the new truth post-ship.
    • Per-SKU Woo breakdown deferred to v2.2.15. The per-SKU table on the AU Monthly SKU tab still merges Woo per-SKU from static au-data.js (only April 2026 has those rows). Channel-level woo total won't exactly sum to the per-SKU woo column until v2.2.15 wires Woo per-SKU from D1 too — chosen split because the per-SKU merge in loadLiveAuSales has approximation logic (woo+amz revenue apportioned by tin share) that needs careful rework when Woo lands a real per-channel revenue number.
    • Verify after deploy. Hit /api/au/pos?refresh=1 — should return open POs only (zero rows where completedDate is set). Then hit /api/au/sales?month=2026-04&refresh=1 — channels.woo should now be present with non-zero tins + revenue + orders. On the AU dashboard, the Woo channel card and chart bar should reflect the higher revenue number once the live fetch lands.

    v2.2.13 — 15 May 2026 (Phase 3a — Woo AU Metorik backfill imported + cron extended to AU)

    • D1 now holds the full AU WooCommerce history Nov 2025 → May 2026. Same shape as the CIN7 v2.36 CSV pivot: one-shot Metorik export → scripts/import-woo-au-csv.py → chunked --command apply (bypasses the --file OAuth quirk). 70,525 orders + 166,268 line items landed in the existing orders + order_items tables with market='AU' — no new migration needed (migration 0001 designed for it; 0003 added the (market, local_date) indexes). Idempotent; INSERT OR REPLACE on orders + DELETE-then-INSERT scoped per order_id on items.
    • Cron extended to AU. runWooCronSync in src/index.js now pushes 'AU' onto its markets list when WOO_AU_URL + WOO_AU_KEY + WOO_AU_SECRET secrets are set; without them the branch silently skips (same gating pattern CA/US use). storeFromEnv in src/admin.js gets an AU entry with currency: 'AUD'; the admin POST /backfill + GET /reconcile routes' market validation lists also accept 'AU' for the Woo source.
    • Watermark starts at the CSV cutoff. The import seeds sync_state(source='woo', market='AU') with watermark='2026-05-12T13:02:35' (the latest Order Created At in the Metorik export). The cron's strict after=<watermark> picks up only orders created after that timestamp, so the cron and CSV import don't overlap.
    • Read-side untouched in this ship. /api/au/sales still serves CIN7 channels only (col / woo2 / dist + CIN7 refunds). The Woo channel + Amazon channel on the AU dashboard continue to come from the static au-data.js snapshot until the next ship folds D1's orders WHERE market='AU' into the AU sales payload — that's a clean separate diff for the read-side cutover.
    • April 2026 parity findings. Static au-data.js Woo row: 18,562 “orders” / $313,417 revenue. D1 imported: 8,980 distinct orders / $398,200 revenue (completed + processing). The 2x “orders” gap is almost exactly the ~2.07 line-items-per-order average — suggests the static count was line items mislabelled as orders. The revenue gap (~$85K) is likely refund netting via the Metorik “Total After Refunds” field; this import uses raw Total to match NA Woo's gross-total semantics. Top SKUs match what we'd expect (AU-OG-NPO-85 leads at 3,169 units in April).
    • Verify after deploy. Wait one cron tick (~15 min), then run Commands/verify-woo-au-import.command and check the sync_state(source='woo', market='AU') row — last_synced_at should now be a recent timestamp (was NULL immediately after the import). If new AU orders have been placed in WooCommerce since the CSV cutoff, the cron should pick them up; if not, the watermark stays put and that's fine.
    • Refund Woo/CIN7 dedupe still parked. The AU dashboard's existing refund double-count (Woo refunds netted into Woo channel revenue + same refunds logged as CIN7 credit notes) needs both sides in D1 to dedupe cleanly — that's the next ship after this one (and Phase 3b Amazon AU).

    v2.2.12 — 15 May 2026 (Sales tab — chart shows fixed trailing-6-month window; matches NA pattern)

    • Bug. Clicking January in the AU sales month picker collapsed the "Revenue by channel" chart to a single bar (just January) instead of showing 6 months. Two interacting causes: auTrailingSixMonths() anchored to the SELECTED month, and AU_MONTHS only extended back to January 2026 — so trailing-6-from-January walked off the start of the array and returned just ['2026-01'].
    • Fix. Chart now always shows the trailing 6 months ending at "now" (the latest entry in AU_MONTHS), regardless of which month is selected in the picker. Matches the NA dashboard's fixed-window behaviour. Selecting a historical month still updates the table and per-channel KPI cards (those remain anchored to the selected month for drill-down), but the chart is a fixed reference frame.
    • AU_MONTHS now extends back to November 2025 (was January 2026) so the trailing-6 window has runway. D1 has the full Nov 2025 → now history after the v2.36 CSV import + cron, so the picker can navigate through any of those months.
    • Chart title reads "6 months ending [latest month]" always — doesn't change as you click around the picker. Previously the title rewrote to match the selected month, which was misleading once the chart's anchor stopped tracking the selection.

    v2.2.11 — 15 May 2026 (Phase C — /api/au/sales reads from D1; full historical months unblocked)

    • Read-side cutover. /api/au/sales now serves from D1 instead of fetching CIN7 directly on every cold-cache miss. New builder buildAuSalesPayloadFromD1 in src/cin7.js runs 4 indexed SQL queries (sales channels aggregate + per-SKU rollup + refunds aggregate + refunds per-SKU) and produces the same payload shape the previous CIN7-driven builder returned. Source field changes from 'cin7-live' to 'cin7-d1'.
    • Why. Live CIN7 fetches were ~50 paginated calls per cold-cache miss × 5 concurrent month tabs on the front-end pre-fetch — the v2.35.6 KV-key-bump incident showed this could trip CIN7's 3-call/sec rate limit. D1 reads are 4 SQL queries on indexed columns (sub-second) and don't touch CIN7 at all. The cron + the v2.36 CSV import keep D1 current.
    • Cache key bumped. au:sales:YYYY-MM:v3 → :v4 — the source field changed and the counts shape simplified, so v3 caches are invalidated cleanly.
    • What's now visible on the dashboard. All months Nov 2025 → May 2026 for Coles / Woolies / Distributors, not just April. (Woo + Amazon channels still come from the static au-data.js snapshot pending Phase 3.) April 2026 parity exact: Coles 78,336 / Woolies 73,728 tins. May 2026 distributor numbers will run higher than the legacy static figure because cron-imported rows (post-2026-05-12) classify dist by company substring without the channel filter the CSV import applies — per the brief, this is the new truth, not a regression.
    • Legacy CIN7 path retained for reference. The old buildAuSalesPayload function (which fetched /SalesOrders + /CreditNotes on demand) is left in src/cin7.js unused for now. Future cleanup pass can remove it once the D1 path has soak-tested for a week or two.
    • Phase A invariant retired. Phase A (v2.2.7+) made the cron invisible to the dashboard. Phase C ends that — the dashboard is now reading from D1, so the cron's reliability is now load-bearing. The v2.2.10 cron simplification + CSV-import reconcile path together cover the failure modes.

    v2.2.10 — 15 May 2026 (Phase A simplification — pivot to CSV-driven backfill, drop composite cursor)

    • Pivot from incremental backfill to CSV import. v2.2.7–v2.2.9 chased an incremental-sync watermark cursor through CIN7's API; v2.2.9 surfaced the dealbreaker — CIN7's where clause silently drops Id filters, so the composite (modified_date, id) cursor reduces to a simple ModifiedDate >= watermark at the API layer and stalls forever on the Nov 8 13:00:06Z cluster of 250+ records. New backfill mechanism: scripts/import-cin7-csv.py parses CIN7's full Sales Orders OrdersExport CSV (one denormalised row per line item), applies the cron's attributeCin7Order classifier with a CSV-only Channel='Backend' refinement on dist, and writes to D1 via INSERT OR REPLACE. Companion scripts/apply-cin7-import-chunked.py bypasses wrangler's --file path (which routes to the D1 /import endpoint that 10000s on OAuth tokens) by pushing each statement via --command.
    • Cron simplification. Both runCin7SalesOrdersChunk and runCin7CreditNotesChunk drop the composite cursor entirely. New strategy: WHERE ModifiedDate > '${watermark}' (strict >, not >=) + advance watermark to MAX(modifiedDate) seen. Trade-off: if >250 records ever share a single modifiedDate in the future, the cluster members that don't fit on the first page get skipped — recoverable via a CSV re-import of the affected date range. In steady state (a few new records per day) clusters are vanishingly rare. buildCompositeWhere and sortByCompositeKey helpers removed; sync_state.watermark_id column kept in schema but no longer read or written.
    • April 2026 parity verified on prod. Post-CSV-import D1 query returns Coles 78,336 / Woolies 73,728 / Distributors 1,632 tins for April — exact match against the legacy static-side numbers. Per-month coverage Nov 2025 → May 2026 fully populated. The cron has self-healed since the watermark advanced past the Nov 8 cluster (post-import row counts: 2,830 sales orders / 6,255 line items, watermark at 2026-05-15T00:59:16Z).
    • Phase A invariant still holds. Dashboard reads (/api/au/sales, /api/au/pos, /api/au/inventory) are untouched. Cron stays invisible to the user. KV cache key remains au:sales:YYYY-MM:v3. Phase C (read-side cutover to D1) is now unblocked.

    v2.2.9 — 12 May 2026 (Phase A hotfix #2 — restore order:ModifiedDate ASC; reset sales-orders watermark to recover 58K skipped records)

    • The bug. v2.2.8's composite-cursor fix worked literally (the cluster stall went away), but I had also removed order: 'ModifiedDate ASC' from the CIN7 fetch call thinking it wasn't being honored. Without that, CIN7 returned the 250-row batch in its default order (probably by Id), the batch had a wide modified_date spread (Nov 2025 through May 2026), and my code took MAX(modified_date) as the new watermark — jumping the cursor 6 months forward in a single tick and skipping roughly 58,000 records in between. The admin backfill confirmed it: after the jump, only 15 more records existed past the cursor, system thinks it's "caught up".
    • The fix — restore the order param. Both runCin7SalesOrdersChunk and runCin7CreditNotesChunk now pass order: 'ModifiedDate ASC' back to CIN7. With ascending order, the first 250 records by (modified_date, id) are returned contiguously, and advancing to the last one's (mod_date, id) walks forward without skipping. v2.2.7's stall behaviour was actually evidence that CIN7 DOES honor the order param — I misread it as evidence that it didn't.
    • Recovery — reset the sales-orders watermark. Migration 0007 (or the reset-cin7-watermark.command file in Commands/) sets sync_state.watermark back to 2025-11-08T13:00:06Z and watermark_id = 0 for source cin7_sales_orders. The cron (or admin backfill) then re-walks the Nov 8 → May 12 history, this time correctly. Credit notes don't need reset — they reached steady state legitimately at 425 records (CIN7's full credit-note history is small).
    • Lesson learned. Removing a working param because I misdiagnosed it as broken is the kind of mistake an extra sanity-check would catch. For Phase C onward I'll keep CIN7's order param explicit on every fetch and verify behaviour with a small integration test before deploy.
    • Verify after deploy + watermark reset. Wait one cron tick (~15 min), then run Commands/check-cin7-sync.command. Expect: cin7_sales_orders row count growing past 1,547 in steady chunks of 250; watermark_after advancing slowly (not jumping); watermark_id growing as the cluster gets walked. To fast-forward backfill: hit POST /api/admin/cin7-backfill?source=sales-orders via the browser DevTools fetch trick — with ordering correct each call should ingest ~7,500 records and walk a few weeks forward.
    • Phase A invariant still holds. Dashboard reads (/api/au/sales, /api/au/pos, /api/au/inventory) are untouched. Cron stays invisible to the user. KV cache key remains au:sales:YYYY-MM:v3.

    v2.2.8 — 11 May 2026 (Phase A hotfix — composite (modified_date, id) cursor to unstick watermark stall)

    • The bug. v2.2.7's first cron tick on prod surfaced exactly the edge case the code comments flagged: CIN7 has 250+ sales-order rows with the EXACT same modifiedDate of 2025-11-08T13:00:06Z (most likely a bulk import or bulk edit). The WHERE ModifiedDate >= watermark filter + watermarkAfter = MAX(modified_date) advance keeps re-fetching the same 250 rows forever because MAX always equals the watermark when 250+ rows share that timestamp. Admin-backfill loop confirmed: 10 chunks in a row, identical batches, zero forward progress.
    • The fix — composite cursor. Migration 0006_cin7_composite_cursor.sql adds a watermark_id column to sync_state (default 0, applies to all existing rows automatically). The cron's WHERE clause becomes (ModifiedDate > 'w_mod') OR (ModifiedDate = 'w_mod' AND Id > w_id) — "give me records past this timestamp, OR records at the same timestamp with a higher Id." Monotonic forward progress through ties guaranteed.
    • Batch sort + advance. Each chunk now sorts the returned batch by (modified_date, id) in JS before processing, so the LAST sorted record carries the highest composite cursor we just persisted. That tuple becomes the new (watermark, watermark_id), guarded against accidental backwards moves.
    • Backfill loop safety. The more flag returned from each chunk now requires BOTH a full page AND the watermark to have actually advanced. If watermark didn't move on a full page (the pathological case we just hit), more=false so the backfill loop bails instead of spinning. Without this, the admin backfill could loop 30 times burning CIN7 calls for zero progress.
    • Field-name caveat. The composite WHERE clause uses Id (PascalCase) to match the ModifiedDate/CreatedDate convention. If CIN7 rejects the casing the next cron tick will surface an error in sync_logs (look for status='error' rows on the cin7_sales_orders / cin7_credit_notes sources) and we'll iterate (likely ID uppercase).
    • Phase A invariant still holds. Dashboard reads (/api/au/sales, /api/au/pos, /api/au/inventory) are untouched. Cron stays invisible to the user. Phase C is unblocked by this fix — without it, the D1 tables would never have a complete dataset to read from.
    • Verify after deploy + migration apply. Wait one cron tick (~15 min), then in the D1 console: SELECT id, run_at, source, orders_added AS rows, watermark_after, status FROM sync_logs WHERE source LIKE 'cin7_%' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 10; — the watermark_after column should now ADVANCE past 2025-11-08T13:00:06Z on subsequent ticks. If you want to fast-forward backfill (recommended), hit POST https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/admin/cin7-backfill a few times via the browser DevTools fetch trick; with the composite cursor, each call should walk hundreds of thousands of records forward instead of stalling.

    v2.2.7 — 11 May 2026 (v2.36 Phase A — CIN7 → D1+cron architecture, invisible to the dashboard)

    • Phase A of the v2.36 architecture port lands. CIN7 SalesOrders + CreditNotes now sync to D1 in the background on the existing 15-min cron. Dashboard behaviour is unchanged in this release — /api/au/sales and /api/au/pos still read directly from CIN7. The point of Phase A is to start populating D1 so the read-side cutover in Phase C can be verified against a full local dataset. Full design lives in Melanie's Context/next-session-brief-v2.36-au-d1-port.md.
    • Why the architecture port exists. AU dashboard reads CIN7 on demand on every cold cache (~50 paginated calls per month tab). NA solved the same problem in v2.31 with a D1+cron architecture — the v2.35.6 KV-key-bump incident (5 concurrent month fetches into CIN7's 3/sec rate limit) was the predictable consequence of that gap. Phase C cuts dashboard latency from 5–15s to under 500ms and drops CIN7 dashboard-driven traffic to zero.
    • New D1 tables. Migration 0005_cin7.sql adds cin7_sales_orders + cin7_sales_order_items + cin7_credit_notes + cin7_credit_note_items. The *_items tables precompute tins at write time (encoding the v2.35.4 Alt UOM rule once, at the sync layer); credit-note items also precompute revenue_signed = -ABS(total). Read queries become simple SUM(tins) / SUM(revenue_signed).
    • Watermark on ModifiedDate. New rows in sync_state (source: 'cin7_sales_orders', 'cin7_credit_notes') track the latest modifiedDate we've persisted. Captures retroactive edits too — e.g. a DRAFT credit note promoted to APPROVED will be re-fetched on the next tick and the row's status updated.
    • Cron load is tiny. 1 page (≤250 rows) per endpoint per tick × 2 endpoints × 4 ticks/hour = 8 CIN7 calls/hour. Sits well under all three of CIN7's rate caps (3/sec, 60/min, 5000/day).
    • Soft-delete VOID / DRAFT. VOID and DRAFT rows are written to D1 with their status preserved (rather than dropped), so the line items remain auditable and a future promotion (DRAFT → APPROVED) propagates via the next ModifiedDate-watermarked fetch. Phase C read queries filter them out.
    • Admin backfill endpoint. POST /api/admin/cin7-backfill?source=sales-orders|credit-notes|both runs the same chunk functions cron does, in a loop, until either caught up or a ~25s wall-clock budget hits. Re-invoke to resume (watermark state is durable). Useful for the initial backfill from epoch and as a recovery tool if D1 ever needs rebuilding.
    • What ships next. Phase B (this PR's admin endpoint is technically the Phase B work, bundled in). Phase C: switch /api/au/sales to read from D1, verify April 2026 numbers match exactly (Coles 78,336 / Woolies 73,728 / Distributors 11,868 / refunds within v2.35.6's −181 tins / −$73,445), then bump the KV key to au:sales:YYYY-MM:v4. Phase D: /api/au/pos same pattern. Phase E: cleanup (drop the old fetch helpers, disable the front-end pre-fetch).
    • Verify after deploy. Watch sync_logs for source IN ('cin7_sales_orders', 'cin7_credit_notes') entries via npx wrangler d1 execute logistics-db --remote --command="SELECT source, action, orders_added AS rows, items_added AS items, watermark_after, status FROM sync_logs WHERE source LIKE 'cin7_%' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20". Expect status='ok', growing watermark_after, non-zero rows for the first few ticks. If you want a faster backfill, hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/admin/cin7-backfill (POST). After ~3h of cron ticks the D1 tables should hold AU's full CIN7 history.
    • Hidden risk to watch. CIN7's ModifiedDate may not be populated on all record types — if the first cron tick logs rows=0 for credit notes despite known APPROVED credit notes existing, fall back to a CreatedDate watermark and arrange a periodic re-sync. See brief §8 for details.

    v2.2.6 — 11 May 2026 (Refund-data correction: exclude code:196 placeholder + DRAFT credit notes)

    • Two CIN7 quirks were inflating April refunds. The first live /api/au/sales response with refunds populated came back at −181.71 tins / −$100,519 / 73 orders vs the static April snapshot of −110 tins / −$71,317 / 18 orders. Two issues, both fixed here:
      • Non-AU placeholder line code "196" was being treated as a real SKU. Two Woolworths reconciliation credit notes (CRN-72795 + CRN-72796, member 21 / Luke Driver / Better Brands Group) carry a line with code: "196", name: "Credit Note", styleCode: "Credit Note", qty: -0.1775 (a fractional ratio, not a tin count), and unitPrice in the $13K–$152K range. v2.35.5's loop accepted any non-empty code as a SKU and rolled it up under the literal key "196", contributing −$98,450 of phantom “SKU” sales + −0.71 fractional tins. New isAuSkuCode(code) helper enforces the real AU pattern (/^(?:WC-|D-)?AU-/i); credit-note lines that don't match are now treated like empty-code “Amount” lines — revenue contributes (the total is still a real refund amount), tins and per-SKU rollup do not.
      • DRAFT credit notes were being aggregated alongside APPROVED ones. The April dump contained one DRAFT credit note (CRN-72795, the larger of the two Woolworths reconciliations at −$29,781) which alone explains nearly all of the revenue overage vs static. DRAFT means “not yet finalized in CIN7” — pending, not booked. The static side excluded DRAFT implicitly (the pivot CSV only contained posted documents). Status filter now drops DRAFT alongside the existing VOID/VOIDED/CANCELLED.
    • Counts field renamed for accuracy. creditNotesVoidsDropped is now creditNotesNonFinalDropped since it covers DRAFT too.
    • KV cache key bumped au:sales:YYYY-MM:v2 → :v3. Refund tins and revenue numbers shift with this fix, so v2 caches with inflated refunds get invalidated automatically. ?refresh=1 not needed.
    • Verify after deploy: hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/sales?month=2026-04&refresh=1. Expected April refunds: ~−110 tins (clean integer, no fractional decimal) / ~−$71K revenue / ~17–18 orders. The refunds.sku_lines object should NOT contain a key called "196".

    v2.2.5 — 11 May 2026 (Refunds via /CreditNotes + NA changelog cleanup)

    • Refunds row now populated from CIN7 /CreditNotes. v2.35.3 attribution returned 0 refunds for live months because CIN7 Omni v1 splits credit notes onto a separate /CreditNotes endpoint that we weren't fetching. The static side combined sales + credit notes via the pivot CSV; the API splits them. New fetchCreditNotesForMonth(env, month) helper in src/cin7.js mirrors the fetchSalesOrdersForMonth shape (paginated via cin7FetchAll, where=CreatedDate… filter for the named month). buildAuSalesPayload now fetches both endpoints serially — never Promise.all'd, per the 3/sec rate-limit rule — and aggregates credit-note line items into the existing refunds object as a single global negative pool.
    • Same v2.35.4 line-item rules apply to credit notes. Skip child lines (parentId > 0) to avoid bundle double-counting; conditional multiplier on Alt UOM lines (uomSize > 1 ? qty : qty * mult) so wholesale carton refunds don't 54×-over-count. normalizeAuSku rolls credit-note SKUs up to base SKUs the same way sales do, so a refund on WC-AU-OG-NPO-35 shows up against the AU-OG-NPO-35 base on the per-SKU breakdown.
    • Empty-code “Amount” refund lines counted toward revenue but not tins. Diagnostic dump confirmed ~62 of 73 April credit notes contain at least one line with code: "" — these are $-only adjustments (Amazon GST corrections, shipping refunds, MOTO discount, etc.) where no product was actually returned. The naive skip-if-no-code rule from the sales-orders loop would have dropped roughly $60K+ of legitimate April refund revenue. Fixed by splitting the rule: revenue contributes from every line; tin counter and per-SKU rollup only fire when a SKU code is present. Order count accumulates only on lines that contribute either revenue or tin movement, so weird zero-zero placeholder lines don't inflate the refund order count.
    • Refunds always stored as NEGATIVE values. Mirrors what the static side does (tins: -110, revenue: -71317 for April 2026) so the “Less refunds” row on the Sales by Channel table renders consistently regardless of whether CIN7 returns credit-note qty/total as positive or pre-negated. Implementation uses -Math.abs() for robustness against either sign convention.
    • Single global refund pool, not per-channel attribution. Matches the static side's behaviour (one negative row across all channels). Per-channel refund attribution (Coles refund / Woolies refund / dist refund via the same attributeCin7Order matcher) is parked as a Phase 3 polish item once Cloud-channel data lands.
    • Diagnostic variant added: GET /api/au/sales/debug?type=credit-notes. Dumps a sample of raw /CreditNotes rows + frequency tables of status / company values + parent/child + Alt UOM line counts. First thing to hit after deploy — if it returns 401/403 (“CIN7 CreditNotes: 401 Forbidden”), the connection key needs to be regenerated in CIN7 and the new value pasted into the Cloudflare CIN7_CONNECTION_KEY secret. Granting the Read CreditNotes permission alone doesn't propagate to existing keys.
    • KV cache key bumped au:sales:YYYY-MM:v1 → :v2. Forces invalidation of v1 caches that have refunds: 0 for live months; the next /api/au/sales hit re-fetches with refunds populated. ?refresh=1 is unnecessary — the key change does it automatically.
    • Counts breakdown extended in the response. The counts field now also reports creditNotesFetched, creditNotesVoidsDropped, and creditNotesConsidered alongside the existing sales counts, so the diagnostic story is in the response itself.
    • NA changelog cleanup (cosmetic). Removed the inadvertent v2.35 + v2.35.1 entries from the NA changelog panel — those were AU-only Phase 2b changes that shouldn't have surfaced on the NA panel per the per-region versioning rule. NA version-toggle reverted from v2.35.1 → v2.35.2 with a single cleanup entry; NA dashboard behaviour itself is unchanged since v2.34.2.
    • Verify after deploy: first hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/sales/debug?type=credit-notes&month=2026-04 — if it returns rows, the connection key has the new permission. Then hit https://logistics.apps.nopong.com/api/au/sales?month=2026-04&refresh=1 — expected April 2026 refunds: ~−110 tins / ~−$71,317 / 18 orders (matches static).

    v2.2.4 — 11 May 2026 (CIN7 line-item double-counting fix)

    • Two compounding line-item bugs fixed. v2.35.3 attribution worked perfectly for Coles + Woolies (78,336 / 73,728 tins — exact static match), but distributors came back at 330,913 tins / 302 orders vs static's 1,648 tins / 2 orders (200× over-count). Tracking the inflation back to the line-item loop revealed two CIN7 quirks the original code didn't handle:
      • Alt UOM lines (WC- wholesale cartons). When a line has uomSize > 1 (e.g. WC-AU-OG-NPO-35 with uomSize: 54), CIN7 reports qty already in BASE units (tins), not in alt-UOM units (cartons). The original code applied normalizeAuSku's ×54 multiplier on top, 54×-over-counting every wholesale carton line. Fix: tins = uomSize > 1 ? qty : qty * mult.
      • Bundled product children. When CIN7 sells a bundled product like AU-CTN-SRT-MIX-72-V3 ("6-pack of trays"), it generates a parent line for the bundle PLUS auto-generated child lines for each constituent SKU. Children have parentId pointing at the parent. The original code summed both, double-counting tins. Fix: skip child lines (parentId > 0) — the parent's multiplier already represents the children's tin volume.
      Both bugs only affected wholesale orders with carton/Alt-UOM patterns (Priceline / Live Wise / etc.) — which is why Coles + Woolies EDI orders (which use standalone AU-CTN-...-48 SKUs) matched static exactly even before the fix.
    • Verified arithmetic. Reproduced the Priceline order from the v2.35.2 diagnostic dump as a synthetic test: parent AU-CTN-SRT-MIX-72-V3 (qty 1 → 72 tins) + 2 wholesale cartons (54 tins each) + 12 child lines (skipped) = 180 tins. Matches manual calculation. Woolies subset (3 carton SKUs at qty 240/144/96 with uomSize=1) = 23,040 tins, also matches.
    • Distributor count will still be higher than static (~50–100 orders / ~10–30k tins instead of 2 orders / 1,648 tins). That gap is the broader attribution rule from v2.35.3 surfacing wholesale orders the static "Backend channel only" filter missed — no longer the line-item bug.

    v2.2.3 — 11 May 2026 (CIN7 attribution rewritten for real Omni v1 shape)

    • Sales attribution rewritten. v2.35 / v2.35.1 attributed only 6 orders out of 10,425 because the original rules from build_data.py:attribute_cin7() assumed CIN7 Omni's /SalesOrders would expose channel + posRegister fields like the static pivot CSV does. The v2.35.2 diagnostic confirmed those fields are empty for every order on the live API. Attribution now inspects the company field (which holds the shipping-address company name) with substring matching against known retailer / parent-co / DC name patterns:
      • Substring "woolworths" → Woolworths (catches "WOOLWORTHS - Moorebank - NDC" and any other Woolies warehouse name)
      • Exact "Coles" OR substring "grocery holdings" (Coles parent company) OR known DC names like "Kemps Creek" → Coles
      • Substring "amazon" → null (Amazon SP-API mirror — Phase 3)
      • Empty company OR "[Redacted]" → null (likely Woo retail mirror — Phase 3)
      • Personal markers (Mr / Mrs / Ms / Private) → null (individual retail orders)
      • Anything else with a real company name → Distributors (broader than the static "Backend channel only" filter, but more honest — static was under-counting wholesale)
    • VOID-status orders are now filtered out before attribution. v2.35 was counting them as sales, including a duplicate of one of the big Woolworths orders. April 2026 had 16 VOID orders out of 10,425.
    • Counts breakdown surfaced in the response. The counts field now reports ordersFetched, voidsDropped, ordersConsidered, attributedRefs, skuRows — so the diagnostic story is in the response itself, not just on the debug endpoint.
    • Refunds still empty — known limitation. CIN7 Omni v1's /SalesOrders doesn't include credit notes / refunds. Real refunds live on /CreditNotes (separate endpoint). The static side combined both via the pivot report; the API splits them. /CreditNotes fetching lands in a follow-up — until then, the refunds row on Sales by Channel will show zero for live months (and the static April refund snapshot continues to render for April).
    • Distributors over-count vs static is intentional. Static April had only 2 distributor orders ($7k) because it filtered to CIN7 pivot rows with Channel = 'Backend'. The API doesn't expose that field, so we identify dist as "any non-Coles, non-Woolies, non-Amazon, non-personal company name". This will surface more wholesale activity than static (Priceline, Live Wise Health Store, etc.) — flag for review if the inflation is misleading.
    • 27/27 unit tests pass on the new attributeCin7Order() covering all the company-name patterns observed in the v2.35.2 diagnostic dump (Coles + Coles DCs + Woolies warehouses + Amazon mirrors + Stock Adjustments + personal markers + distributor businesses).

    v2.2.2 — 11 May 2026 (CIN7 attribution diagnostic)

    • Diagnostic endpoint added: GET /api/au/sales/debug?month=YYYY-MM. Dumps a sample of raw CIN7 SalesOrders + frequency tables of unique channel / posRegister / company / status values for the named month. Reveals where customer info actually lives in CIN7 Omni's /SalesOrders response so the attribution rules can be tuned to real data instead of the assumed shape.
    • Why this exists: v2.35.1 returned only 6 attributed orders out of 10,425 fetched for April 2026 (expected ~36 per the static snapshot). All 6 were Coles, with Woolworths / Distributors / Refunds at zero. Fix follows in v2.2.3 once we see what fields CIN7 actually populates on EDI + refund orders.
    • No frontend changes; no production-path changes. Diagnostic-only.

    v2.2.1 — 11 May 2026 (Phase 2b hot-fix)

    • Open POs now correctly filtered to ~26 rows instead of 136 (full history). Filter moved client-side; CIN7's actual status values are Completed / Voided, not all-caps as v2.35 assumed.
    • ETD column added to the Purchase Orders table, with red/amber colouring for past-due and within-7-days. PO table sorts by ETD ascending so the most urgent rows are at the top.
    • Month picker now spans January 2026 to the current month. Static had only April; live CIN7 sales lazy-load per-month and a background pre-fetch fills the other months without user clicks. Chart will populate Jan–current with real CIN7-channel data once the SalesOrders permission below is granted.
    • SalesOrders 403 is a CIN7 permissions issue, not a code bug. The CIN7 API user needs Read access to Sales granted in CIN7 Pro → Setup → Users. Once enabled, the Sales / Monthly SKU / Incoming-by-SKU chips will flip from fallback (static) to CIN7 live.

    v2.2 — 11 May 2026 (Phase 2b: live CIN7 sales / refunds / POs)

    • Sales by Channel reads live from CIN7 for Coles, Woolworths, Distributors, and Refunds. Woo + Amazon stay on the April static snapshot (Phase 3 wires those live). Per-channel source label flips to CIN7 live on rows that have been swapped in.
    • Monthly SKU Sales merges live CIN7-channel columns over the static woo/amz columns. Total tins recomputes; revenue is approximated for the woo+amz portion until Phase 3.
    • Refunds row uses the live CIN7 Cloud-channel data for the selected month.
    • Purchase Orders tab pulls live from CIN7 with the date + status fields the manual CSV export was missing. Days-until-arrival and late-PO column wiring is a small follow-up.
    • Incoming stock by SKU run rate is now a trailing 3-month average (CA-style smoothing) instead of April÷30. Tooltip surfaces the months that fed the average + the CIN7-only-channels caveat.
    • Per-tab source chips show CIN7 live / cached / fallback (static) on Sales, Monthly SKU, POs, and Incoming-by-SKU. Same graceful-degradation pattern as v2.1 inventory.
    • Server-side: src/cin7.js gains fetchSalesOrdersForMonth, fetchOpenPurchaseOrders, buildAuSalesPayload, buildAuPosPayload, plus a KV-cached getMonthSales helper. Cache keys: au:sales:YYYY-MM:v1 per month, au:pos:v1; both 15-min TTL with stale-fallback. All CIN7 calls serialised — never Promise.all'd — per the v2.1 rate-limit lesson.

    v2.1 — 10 May 2026

    • AU dashboard goes live with the prototype's full six-tab layout: Sales by Channel, Monthly SKU Sales, Inventory SKUs, Purchase Orders, Forecasting, Packaging.
    • Real April 2026 data ported from AU Dashboard/au-data.js — Woo 38,223 tins · Amazon 1,866 · Coles 78,336 · Woolies 73,728 · Distributors 1,648 · refunds −110.
    • Static-data Phase 1: au-data.js served as a Worker static asset. Phase 2 will wire CIN7 + Metorik + Amazon SP-API live against KV/D1.
    • Five-channel split (Woo / Amazon / Coles / Woolies / Distributors) replaces the placeholder shell. Coles & Woolies stay separate so supermarket lumpiness is visible.
    • Channel colours: Woo pink, Amazon amber, Coles red, Woolies green, Distributors blue.
    • Global month picker (dropdown) drives Sales + Monthly SKU. Chart re-anchors to trailing 6 months ending at the selected month.
    • Inventory shows base tins + cartons (×48) as separate rows; Spicy Chai sunk to the bottom as discontinued.
    • Purchase Orders: 26 open POs from CIN7 with Type/Supplier filters. Below it, per-SKU incoming stock view with risk badges (critical / low / monitor / stable).

    v2.0 — 18 April 2026

    • Australia dashboard shell added with CIN7 + Xero AU integration (pending credentials)
    • Region toggle: switch between North America and Australia from the header
